Teachers and pupils did not attend school for the past two weeks in Keringet and Kuresoi areas for fear of their security. Several arson attacks have happened since October 19.

Original_Search_Title: Cultural Festival for All Citizens
Headline: Uganda: Cultural Festival for All Citizens
Body_Text:
Kampala — IFyou were feeling dejected about missing out on the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting this month, take heart. The Unity Cultural Festival will give the ordinary Ugandan a chance to be part of the Commonwealth activities.
It is a chance to get re-acquainted with our culture and the things that make Uganda unique. Ugandans will showcase the country's resources and interact with people from different parts of the world.

Original_Search_Title: Ciao Etteh, Welcome Bankole!
Headline: Nigeria: Ciao Etteh, Welcome Bankole!
Body_Text:
Finally, a bone stubbornly stuck on the chamber of Nigeria's House of Reps for well over four months has not only been softened but extricated from the lower house of the National Assembly.
Former Speaker, Patricia Etteh, last Tuesday, kissed the dust resigning as speaker alongside her deputy, Babangida Nguroje. The duo's resignation came as a sigh of relief to all well-meaning people of Nigeria who for well over two months had been held hostage by Etteh's incalcitrance and impunity (permit my English).

Original_Search_Title: Ex-Prisoner Wants to Sell His Son
Headline: Kenya: Ex-Prisoner Wants to Sell His Son
Body_Text:
Nairobi — "I am looking for someone to buy this child," came a booming voice from the reception.
Standing at the Standard Group Kakamega Bureau's reception was a fairly dressed wiry man, towering six feet tall and wearing a black godfather hat.

Original_Search_Title: Local, DRC Labour Ministries to Improve Ties
Headline: South Africa: Local, DRC Labour Ministries to Improve Ties
Body_Text:
Pretoria — Labour Minister Membathisi Mdladlana on Friday accepted an official invitation to visit the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) as part of improving working relations between his Ministry and that of the DRC.
The invitation was extended by DRC Labour Minister Mary-Ang Lukiana during their meeting at Minister Mdladlana's office.

Original_Search_Title: Avoiding Bankolegate
Headline: Nigeria: Avoiding Bankolegate
Body_Text:
The emergence, on Thursday, of Dimeji Saburi Bankole as the fifth speaker of the House of Representatives in the Fourth Republic, no doubt, marks a watershed in the political development of Nigeria.
Before Bankole's emergence, there were Speakers Salisu Buhari, Umar Ghali Na'Abbah, Aminu Bello Masari and then the ousted Patricia Olubunmi Etteh. Of the four, only two of them, Na'Abbah and Masari, successfully served their tenures, though with a lot of crisis.

Original_Search_Title: Speakership - How Reps Defied PDP
Headline: Nigeria: Speakership - How Reps Defied PDP
Body_Text:
Abuja — A new leadership emerged in the House of Representatives on Thursday. Weekly Trust examines the intrigues and behind-the-scenes.
If there is any loser from the beginning of the renovation scandal involving the former Speaker of the House of Representatives Patricia Etteh to the election of Dimeji Bankole as the new speaker on Thursday, it is the People's Democratic Party (PDP).

Original_Search_Title: Poverty in the Midst of Plenty
Headline: Kenya: Poverty in the Midst of Plenty
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Columns of dust spiral into the air as a truck carrying cargo cruises on the main Voi-Taveta road leading to the Tanzania border. Taveta town residents, accustomed to the situation, do not seem overly disturbed as they go about their businesses as usual.
The pattern of life has not changed much in the border town that overlooks the picturesque snowcapped Mt Kilimanjaro and the Pare hills that seem to be escorting it from afar.

Original_Search_Title: 68 Seized Over Kuresoi Clashes
Headline: Kenya: 68 Seized Over Kuresoi Clashes
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Sixty eight people have been arrested in connection with the clashes that have claimed at least four lives in Kuresoi, Molo, the police commissioner has said.
Tens of houses have also been burnt down in the violence initially blamed on cattle raiders. The area was in the last week put under a police curfew in an attempt to restore security.

Original_Search_Title: Manage Secondary Roads Through Toll
Headline: Liberia: Manage Secondary Roads Through Toll
Body_Text:
The Liberian government, in collaboration with the International Labour Organization (ILO), has begun the construction of 11-kilometer road linking Mount Barclay, Johnsonville and Barnersville in Montserrado County.
The Mount Barclay-Johnsonville-Barnersville road construction is being undertaken as part of a US$3 million grant from The Netherlands. Portion of the grant is also expected to be used to construct a 27-kilometer demonstration road linking Bensonville to Todee District also in Montserrado County.

Original_Search_Title: Sh435,000 for Katatumba's Decent Look
Headline: Uganda: Sh435,000 for Katatumba's Decent Look
Body_Text:
Kampala — ANGELA Katatumba, the managing director of Hotel Diplomate in Muyenga, is also a well-known local pop singer, a charity worker and a 'face' for the Savannah Sunrise (SAS) Foundation.
Katatumba is known for decent and classy dressing. Here, Katatumba dons a cream khaki jacket, a black camisole, black pants and strap-on high-heeled shoes. She has accessorised her outfit with a red-bead set.

Original_Search_Title: There Is No End to Western Prejudice on Continent
Headline: Africa: There Is No End to Western Prejudice on Continent
Body_Text:
Assinapol Rwigara, a businessman looked for by the police, surrenders himself. UNDP brings out a report on Rwanda, full of mistakes. Apparently, these two stories that grabbed the headlines recently do not have anything in common. Except for Western prejudice.
The UNDP story is the shorter one. This esteemed organization brings out a report on Rwanda, yet a little while later several inaccuracies, if not errors, in the report are revealed.

Original_Search_Title: Friends in the Journey
Headline: Rwanda: Friends in the Journey
Body_Text:
You may grow weary of the story, but I cannot stop telling it. The memories are burned too deeply into my mind and body. From the years of 1994 to 2001 I fought a battle with back pain. When I was 29 my youthful strength was taken from me. I walked with a cane. Three times, I underwent surgery to my spine. But like those strange fellows 2,000 years ago,
The Lord has been kind to me. At the age of 40 I have both gray in my beard and my youthful strength. With my five kids, maybe the Lord saw fit to give my youth back to me. I know both humanity's and my own story well enough to know that eventually age will catch up with me, so for this season of life I must celebrate the Lord's goodness.

Original_Search_Title: There's More to a Banana Peel Than Meets the Eye
Headline: Rwanda: There's More to a Banana Peel Than Meets the Eye
Body_Text:
With bananas being a staple food, every week thousands of banana peels are thrown away. That is big waste, because they can be transformed into fuel blocks, which you might use for cooking your next banana meal.
It is indeed a brilliant idea: to collect banana peels and other garbage, turn them into flour which you then compact into fuel blocks.

Original_Search_Title: Man Killed in Clashes-Hit Area
Headline: Kenya: Man Killed in Clashes-Hit Area
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A man was killed and three others injured in clashes-torn Kuresoi area on Tuesday night. Fifteen houses were set ablaze.
Potato dealer Mbugua Gikonyo was hacked to death by raiders at his Matunda home at around 8pm.

Original_Search_Title: It Will Be War On 2010 Black Marketeers
Headline: South Africa: It Will Be War On 2010 Black Marketeers
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — IN A bid to prevent the resale of 2010 Soccer World Cup tickets on the black market, local organising committee CEO Danny Jordaan said yesterday government might have to pass legislation making fraudulent sales of tickets for the showpiece a criminal offence.
Speaking at a briefing to announce a First National Bank (FNB) initiative that would enable thousands of South Africans to win tickets for the 2010 event, Jordaan said the resale of tickets was seen in such a serious light that deterrents to discourage the practice were being investigated.

Original_Search_Title: General Abubakar and America's Selective Justice
Headline: Nigeria: General Abubakar and America's Selective Justice
Body_Text:
On September 28, Judge Mathew F. Kennelly of a Chicago district court in America issued a ruling that former Head of State, General Abdussalami Abubakar, was liable to sanctions for refusing to appear before it in a case filed before it several years ago by a threesome of Chief Anthony Enahoro, Dr Arthur Nwankwo and Hafsat Abiola.
The three had accused Abubakar of human rights violations which included the detention and exiling of Enahoro and Nwankwo, the detention and eventual death in detention of Chief M.K.O. Abiola, the presumed winner of the June 12, 1993 presidential election that was nullified by military president, General Ibrahim Babangida, and the extrajudicial killing of Hajiya Kudirat Abiola, wife of Chief Abiola and mother of Hafsat during the regime of General Sani Abacha, Abubakar's predecessor.

Original_Search_Title: Citizens Urged On Banana Value-Addition
Headline: Uganda: Citizens Urged On Banana Value-Addition
Body_Text:
Kampala — UGANDA will double its foreign exchange when the country starts exporting banana flour, the chairman of the Presidential Initiative on Banana Industrial Development (PIBID) has said.
Speaking during a workshop for chefs from hotels in the CHOGM Zone recently, Dr. Frank Mabirizi, said the initiative would kick off in the next two to five years.

Original_Search_Title: EU May Have to Cut Ambitions
Headline: Africa: EU May Have to Cut Ambitions
Body_Text:
Brussels — Just two months before a deadline set by the European Union for signing all-embracing free trade accords with some of the world's poorest countries, the EU has admitted that it will have to reduce its ambition.
As a result, most of the so-called Economic Partnership Agreements it wants to reach with almost 80 African, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) countries by Dec. 31 are likely to be restricted to trade in goods, leaving such issues as the opening up of service markets and new rules on investment and competition to further discussion.

Original_Search_Title: Hungry, Courageous And Casually Racist
Headline: Kenya: Hungry, Courageous And Casually Racist
Body_Text:
Nairobi — I have been reading a marvellous book called Private Battles, excerpts from diaries which four ordinary British people kept through the Second World War, 1939 to 1945.
During six years of great danger and considerable deprivation, they depict a society which is dogged, patriotic, bitchy, courageous and, by today's standards, amazingly and thoughtlessly racist.

Original_Search_Title: Can You Do Away With Buveera?
Headline: Uganda: Can You Do Away With Buveera?
Body_Text:
On Thursday last week, the National Environment Management Authority (Nema) seized a large number of polythene bags (buveera) from many supermarkets around Kampala. Buveera of less than 30 microns were banned in June this year and Nema swung into action to enforce the ban last week. We asked people around the city what they think about the ban and if they can do away with using polythene bags.
Ms Faridah Namugenyi (Bread seller)

Original_Search_Title: Members of Central Region Assembly Conduct Tour in Gash-Barka Region
Headline: Eritrea: Members of Central Region Assembly Conduct Tour in Gash-Barka Region
Body_Text:
Barentu — Members of the Central Region Assembly conducted a tour in the Gash-Barka region to observe the ongoing development activities.
They stated that the nation is registering remarkable achievements in endeavors to improve the living standard of the people.

Original_Search_Title: Jesus Said the Poor Will Always Exist
Headline: Uganda: Jesus Said the Poor Will Always Exist
Body_Text:
Opinion | BONNA BAGAGAWALE | October 25, 2007
There is an amazing story in the Bible (Matthew 26:6-11) which I believe those who read the scriptures are quite familiar with. According to the story, our Lord Jesus was dining in a home in Bethany when a woman came over to him with an alabaster jar full of very expensive perfume, which she promptly emptied on his head.

Original_Search_Title: Shs400m Required for Industrial Park
Headline: Uganda: Shs400m Required for Industrial Park
Body_Text:
Kabarole — Kabarole District LC5 chairman Michael Mugisa has requested the government to provide the district with Shs400 million for establishing an industrial park.
The chairman's request was contained in a letter dated October 19 and addressed to the Minister of State for Micro Finance, Gen. Salim Saleh.

Original_Search_Title: 'No Postponement of EPA Deadline' - EU
Headline: West Africa: 'No Postponement of EPA Deadline' - EU
Body_Text:
Brussels — The European Union has rejected calls by West African states to postpone the end-of-year deadline for concluding a free trade agreement.
Earlier this month, West African governments urged the EU not to insist that the proposed economic partnership agreement (EPA) be finalised by the deadline of December 31.

Original_Search_Title: Citizens Throw Away Food
Headline: Uganda: Citizens Throw Away Food
Body_Text:
Kampala — CLAD in an old, stained garment, Patrick Aine, 30, regularly moves from home to home collecting garbage for a living. He earns sh1,000 - sh2,000 a day, which cannot guarantee him two meals daily.
"Someone gives you his dustbin and you find a polythene bag full of mawoolo (left-over food). Do you expect me to spare it when I am not sure of what I will eat in the evening?" he asks.

Original_Search_Title: EU Rejects Delay in WA Trade Talks
Headline: West Africa: EU Rejects Delay in WA Trade Talks
Body_Text:
THE EU has rejected a request from West Africa for a two-year extension of preferential trade terms but is offering flexibility in talks about a new deal, a European Commission official said on Wednesday.
The European Union and nearly 80 countries in the Africa, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) group have been talking for seven years about new, so-called Economic Partnership Agreements.

Original_Search_Title: Opadokun - Only Etteh's Ouster Can
Headline: Nigeria: Opadokun - Only Etteh's Ouster Can
Body_Text:
Lagos — Former General Secretary of Afenifere, the pan-Yoruba socio-political organisation, Mr.Ayo Opadokun was among the few Nigerians who staked their lives for the return of Nigeria to civil rule during the military era. As the then National Democratic Coalition (NADECO) scribe, he was moved from one prison to the other for daring to resist the activities of the military junta of the late Head of State, Gen. Sani Abacha. However, the outspoken former Afenifere scribe until recently hardly made public comments because in his words: 'I prefer to be on the side lane, watching.' He however, largely broke his docile attitude recently in an encounter with Philip Ogunmade, where he dissected the N628 million scandal rocking the House of Representatives. He also commented on the state of the nation. Excerpts
Recently, it was widely reported that the National Chairman of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), Col. Ahmadu Ali threatened to ensure the recall of PDP lawmakers in the House who may move to remove the embattled Speaker, Mrs. Ptricia Etteh. Although the party later denied its chairman making such a comment, what do you make out of it?

Original_Search_Title: Muk's Artistic Trip Down Memory Lane
Headline: Uganda: Muk's Artistic Trip Down Memory Lane
Body_Text:
Kampala — CURIOSITY grips me when I arrive at the Makerere University School of Industrial and Fine Arts gallery. A two-week exhibition depicting artworks from the school's archives opened on Tuesday.
Paintings and sculptures executed by students since 1960 are on display. The exhibition attempts to depict how art has evolved at Makerere over the decades, from the Trowellian religiously-inspired themes as shown in Kefa Ssempangi's sculpture: "Religion versus Witchcraft" to the representational semi-realistic images of the 80s to the semi-abstract imagery of the 90s and 2000s.

Original_Search_Title: Principal Charged With Wife's Murder
Headline: Kenya: Principal Charged With Wife's Murder
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A secondary school principal in Embu was on Wednesday charged with the murder of his wife.
Mr Jacob Njue Kamwitha, the principal of St Marks Kigari Day Secondary School, appeared before Embu Resident Judge Mary Khaminwa accused of committing the offence at Mbiri location, Kirinyaga District, on September 29.

Original_Search_Title: Ethiopian Feted in Country Was Not Mau Mau Hero
Headline: Kenya: Ethiopian Feted in Country Was Not Mau Mau Hero
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It is now official. Kenyans were short-changed in the Lemma Ayanu saga.
The 84-year-old octogenarian was not the real Mau Mau hero, General Stanley Mirugi Mathenge, who disappeared during the battle for freedom with colonial government in 1953.

Original_Search_Title: As I See It - Will Ian Appoint His Brother VP?
Headline: Botswana: As I See It - Will Ian Appoint His Brother VP?
Body_Text:
I can imagine varied rhetorical questions in answer to the question above: Why not? Are we not destined to be ruled by a Khama dynasty?
The constitutional amendment providing 'automatic succession' has unleashed an awful spate of questions; it poses innuendos of unprecedented proportions in the management of our hitherto shining democracy. It looks like under this automatic succession, anything goes. What with the conditions, a nominated VP candidate can propose: fine, if I am allowed to combine politics with bogosi, BDF helicopter pilot with the Vice Presidency and be prime minister whatever the constitution stipulates? And fancy having all these on a platter! It goes without saying that if a nominee can set such outrageous preconditions, the nominator can also wangle his way around to appoint a VP to his heart's content.

Original_Search_Title: Kamwenge Adults Embrace Education
Headline: Uganda: Kamwenge Adults Embrace Education
Body_Text:
Kamwenge — Politically divided in two constituencies of Kibale and Kitagwenda, Kamwenge is a seven year old district which was curved out of Kabarole District. It is one of the highest maize and bean producing districts in the country.
Despite the production boom, poor roads, lack of safe and clean water and high illiteracy levels among the adults iare still a problem. It is for the latter reason that three years ago, saw the emergence of the Swedish International Development Agency and Adventist Development Relief Agency in the district, to try and reduce illiteracy levels through the Functional Adult Illiteracy programme.

Original_Search_Title: Politics of Language
Headline: Kenya: Politics of Language
Body_Text:
Nairobi — When speaking in public, politicians seem to thrive on an uncanny style of language that, on closer scrutiny, is opaque, ambiguous and empty.
In their quest to appear statesman-like, sound clever, scorn opponents, pour platitudes or demonstrate loyalty, politicians play 'the dirty game' by giving us expressions that capture the public's imagination and end up either defining certain periods of our political history or providing fodder for satire.

Original_Search_Title: How Nigeria Was Spared Another Civil War -- Prof. Alaba Ogunsanwo
Headline: Nigeria: How Nigeria Was Spared Another Civil War -- Prof. Alaba Ogunsanwo
Body_Text:
NIGERIA would have been plunged into another civil war had the constitutional amendment to ensure a third term for former President Olusegun Obasanjo not been scuttled by the Senate led by Ken Nnamani, president of the Senate, 2005 - 2007. This was the submission of erudite scholar, diplomat and political scientist, Professor Alaba Ogunsanwo, at the Faculty of Arts, University of Lagos Lecture delivered by Senator Nnamani titled: "Transformative Leadership: Lessons from the Fifth Senate," recently.
"Our guest speaker is a phenomenon in the Nigerian political landscape. If the history of this country is written, his name will resound tremendously because of his inner strength, courage, determination, fearlessness, strong character, resolute, in scuttling the third term bid by taking the bull by the horn and saved this country from civil war." He added: "The Senate under leadership of Ken Nnamani refused to be bamboozled and railroad a constitutional amendment that would have brought third term for one person to remain in office till year 2020 as it will not be just the next four years but for another 12 years. Nnamani saved Nigeria from this trouble.

Original_Search_Title: Eedris Abdulkareem - the King is Back!
Headline: Nigeria: Eedris Abdulkareem - the King is Back!
Body_Text:
After his last album, 'Letter to Mr. President' met rave reviews but lukewarm sales, Eedris Abdulkareem went somewhat under the radar.
His disappearance from the growing music scene in Nigeria sparked rumours of a move to neighbouring Ghana. Some of his critics attributed his fall from grace - if you can call it that - to his now-infamous altercation with visiting American rapper 50 Cent, his not-so-private feud with former President Obasanjo and many other things. But through it all, Abdulkareem remained silent, speaking in bits and pieces. But when Weekend Magazine spoke to him, he opened up about all the aforementioned issues, his upcoming release, personal issues and then some more. Herewith, are excerpts from the interview.

Original_Search_Title: Why I Would Fire All Those With Me If I Were Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: Why I Would Fire All Those With Me If I Were Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — If I were President Kibaki, I would sack all my political strategists and advisers.
I would do this for the simple reason that every piece of advice they have given me in the last three years, every strategy they have presented to me as foolproof, has gone haywire.

Original_Search_Title: UMA Petitions Museveni Over 120 Percent Plastics Tax
Headline: Uganda: UMA Petitions Museveni Over 120 Percent Plastics Tax
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Uganda Manufacturers Association (UMA) has asked President Yoweri Museveni to suspend the 120% tax on plastics.
James Kalibbala, the chairman, said at the closure of the 15th Uganda International Trade Fair on Wednesday that they support protection of the environment but the tax should be suspended to make the sector competitive.

Original_Search_Title: Monuc Radio Monitoring - 11 October 2007
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Monuc Radio Monitoring - 11 October 2007
Publisher: United Nations Mission in the Democratic Republic of Congo (Kinshasa)
Body_Text:
RFI of 11/10/07 to 4.30GMT
After three days of fighting, Laurent Nkunda asks for a cease-fire.

Original_Search_Title: 'Buveera' Banned, What Next?
Headline: Uganda: 'Buveera' Banned, What Next?
Body_Text:
Kampala — Kampalans are living in fear after the Government recently banned polythene bags commonly known as buveera.
The ban on polythene bags and materials of 30 microns or less took effect on October 1. Anyone found using the banned type of buveera will be arrested and charged.

Original_Search_Title: From State House to Jail House
Headline: Nigeria: From State House to Jail House
Body_Text:
The image of a banana republic which many Nigerians try to shrug off about their country is fast sticking.
A land of excesses and strange contraries, Nigeria is one of few countries where a state executive could move straight from government house to prison on account of their activities while in office. Such strange transition from an agbada-wearing politician to a skimpy, prison uniform-wearing convict has been taking place in Nigeria in the last few months. After months of hide-and-seek, some former governors believed to have milked their state treasuries dry are now being tracked and hunted down by EFCC sleuths. It was a long and bitter encounter between the governors and the EFCC chairman Nuhu Ribadu whom they saw as the hounding dog of Obasanjo. There is a strong likelihood that former President Obasanjo might have used the EFCC to settle personal scores with some persons, including highly placed public officials, to make them tow certain lines drawn up for them.

Original_Search_Title: Roko Wins Multi Million Dollar Rwanda Forest Ecolodge Venture
Headline: Rwanda: Roko Wins Multi Million Dollar Rwanda Forest Ecolodge Venture
Body_Text:
Rusizi — The first high end eco-lodge in Rwanda will be housed in the 1,015 sq km Nyungwe forest located in the south western part of the country.
Owned by the Rwanda Office of Tourism and National Parks (ORTPN) and raised from the ground by ROKO Construction, the US$7.2million (Rwf3.99billion) 25 villa complex will stand out tall in the region.

Original_Search_Title: Kamwenge Adults Embrace Education
Headline: Uganda: Kamwenge Adults Embrace Education
Body_Text:
Kamwenge — Politically divided in two constituencies of Kibale and Kitagwenda, Kamwenge is a seven year old district which was curved out of Kabarole District. It is one of the highest maize and bean producing districts in the country.
Despite the production boom, poor roads, lack of safe and clean water and high illiteracy levels among the adults iare still a problem. It is for the latter reason that three years ago, saw the emergence of the Swedish International Development Agency and Adventist Development Relief Agency in the district, to try and reduce illiteracy levels through the Functional Adult Illiteracy programme.

Original_Search_Title: Kilimo - This is Why I Ran Away From ODM
Headline: Kenya: Kilimo - This is Why I Ran Away From ODM
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Marakwet East MP Linah Kilimo yesterday expressed regret for supporting the Orange team during the 2005 referendum, saying that the move cost her the ministerial post.
It was unfortunate, she said, that out of euphoria she "blindly" supported the No group, resulting in her sacking as the Immigration Minister. "It was an unwise decision to ditch the Banana side in favour of Orange, resulting in my sacking as a minister," she said.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki Kicks Ngilu Out of the Cabinet
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki Kicks Ngilu Out of the Cabinet
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki last night fired Health minister Charity Ngilu, his political ally turned fierce critic.
The sacking happened shortly after she described ODM presidential candidate Raila Odinga as "the second Nelson Mandela of Africa."

Original_Search_Title: Africa Pot of Le Jardin Home of Traditional Food
Headline: Zimbabwe: Africa Pot of Le Jardin Home of Traditional Food
Body_Text:
Harare — Have  a craving for the roots? Then that crave should be satiated not by visiting the rural areas but having some traditional food being offered right in the city centre.
Those who fall for okra dishes, matumbu, guru, traditionally prepared goat meat, these are offered at a Nigerian restaurant called Africa Pot of Le Jardin nestled just near the luxurious Meikles Hotel. After a time of spoiling myself with fast foods -- others call it junk food -- this week I felt the yearning of going traditional and the African Pot of Le Jardin was my destination as I decided to dine with the Nigerians.

Original_Search_Title: Isn't Kabalega the 'Greatest' Hero of Country's Independence?
Headline: Uganda: Isn't Kabalega the 'Greatest' Hero of Country's Independence?
Body_Text:
Kampala — "Some are born great, some achieve greatness and some have greatness thrust upon them", so said Malvolio to Olivia in William Shakespeare's Twelfth Night. As we celebrate the 45th anniversary of independence from Britain this week, it is appropriate for us to bow our heads in respect for our sung and unsung great "heroes" who played different roles, leading to our independence on October 9, 1962.
Sadly, like the much-misused word "treason" and "patriotism"; heroism is another word which means different things to different Ugandans, depending on their political standpoint or even ethnicity.

Original_Search_Title: Swimming Against Tide - Candidates Face Uphill Task As Poll Clock Ticks
Headline: Kenya: Swimming Against Tide - Candidates Face Uphill Task As Poll Clock Ticks
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Major political realignments are taking place as the clock ticks towards the General Election.
Across the country, politicians are choosing sides that give them the best chance of victory. But then there are those who have so far opted to swim against the tide and stick to camps that may struggle in their regions.

Original_Search_Title: Leaving the Professional Nest for Political Train
Headline: Kenya: Leaving the Professional Nest for Political Train
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kieni parliamentary aspirant, Mr Mwangi Macharia, then a director of the Clinton HIV/Aids Initiative, meets President Kibaki and former US President, Bill Clinton at a past function.
Until early this year, Mr Edwin Mwangi Macharia led a quiet life as an executive with the Bill Clinton Foundation.

Original_Search_Title: Presidents Should Serve Two Terms - Raila
Headline: Kenya: Presidents Should Serve Two Terms - Raila
Body_Text:
On Thursday night, 93.3 KFM's political talk show the Hot Seat moderated by Charles Mwanguhya Mpagi hosted Kenya's leading opposition presidential contender Raila Odinga. Mr Odinga who is the flag bearer of the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) was accompanied by his associate Najib Balala. They discussed the Kenyan elections, the East African Political Federation and African leadership. Daily Monitor's Jan Ajwang listened in and now brings the excerpts:
Mr Odinga many people here wonder which political party you belong to; they don't seem to understand the evolution of political parties in Kenya; which party do you represent?

Original_Search_Title: Ngilu Backs Raila As ODM Set for Launch
Headline: Kenya: Ngilu Backs Raila As ODM Set for Launch
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Health minister Charity Ngilu has ended the guessing game over her political future after declaring she would support ODM's Raila Odinga in the race for State House.
However, she also said she would continue serving in President Kibaki's Government but campaign against him ahead of the General Election.

Original_Search_Title: Foul-Mouths in Campaign Mode a Bane to Kibaki Re-Election
Headline: Kenya: Foul-Mouths in Campaign Mode a Bane to Kibaki Re-Election
Body_Text:
With the cast President Kibaki showcased at the formal launch of his re-election campaign last week, he does not need enemies.
Most of the speakers exhibited legendary endowment in abrasion, foul language and acrid imagery. They were very much unlike the President who long stopped talking about morons and chicken droppings.

Original_Search_Title: Bertha's Talent for Stretching Truth Exposed
Headline: Zimbabwe: Bertha's Talent for Stretching Truth Exposed
Body_Text:
Harare — WHEN Bertha Zakeyo, Zimbabwe's representative in the Big Brother Africa 2 show on MNet, walked into the Big Brother House, she carried a Bible, probably showing off her deep religious beliefs.
All this seems to have changed, however. She has undergone a complete metamorphosis and has now revealed another side of herself by taking to the bottle.

Original_Search_Title: Etteh - Let Justice Prevail - Mark
Headline: Nigeria: Etteh - Let Justice Prevail - Mark
Body_Text:
Kaduna — Senate President and Chairman of the National Assembly, Senator David Mark has urged those calling for the head of Speaker of the House Representatives, Mrs. Patricia Olubunmi Etteh over her alleged role in the award of N579 million contract for the renovation of her residence and that of her Deputy not to preempt the ouctome of the final resolution of the House on the matter.
But the Integrity Group in the House of Representatives at theforefront of the move to impeach the Speaker, Honourable Patricia Etteh over the N628million contract controversy, has said Chairman of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), Alhaji Ahmadu Ali's visit to the National Assembly on Tuesday afternoon will not save the embattled Speaker from impeachment.

Original_Search_Title: Alternatives to EPAs If Not Signed?
Headline: Ghana: Alternatives to EPAs If Not Signed?
Body_Text:
As there is strong likelihood that the economic partnership agreements will not be signed at the set date of 31st December, 2007 because of the delay recorded in the ratification process, several alternatives have been suggested by governments, civil society and the European Union (EU).
As a result, the concern of African countries engaged in the negotiations is to see the European Union adopt transitional measures that make it possible to ensure the access of products exported from Africa on its market after the deadline.

Original_Search_Title: Gabes Witnesses a Welcome Rebirth of Its Coastal Oases
Headline: Tunisia: Gabes Witnesses a Welcome Rebirth of Its Coastal Oases
Body_Text:
Tunis — The coastal city of Gabes which boasts some of the Mediterranean 's most unique coastal oases, is also one of Tunisia 's most vibrant industrial cities.
Due to the city's growing industrialization over the years, the city's coastal oases have paid a heavy price, which has prompted local and national authorities to take steps to rehabilitate what is considered to be the city's oxygen reservoir, in the face of a relentless industrial growth.

Original_Search_Title: EPA - A Veritable Hobson's Choice
Headline: Ghana: EPA - A Veritable Hobson's Choice
Body_Text:
Ghanadot — The economic Partnership Agreement (EPA) between the EU and Third World countries is to take effect beginning 2008. With it, The EU hopes to create trade reciprocity with developing nations.
Reciprocity is the word used, but in reality what is happening is an arm twisting exercise against Third Word countries in an attempt to offer them a choice that has already been used under colonialism.

Original_Search_Title: From State House to Jail House
Headline: Nigeria: From State House to Jail House
Body_Text:
Lagos — The image of a banana republic which many Nigerians try to shrug off about their country is fast sticking. A land of excesses and strange contraries, Nigeria is one of few countries where a state executive could move straight from government house to prison on account of their activities while in office.
Such strange transition from an agbada-wearing politician to a skimpy, prison uniform-wearing convict has been taking place in Nigeria in the last few months.

Original_Search_Title: Exports Now Seen As Key to Growth
Headline: Uganda: Exports Now Seen As Key to Growth
Body_Text:
Kampala — The Ugandan government has identified rapid growth of exports as key to lifting the country's flagging economic fortunes.
And for the last two years it has devoted profound energy to catalysing the nation's export trade; free land has been doled out, tax breaks lavished on investors, cheap loans dangled, and a whole gamut of other incentives rolled out to get businesspeople to export and bring in more foreign exchange receipts.

Original_Search_Title: NGO Seeks Policy On Conflict
Headline: Kenya: NGO Seeks Policy On Conflict
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A Non-Governmental organisation wants the State to set up a national policy on peace and conflict management.
It says that the policy will play a critical role in addressing issues of insecurity in the country.

Original_Search_Title: Ruckus in the NRM Suits Museveni Just Fine
Headline: Uganda: Ruckus in the NRM Suits Museveni Just Fine
Body_Text:
Kampala — AS President Yoweri Museveni looks back nearly one and a half years since he was elected for his third elective term, and three and a half years to the next election, two main challenges within his National Resistance Movement Organisation (NRM-O) confront him.
The party is faced with what loyalists call "healthy internal disagreements" driven by a group of young generation members, especially in Parliament who refuse to be bulldozed into sheepishly towing the party line. But for the party chairman, Mr Museveni, the trouble is beyond the young Turks.

Original_Search_Title: Chuba Okadigbo - Four Years After
Headline: Nigeria: Chuba Okadigbo - Four Years After
Body_Text:
Abuja — It is exactly four years since the Oyi of Oyi and one-time president of the Nigerian Senate, Dr. Chuba Okadigbo, passed on. He died in Abuja on September 25, 2003.
Dr. Okadigbo's absence in the last four years has given me, his former special assistant and indeed one of his closest aides, an unusual opportunity and reason to reflect deeply on several issues­, spiritual and physical. For those who knew Dr. Okadigbo well, he exuded so much warmth, intelligence, charisma, strength of character, force of personality and courage. Death, on September 25, 2003, wiped away all these attributes which made Dr. Okadigbo a rare personality and robbed us, his disciples, of the opportunity to enjoy his continued guidance.

Original_Search_Title: It Took Defection to ODM to Give MP a Minute of Mention On Television
Headline: Kenya: It Took Defection to ODM to Give MP a Minute of Mention On Television
Body_Text:
Nairobi — If David Were was not a prefect in charge of something in his high school days, it must be a naked proof of discrimination or incredible disregard for innate capacities by the institution's management.
Granted, Were may not exude raw authority that would naturally make Fred Gumo an undisputable candidate for the dining hall captain. Unless the Westlands MP, 'Mr Toughie' image is plastic, he would be ideal for the kitchen not so much for his sense of order but for the honour among natural rebels to authority and the logic of keeping the enemies of administration close to power or even the kitchen, especially if food is their number one problem.

Original_Search_Title: Brides Who Pay Their Price
Headline: Kenya: Brides Who Pay Their Price
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The bride is making her entry alone: no groom, no best man, no best maid. Still, the village is euphoric. Locals gather in frenzy, tapping their feet rhythmically to a song. The smell of roast meat blends effortlessly with that of traditional brew, chitchat, the clatter of cutlery, foot trumping and the shrill songs in a neat choreography.
It is a big day. The clan is receiving the bride wealth of their daughter - only that this is coming from the daughter's reserves, rather than from a groom. Further, the daughter is old enough to have had grandchildren. In this area, as in much of Kikuyu land, bride price is more than just a cementing reward that parents receive for the 'loss' of their daughter to the suitor. Dowry here has another symbolic meaning: before it is paid out, the woman cannot graduate to a level where she may enjoy certain adult privileges. This is why women are increasingly having to pay their own bride wealth.

Original_Search_Title: Get the Hell Out of Juba And Build a Market in Nimule
Headline: Uganda: Get the Hell Out of Juba And Build a Market in Nimule
Body_Text:
Kampala — Let us stop crying and complaining over the murder and rape of Ugandans in Juba. Let us not waste any more time being angry at the Southern Sudanese officials for not effectively protecting our people who are legitimately trading in their capital.
We should stop expecting any gratitude or positive attitude from Juba for whatever we did for those brothers during their hour of need.

Original_Search_Title: World Artists Converge for Forests' Benefit
Headline: Kenya: World Artists Converge for Forests' Benefit
Body_Text:
Nairobi — For the past two weeks, Ngong Forest has been the site of an artistic rescue effort. Eighteen artists have gathered here for the second annual Art in the Forest workshop, secluding themselves amid the endangered woods for the sole purpose of creating whatever art they can from the materials provided by nature.
The products of their imagination (all biodegradable) will be unveiled tomorrow for a free public show aimed at promoting the arts while raising awareness of the ecological damage taking place in Nairobi's backyard.

Original_Search_Title: Will Botswana Host Africom?
Headline: Botswana: Will Botswana Host Africom?
Body_Text:
Gaborone — Will Botswana succumb (if approached) to the request by United States (US) to host Africa Command (AFRICOM)?
The US is considering deploying some small army base in some unidentified African countries. Some reports have already identified some of those countries eyed by US being; Liberia, Senegal, Djibouti and Botswana. This is coming at a time when the United States is trapped in endless wars in both Iraq and Afghanistan and finding it difficult to completely wipe out the so called Islamic militants thought to be linked to Al-Quaeda.

Original_Search_Title: Country and Phantom Terror Threat
Headline: Nigeria: Country and Phantom Terror Threat
Body_Text:
Abuja — Not long ago, America issued a warning to her nationals living in the country to be extra careful about the possibility of terrorist attacks in Nigeria!
A similar warning followed, this time by Australian governments reminding her citizens to always be mindful about their safety while conducting their businesses.

Original_Search_Title: Egwu, Anyim in Round Two Duels
Headline: Nigeria: Egwu, Anyim in Round Two Duels
Body_Text:
Abakaliki — Ordinarily, Dr. Sam Egwu, former governor of Ebonyi state and Senator Anyim Pius Anyim, former Senate President are supposed to be best of friends.
One is that both are bound by the same religious affiliation - that is, the Assembles of God Church. Two, both are of the same state, Ebonyi, and of the same political party, PDP, from where both men shot into political limelight in 1999.

Original_Search_Title: National Assembly Pays Enwerem Last Respect
Headline: Nigeria: Nass Pays Enwerem Last Respect
Body_Text:
Abuja — Having vacated the impeachment hanging on late Senator Evan Enwerem Wednesday, the National Assembly yesterday in a joint session in the Senate chamber paid their departed colleague his last respect before the body was airlifted to Owerri, Imo State for final burial rites.
Enwerem who was first Senate president of the fourth republic had full honours when his body was dressed in national colours in the National Assembly which he headed.

Original_Search_Title: Jazz Icon Butler Arrives in Country
Headline: Uganda: Jazz Icon Butler Arrives in Country
Body_Text:
Kampala — Celebrated South African artiste, Jonathan Butler, arrived in Uganda yesterday afternoon for a charity show. Butler will tomorrow evening perform at Kampala Serena Hotel. Part of the show's proceeds will benefit The Aids Support Organization (TASO).
"About 70% tickets have been sold (each costs sh120,000) and about 85% of the proceeds will go to TASO's orphaned children," said Justina Ntabgoba of Celtel Uganda, which is organising the event.

Original_Search_Title: Tashin Hankali - Upper - Cut in the Assembly
Headline: Nigeria: Tashin Hankali - Upper - Cut in the Assembly
Body_Text:
Abuja — A few days ago, bedlam was let loose in the House of Representatives. Members threw good sense to the dogs and threw punches at one another across the aisle, others hollered and shouted at the top of their voices like demons let loose from their tethers.
In all this confusion, the panel established to investigate the 628 million naira contract scam in the House of Representatives was halted from continuing its proceedings.

Original_Search_Title: Review of  Blogs
Headline: Africa: Review of  Blogs
Body_Text:
Dispatches from the Trench comments on the suspension of South African Chief Prosecutor, Vusi Pikoli, by President Thabo Mbeki:
'Am I alone in feeling increasingly perturbed by these actions by our president? The timing of this announcement was clearly also designed to limit any public discussion or interrogation of the president's decision, coming as it did on a public holiday and as the president jets out of the country away from the domestic limelight.

Original_Search_Title: Makerere Should Unite And Correct False Information Against It
Headline: Uganda: Makerere Should Unite And Correct False Information Against It
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRIME Minister Prof. Apolo Nsibambi will today and tomorrow preside over the 56th and 57th graduation ceremonies of Makerere University, his last as the Chancellor. Below is his speech, which has been slightly edited
I come to say farewell for I shall cease being Chancellor on October 23. I gave notice to the President that although I was eligible for re-appointment for another term, I wish to focus on my heavy responsibilities as Prime Minister.

Original_Search_Title: Leftist Politics Ideal for Third World
Headline: Zimbabwe: Leftist Politics Ideal for Third World
Body_Text:
Harare — American  essayist Richard Armour once noted: "Politics, it seems to me, for years, or all too long, has been concerned with right or left instead of right or wrong."
While the dividing line between right and left has been nuanced by dynamic changes in global geo-politics so that we now have centre-left and centre-right formations, among others, ordinary people have remained in search of political dispensations that are concerned with what is right and that which can right wrongs. And in this pursuit of politics that serve their own needs rather than those of politicians, they have found themselves rediscovering the progressiveness of the left, or what Mikhail Gorbachev would probably have called a neo-perestroika.

Original_Search_Title: Rock Stars Collect 600,000 Signatures for Fair Trade With Africa
Headline: Africa: Rock Stars Collect 600,000 Signatures for Fair Trade With Africa
Body_Text:
Madrid — Several popular Spanish rock groups helped collect 600,000 signatures and delivered them to the government Tuesday in support of an international campaign calling for the Spanish government to modify its trade policy towards Africa and eliminate restrictions on imports from that region.
Singers Miguel Bosé and Shuarma, members of the Dover rock band, and Ariana Arpa, the head of Intermón Oxfam - Oxfam International's Spanish affiliate - were received in the seat of government where they presented a statement titled: "100 Days: Trade Negotiations Threaten Europe's Commitment to Africa".

Original_Search_Title: Chuba Okadigbo - Four Years After
Headline: Nigeria: Chuba Okadigbo - Four Years After
Body_Text:
Lagos — It is exactly four years since the Oyi of oyi and one time President of the Nigerian Senate, the Rt. Hon. Chuba Okadigbo passed on. Dr. Okadigbo died in Abuja on 25th September, 2003.
As Dr. Okadigbo's former Special Assistant and indeed one of his closest aides, his absence in the last four years has given me an unusual opportunity and reason to reflect deeply on several issues spiritually and physically. For those who knew Dr. Okadigbo well, he exided so much warmth, intelligence, charisma, strenth of character, force of personality and courage. Death, on 25th September, 2003 wiped away all all these attributes which made Dr Okadigbo a rare personality and robbed us, his disciples of the opprtunity to enjoy his continued guidance.

Original_Search_Title: Chuba Okadigbo - Four Years After
Headline: Nigeria: Chuba Okadigbo - Four Years After
Body_Text:
Abuja — It is exactly four years since the Oyi of Oyi and one time President of the Nigerian Senate, the Rt. Hon. Dr. Chuba Okadigbo passed on.
He died in Abuja on 25th September, 2003. As his former Special Assistant and indeed one of his closest aides, his absence in the last four years has given me an unusual opportunity and reason to reflect deeply on several issues spiritual and physical. For those who knew Dr. Okadigbo well, he exuded so much warmth, intelligence, Charisma, strength of character, force of personality and courage. Death, on 25th September, 2003 wiped away all these attributes which made Dr. Okadigbo a rare personality and robbed us, his disciples, of the opportunity to enjoy his continued guidance.

Original_Search_Title: After Oladimeji's Murder - Who is Next?
Headline: Nigeria: After Oladimeji's Murder - Who is Next?
Body_Text:
Let me pretend that I did not watch House Fight, Part 40, which was acted in the National Assembly on Thursday. Let me pretend it was a film trick meant to deceive Nigerians, and let me move over to Oyo State where a House member was murdered last week.
The nation's euphoria over the treat ac-corded the Golden Eaglets of Nigeria by the federal government, last Saturday, died when news filtered in that Hon. Olusegun Oladimeji, aka Segelu, a Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) federal legislator representing Lagelu/Akinyele federal constituency, had been assassinated by gunmen at a relaxation joint at Ejioku town in Lagelu local government area of Oyo State.

Original_Search_Title: 'I Saw My Family Hacked to Death'
Headline: Uganda: 'I Saw My Family Hacked to Death'
Body_Text:
Kampala — ON Saturday, September 15, Owen Nayebare, five, went to bed peacefully with his father and two sisters in their three-bedroomed mud-and-wattle house in Nyakizinga village, Kagango sub-county, Bushenyi district. Little did he know that he was seeing his family for the last time.
In the middle of the night, unknown assailants broke into the house and slaughtered Moses Mwebembezi, 37, Nayebare's father and Sharon Nareeba, 16 and Lonati Natukwasa, 6; his sisters.

Original_Search_Title: 2,000 Families Face Eviction From Kibaale
Headline: Uganda: 2,000 Families Face Eviction From Kibaale
Body_Text:
DESPERATION, fear and resignation describe the situation under which over 2,000 families in Bugangaizi County are currently living. Their land has been allegedly given away by Sub county Land committees in the area to few individuals whom the locals call "rich and non resident" in the area.
Verbal and written orders to vacate have been issued to these estimated 10,000 people. But good enough, they have sale agreements. The epicenter of this crisis is in Nalwenyo Sub-county and it has spread to the neighbouring sub-county of Kakindo. The 11 affected villages include Kijwenge, Kyakijuta, Kyakasimbazi, Katikara, Bujwara, Mwitanzigye, Kikoora, Kyangota, Kitagenda, Kasambya and Nyakabingo. Fences have been erected and marks put with the residents looking on helplessly.

Original_Search_Title: Widow Recalls Her Big Day With the President
Headline: Kenya: Widow Recalls Her Big Day With the President
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As she welcomed President Kibaki to her modest home in Matisi village, Webuye, Bungoma East District, her heart pounded with joy and excitement.
She appeared a little nervous at first, but a light smile on the President's face as he warmly shook her hand calmed her nerves.

Original_Search_Title: Bakers Maintain High Bread Price
Headline: Cameroon: Bakers Maintain High Bread Price
Body_Text:
Bakers and bread sellers have defied an appeal by the Minister of Commerce, Luc Magloire Mbarga Atangana, to cut down the price of bread.
In a recent release, the Minister had urged bread sellers to maintain the original price of bread, while investigation was being carried out on the cause of the sudden hike in the price of flour.

Original_Search_Title: Artists Union to Sue Filmmakers
Headline: Namibia: Artists Union to Sue Filmmakers
Body_Text:
Windhoek — The Filmmakers Association of Namibia (FAN) is to be legally sued on serious charges of libel and defamation of character.
This turn of events in the local film industry is the result of a public personal attack on the president of the Oruuano Artists Union, Banana Shekupe, who yesterday confirmed that he has instructed his lawyers to demand an apology from FAN.

Original_Search_Title: Madam Speaker and the Banana Peel
Headline: Nigeria: Madam Speaker and the Banana Peel
Body_Text:
Abuja — From the revelations at the on-going probe panel which was instituted to look into the controversial N628 million contract that was awarded to up-grade the residences of House Speaker Patricia Olubunmi Etteh and Alhaji Babangida Nguroje, her deputy, it looks like Madam speaker may slip from the exalted chair of the the number four citizen, to become a floor member of the House of Representatives.
Since 1999, when she first got elected, Mrs Patricia Olunmi Etteh, the current speaker, has celebrated about eight birthdays as a legislator. However, as House Speaker, this year's gig was marked in style. On August 17, according to reports, Mrs Etteh threw a bash in the United States, where guests ate and drank to their fill. Law-making, cloak-and-dagger politics, on that night, were held in abeyance as guests clicked glasses, gave toasts and exchanged light banters. Ironically, two days later, the party was over. Sunday Trust, in an expose, blew the whistle on Etteh and since then, the Speaker and her supporters, in the last one month, have been on the defensive. What did she do?

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki's Personal Touch to Campaign
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki's Personal Touch to Campaign
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki embarked on a new one-to-one campaign style in Western Province, where he paid a surprise visit to a peasant widow and addressed villagers in her compound.
It was a day that appeared planned to allow the President to appeal directly to the grassroots, and the Head of State wasted no time in making an impassioned plea for votes.

Original_Search_Title: Apologise And Pay Up - Bunyoro Tells Britain
Headline: Uganda: Apologise And Pay Up - Bunyoro Tells Britain
Body_Text:
This is the second part of a series in which Angelo Izama writes about the Bunyoro Kingdom's demands from the United Kingdom over "war crimes" it inflicted against the Banyoro
Queen Elizabeth II , the head of the Commonwealth, a club of former colonies and properties of imperial and colonial Britain, is facing a fresh lawsuit this month, a $4trillion lawsuit reportedly filed on behalf of Indians who were brought into Malaysia as labourers by colonial Britain.

Original_Search_Title: Man Arrested Over Dead Son
Headline: Uganda: Man Arrested Over Dead Son
Body_Text:
Mukono — Police in Mukono are investigating a case in which a 34 -year-old man is suspected to have connived with a traditional healer to sacrifice his 1- year -old son.
Mwesige David a mechanic at Kisowera Trading Centre is alleged to have connived with Geoffrey Sajjabi a.k.a Kasaada, a resident of Kigunga village to murder Derrick Mwesige in April. The two are said to have buried the child at night. They are held at Mukono Police.

Original_Search_Title: Seminars Set to Advance Cause of Science
Headline: Zimbabwe: Seminars Set to Advance Cause of Science
Body_Text:
Harare — The Tobacco Research Board has launched science seminar series to be held once every month to raise awareness on science and unleash the creative and innovative potential of Zimbabwean scientists.
TRB general manager Dr Anxious Masuka told scientists and policymakers who attended the first high-profile science seminar series at Kutsaga Research Station recently that the seminars would aim to keep the "scientific flame" alive in Zimbabwe.

Original_Search_Title: Obone, the Don Who Shunned Comfort for Love of Books
Headline: Uganda: Obone, the Don Who Shunned Comfort for Love of Books
Body_Text:
Kampala — A rancid smell of age-old paper hit my nose as soon as I stepped at the doorway. I was in Ntinda, a Kampala suburb, at the home of Prof. Aloysius Edeke Obone, the former Makerere University economics lecturer, to pick up for review, his fourth and latest book.
The 77-year-old professor emeritus, also former Ugandan high commissioner to Ghana (1964-66) and former headmaster of Kiira College Butiki (1968-74), had been waiting for me outside his diminutive residence for probably an hour when I arrived.

Original_Search_Title: How to Survive Life in a Queue
Headline: Zimbabwe: How to Survive Life in a Queue
Body_Text:
IT wasn't until a woman in front of me barked angrily "What did you say? Women are responsible for all the hell we are going through? I am a woman. How am I to blame for all this male-made nightmare? How many women are in the Cabinet? Is the president a woman?"
Until then, I had not realised I had either been hallucinating or daydreaming or speaking in tongues -- or something. I had been standing in the queue -- I am not sure for what -- for ages.

Original_Search_Title: Mudavadi's Position Divides Bukusu Elders
Headline: Kenya: Mudavadi's Position Divides Bukusu Elders
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Former Vice-President Mr Musalia Mudavadi's ranking as Mr Raila Odinga's number two in ODM has left the Bukusu Council of Elders divided.
The elders cannot agree on whether to heed the prophecy of former Dini Ya Musambwa sect leader, the late Elijah Masinde, or follow the whims of area MPs.

Original_Search_Title: ODM Oils Its Campaign Machine
Headline: Kenya: ODM Oils Its Campaign Machine
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Orange Democratic Movement presidential candidate, Mr Raila Odinga, arrives from the US later this week to join his team in navigating the most treacherous part of the road to State House.
Raila told The Sunday Standard that his party hits the trail for a three-day non-stop tour of the western circuit that will take its Pentagon members to Kisii, Kericho and Kakamega between September 22 and 24.

Original_Search_Title: Uhuru Walks Tightrope As Elections Draw Near
Headline: Kenya: Uhuru Walks Tightrope As Elections Draw Near
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Official opposition party Kanu took a daring step this week to consider a partnership with others, including President Kibaki's, whatever it will be. But the big question is whether the move marks the beginning of the end for the independence party or if it offers the opportunity for a fresh beginning?
While top officials such as chief whip Justin Muturi believe that the former governing party will come out the strongest party in the new order, although other officials elected in January 2005 have bolted to other parties.

Original_Search_Title: Principles Matter Little in Politics
Headline: Kenya: Principles Matter Little in Politics
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In 1992, Mombasa witnessed the biggest political rally in its history when the father figure of Kenya's opposition politics, Jaramogi Oginga Odinga, hit town accompanied by arguably the country's finest politicians.
These were heady days when President Moi, pushed to the wall by internal clamour for more democratic space and the external donors, had finally acceded to the reforms.

Original_Search_Title: In Central Kenya, It's a Question of the Best Strategy for Victory
Headline: Kenya: In Central Kenya, It's a Question of the Best Strategy for Victory
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As the clock ticks towards the December General Election so is anxiety mounting in President Kibaki's central Kenya backyard about the best political strategy for the region to ensure victory.
But local leaders cannot make any definite decision until the President announces the party on whose ticket he will seek a second term in office.

Original_Search_Title: American Cultural Centre Activities
Headline: Namibia: American Cultural Centre Activities
Body_Text:
Windhoek — Come and join us every Wednesday evening in September at 17h30 for the free screening of the films listed and described below.
The American Cultural Centre also invites you to a digital video conference on September 18 with Michael Kurtz, a production manager at the Putumayo World Music Label, who will talk to prominent figures in the making, distribution and protection of Namibian music and its artists.

Original_Search_Title: Nnabagereka Launches Anti-Sugar Daddy Campaign
Headline: Uganda: Nnabagereka Launches Anti-Sugar Daddy Campaign
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Nnabagereka, Sylvia Nagginda, has embarked on a campaign against sugar daddies and cross-generational sex, reports Josephine Maseruka.
Under the drive supported by the Population Services International, she will this month launch a radio programme for teenagers on Central Broadcasting Service, a local FM station owned by the Buganda Kingdom.

Original_Search_Title: Insurers Concerned About Political, Regulatory Pressure
Headline: South Africa: Insurers Rue Political, Regulatory Pressure
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — SOUTH African insurers have identified political and regulatory pressure as one of the main risks facing them, according to results of an international survey released yesterday.
The survey said the findings mirrored global concerns about the growing political interference, particularly in markets where governments regulate insurance products and prices.

Original_Search_Title: Who Set the House on Fire?
Headline: Nigeria: Who Set the House on Fire?
Body_Text:
Lagos — As a professional hairdresser, Hon. Patricia Etteh, Speaker of the House of Representatives, should have known one or two things about keeping her colleagues looking good.
The little details about washing, setting and styling-as the case may be-are things that she should ordinarily have taken care of without too much effort. But the House is looking so ugly now you wonder what has happened to the skills of the first female Speaker in Africa.

Original_Search_Title: Forced Onto the Streets to Please the Men
Headline: Uganda: Forced Onto the Streets to Please the Men
Body_Text:
Kampala — UGANDA is being refreshed as it prepares for the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting. There is a lot of cleaning and refurbishment of infrastructure countrywide.
However, one problem remains, the children who scattered on the streets of the pearl of Africa. The children are an everyday annoyance, whining insistently for the sh100 coin, so much so that their rehearsed whimpers now blur into the background as you stroll the pavements.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki Allies in Waiting Fatigue
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki Allies in Waiting Fatigue
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki's allies are in a waiting fatigue. They are asking just how long they have to wait for direction.
When Nicollo Machiavelli wrote in The Prince that "unarmed prophets always come to grieve, while armed prophets always conquer", he might as well have had Kibaki in mind.

Original_Search_Title: A Whiff of Nigeria's Millionaire Estates
Headline: Nigeria: A Whiff of Nigeria's Millionaire Estates
Body_Text:
Lagos — Bennett Oghifo writes that it is practically difficult to determine which estates belong to millionaires or billionaires, without first knowing who they are.
Well, who are the people that can be called millionaires? Where and how do they live? The only indices pointing in the direction of homes for millionaires only are probably some stress-free features ingrained in them for ease of life.

Original_Search_Title: Cry, Do Not Laugh, Beloved Land
Headline: Namibia: Cry, Do Not Laugh, Beloved Land
Body_Text:
Windhoek — Turning and turning in the widening gyre,
the falcon cannot hear the falconer;

Original_Search_Title: Trade Fair Boosts Limpopo-Namibia Links
Headline: South Africa: Trade Fair Boosts Limpopo-Namibia Links
Body_Text:
Windhoek — Limpopo's participation in the 2007 Annual Ongwendiva Trade Fair heralded a new era in cementing the socio-economic ties between the province and Namibia and in particular, the people of the northern regions.
"It is our strong view that this second participation in the Trade Fair represents a quantum leap in our endeavour to attain regional integration as instructed by the Provincial Growth and Development Strategy (PGDS)," said the provincial Department of Economic Development, Environment and Tourism on Thursday.

Original_Search_Title: Bata's Diary
Headline: Uganda: Bata's Diary
Body_Text:
Kampala — South Africa-based defender Timothy Batabaire is the assistant captain of the Uganda Cranes. He'll be running an exclusive diary from the Cranes camp daily for The New Vision.
6.00am: Woke up a bit late. And that's because I slept at 10pm - which is 'a bit late' by camp rules.

Original_Search_Title: Family Demands Funeral Refund
Headline: Uganda: Family Demands Funeral Refund
Body_Text:
Kampala — A FAMILY in Bushenyi district which buried a body thought to be their son's wants the Government to refund the funeral expenses, reports Chris Ahimbisibwe.
"The UPDF should come and exhume the body and refund the expenses we incurred in organising the funeral," Mzee Emmanuel Kasubure told The New Vision.

Original_Search_Title: Between Soludo And Blind Obedience
Headline: Nigeria: Between Soludo And Blind Obedience
Body_Text:
Lagos — The current imbroglio at the presidency has many sides to it. First, it unveils the slow and feeble nature of our public service. Here is the case of a vibrant Professor of Economics, bubbling with life and certainly desirous of bringing a lot of positive changes to his country. At a time when he is embarking on a recovery mission for the Naira, he finds himself being held down by our undue reliance on legalism.
Like his boss, Vice President Goodluck Jonathan, he probably also believes that "In the affairs of man, there comes a time when even the law must take a second place, and the public mood must define our actions". But how often must the law be consigned to the second place?

Original_Search_Title: Eroding the Gains of Free Education
Headline: Kenya: Eroding the Gains of Free Education
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The fishmonger, who specialises in Mgongo Wazi (filletless fish bones) at Kisumu's Koginga Beach, says she cannot afford to send her five children to school.
"Employing adults is too costly. Besides, where do I get the money to buy school uniforms and for bus fare?" she asks.

Original_Search_Title: Senate - the Challenge Before Information Managers
Headline: Nigeria: Senate - the Challenge Before Information Managers
Body_Text:
Lagos — Senator Victor Ndoma-Egba (SAN), representing Cross River Central Senatorial Zone, was Chairman of the Senate Committee on Media and Publicity under the leadership of former Senate President, Senator Kenechukwu Nnamani. His solid legal background was no inhibition to him in the discharge of his duty as spokesperson of the Upper Legislative Chamber.
Easy going and unassuming, Ndoma-Egba brought discipline to bear in the management of information in the Senate, challenging as it were those whose area of specialization is information and media management. He brought panache into the task execution. He never allowed the media representatives in the Senate to catch him on the wrong foot. He was always on his toes in anticipation of inquiries into the activities of the House. There was a litany of them.

Original_Search_Title: Equity Fund to Expand Presence in Region
Headline: East Africa: Equity Fund to Expand Presence in Region
Body_Text:
Nairobi — DEG, one of Europe's largest development finance institutions, is planning to enlarge its equity portfolio in East Africa, mainly in Kenya.
It will concentrate on acquiring equity in private infrastructure projects in which Industrial Promotion Services-Kenya (IPSK) will act as the local link, according to DEG regional director Eric Kaleja.

Original_Search_Title: Traders Stranded in Amuru
Headline: Uganda: Traders Stranded in Amuru
Body_Text:
Kampala — OVER 100 trailers and thousands of traders heading to South Sudan are stranded at Pawel-Lalem in Atiak sub-county, Amuru district, because of the impassable roads.
The assistant commissioner of the Ministry of Works, Balamu Bisuti, said a team had been sent to salvage the situation by covering the roads with murram and installing culverts.

Original_Search_Title: Banana Skins Niger Pose a Covert Threat to Superior Cranes
Headline: Uganda: Banana Skins Niger Pose a Covert Threat to Superior Cranes
Body_Text:
Kampala — UGANDA collides with Niger in a decisive Nations Cup mismatch this weekend that wouldn't have been permitted to proceed past the weigh-in had it been a boxing title duel.
Separated by 48 spots in the Fifa rankings, the two nations are in different weight categories; dictating that 146th-ranked Niger will need to punch above its weight to wreck Uganda's Nations Cup dream.

Original_Search_Title: Resign Or Be Impeached, CNPP Tells Etteh, Nguroje
Headline: Nigeria: Resign Or Be Impeached, CNPP Tells Etteh, Nguroje
Body_Text:
Abuja — The Conference of Nigerian Political Parties (CNPP), has warned the Speaker of the House of Representatives, Patricia Olubunmi Etteh and her deputy, Alhaji Babaginda Nguroje, to resign or face impeachment over the alleged N628 million renovation of their official residence.
National Publicity Secretary of CNPP, Osita Okechukwu, gave the warning in a statement he issued yesterday in Abuja. He described the incident as "a monumental corruption against the suffering masses of Nigeria."

Original_Search_Title: Isingiro Hailstorm Victims Get Food
Headline: Uganda: Isingiro Hailstorm Victims Get Food
Body_Text:
Isingiro — Over 450 families in Nyakitunda, Isingiro District, who were affected by a hailstorm on August 15, have received food relief from the Relief and Disaster Preparedness ministry.
Isingiro North MP Alex Byarugaba delivered 180 bags of posho and 80 bags of beans to the victims on Wednesday.

Original_Search_Title: Stormy Week as House of Reps Resumes
Headline: Nigeria: Stormy Week as House of Reps Resumes
Body_Text:
Lagos — As Reps resume from their seven-week recess to deliberate on the numerous allegations against the speaker, ABIODUN ADELAJA, our House of Representatives correspondent who has been following unfolding events at the lower chamber, writes on what to expect.
If there are three very important issues that will engage the serious attention of the House of Representatives as members resume for legislative activities after a seven-week vacation, they will be Etteh, Etteh and Etteh. The embattled speaker, Mrs Patricia Olubunmi Etteh will tomorrow, Tuesday, come face-to-face with her colleagues some of whom have accused her of alleged complicity in the award of contracts for the renovation of her official residence and that of her deputy Mr Babangida Nguroje. Etteh is in the eye of an emerging storm over the controversial N632 million contract.

Original_Search_Title: Soludo - Triumph and Near Tragedy
Headline: Nigeria: Soludo - Triumph and Near Tragedy
Body_Text:
The battle line is drawn and hardening with each passing day. The billion naira question is: Will Soludo survive the impending war as he did that of the banking consolidation? The answer is: may be --Dele Sobowale, Sunday Vanguard, August 19, 2007, p 7.
FORGIVE me for quoting myself but it is merely designed to remind our readers of the fears that were expressed immediately after the Governor of Central Bank of Nigeria, CBN announced the new STRATEGIC ALLIANCE FOR THE NAIRA on Tuesday, August 14, 2007. The Governor received a standing ovation for another virtuoso performance from the audience except for a few. One of the few not standing and clapping was the "Duke", Mr Nduka Obaigbena, the Publisher of THISDAY, sitting very close to me. The Duke was not convinced by the governor. I was also not standing.

Original_Search_Title: Six Foolproof Rules for Getting Your Way in Politics
Headline: Kenya: Six Foolproof Rules for Getting Your Way in Politics
Body_Text:
Nairobi — WHATEVER YOUR TAKE on former President Moi, there's no getting away from the fact that he still wields considerable influence. Some might even say he still runs Kenya, at least as the puppet master pulling not very invisible strings behind the scenes.
Mr Moi's latest reincarnation as the tie-breaker on the political direction that Kenya takes must surely qualify as the latest new wonder of the world. The cycle is complete. Welcome home, Baba.

Original_Search_Title: Paddy Bitama's Musical Debut
Headline: Uganda: Paddy Bitama's Musical Debut
Body_Text:
They say a career is born in public - talent in privacy and for a long time Paddy Bitama kept secret his other talent. We all know him for his funny frolics but little did we know that Amarula's one third could put his voice to use. Paddy Ssali alias Bitama makes his debut with two tracks, Kifefe and Eryenvu (the banana) and expects to release his six-track album soon.
Kifefe, he says, is a song inspired by a personal relationship.

Original_Search_Title: American Cultural Programme
Headline: Namibia: American Cultural Programme
Body_Text:
Windhoek — September is Sports Movies Month at the American Cultural Centre. Come and join us every Wednesday evening in September at 17h30 for the free screening of the films listed and described below.
The monthly book reading day will be held on Wednesday, September 12, at 09h30. The American Cultural Centre also invites you to a digital video conference on September 18 with Michael Kurtz, a production manager at the Putumayo World Music Label, who will talk to prominent figures in the making, distribution and protection of Namibian music and its artists.

Original_Search_Title: MPs Scuttle Kibaki's Sh1 Million-a-Plate Lunch
Headline: Kenya: MPs Scuttle Kibaki's Sh1 Million-a-Plate Lunch
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki's much-publicised Sh1 million-a-plate luncheon has been cancelled following suspicion between organisers and Narc-Kenya politicians, The Standard has learnt.
The official reason given by the 'Kibaki Tena' team for the cancellation of the luncheon at Nairobi's prestigious Safari Park Hotel was that it coincided with the special ODM National Delegates Conference at Kasarani Sports Complex, only a few kilometres away.

Original_Search_Title: Ex-Street Children Seek Help
Headline: Uganda: Ex-Street Children Seek Help
Body_Text:
Kampala — A group of orphans and former street children from Kampala and Mpigi Districts have appealed to good samaritans to contribute towards the construction of a spacious home for them.
The children made the appeal at a breakfast meeting in Kampala on August 25.

Original_Search_Title: 'Graduated Tax' Bounces Back
Headline: Uganda: 'Graduated Tax' Bounces Back
Body_Text:
ANY gainfully employed person who earns at least Shs1 million will pay Shs100,000 worth of Local Service Tax per year if the new Local Governments (amendment) Bill is approved in its present form.
The draft law, which was introduced to Parliament yesterday by the State Minister for Local Government, Ms Hope Mwesigye, will replace the Graduated Tax that was scrapped in 2005.

Original_Search_Title: An Eyewitness Denies That Nshamihigo Committed Murder
Headline: Rwanda: An Eyewitness Denies That Nshamihigo Committed Murder
Body_Text:
A witness denied Tuesday before the International Criminal Tribunal for Rwanda (ICTR) that Siméon Nshamihigo, a former magistrate, accused of genocide and crimes against humanity, committed murder as the prosecutor alleges.
The protected witness HMN, 18th since the beginning of the defence case on 23 April, described the circumstances of the death of Théoneste Karangwa, a rich Tutsi businessman, on 7 April 1994 in Cyangugu. The ICTR prosecutor attributes the responsibility of this murder to Siméon Nshamihigo. The defendant has pled not guilty.

Original_Search_Title: No One is Above the Law and No One is Below It
Headline: Mauritius: No One is Above the Law and No One is Below It
Body_Text:
Port Louis — "I cannot quite understand how things like this happen. In any case, during the days of my father (Sir Seewoosagur), not any Tom, Dick and Harry could have access to the Parliament".
This is the English translation of a statement the Prime minister Navin Ramgoolam is quoted as having made about the incident in the Lunch Room of the Parliament last Friday.

Original_Search_Title: Artists Want a Say in Film Industry
Headline: Namibia: Artists Want a Say in Film Industry
Body_Text:
Windhoek — THE Oruuano Artists Union of Namibia has sent a petition to the Minister of Information and Broadcasting, Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah, demanding an independent investigation into the film industry in Namibia.
They want the Minister to consider their grievances and to find a solution before any new filming is permitted in Namibia.

Original_Search_Title: Kampeu Remained Faithful to Township Jazz
Headline: Zimbabwe: Kampeu Remained Faithful to Township Jazz
Body_Text:
Harare — THERE was a time when the majority of Zimbabwean musicians were hooked onto everything else but township jazz.
This was the time when rock "n" roll had taken over and many musicians would play nothing else but just that.

Original_Search_Title: Anyim's Place in PDP Chairmanship Race
Headline: Nigeria: Anyim's Place in PDP Chairmanship Race
Body_Text:
Lagos — Given the circumstance under which Anyim Pius Anyim, a lawyer and former President of the Senate dignifyingly walked away after his tenure in 2003, one had reasoned that he must have found politics an uncharted territory and therefore decided to bid farewell to the strange profession.
In spite of the loud ovation that greeted his principled conduct of the affairs of the Senate, Anyim was not persuaded to re-contest the 2003 general elections. Many felt he was frustrated out while others thought he was too much on the gentle and humble side of life to cope with the bedlam and uncertainty that normally engulfed the Nigerian electoral process. Still millions of Nigerians see his stepping aside in the midst of the 2003 debacle as an act of courage and integrity.

Original_Search_Title: Board Seeks to Market Tea Based On Area of Origin
Headline: Kenya: Board Seeks to Market Tea Based On Area of Origin
Body_Text:
Earnings from tea could rise four times following the industry regulator's efforts to market the commodity based on its area of origin.
Officials from the Tea Board of Kenya (TBK) will next week tour establishments in Sri Lanka, which is already reaping premium earnings through the concept known as Geographical Indications.

Original_Search_Title: Haco to Launch Flavoured Straws
Headline: Kenya: Haco to Launch Flavoured Straws
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Haco Industries Limited will launch flavoured straws, the first such product in the local market, with the objective of boosting milk consumption among children.
Sipahh milk flavouring straws were developed by Unistraw International, an Australian firm, in 2005. Haco has entered into an exclusive contract with Unistraw's South African distributor to distribute the straws in Kenya and the region including Uganda, Tanzania, and Rwanda starting in September.

Original_Search_Title: Village Turns to Rituals After Landslide
Headline: Kenya: Village Turns to Rituals After Landslide
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Several weeks ago, Khuvasali village in Kakamega was engulfed by mourning.
A devastating mudslide hit the area and claimed eight lives in a quiet village whose name means prayer.

Original_Search_Title: Pain of Hiked Fuel Prices Now Felt In All Areas Inflation Rate Rises to 9 Percent
Headline: Tanzania: Pain of Hiked Fuel Prices Now Felt In All Areas Inflation Rate Rises to 9 Percent
Body_Text:
Arusha — The 2007 budget has started to backfire on people of all walks of life.
Commuters in Arusha are complaining about the rise of bus fare effective last month following the decision of the Surface and Marine Transport Regulatory Authority (SUMATRA) to increase fare by 20 per cent.

Original_Search_Title: Rapists On the Prowl -Why Cases Rise-NHRC
Headline: Nigeria: Rapists On the Prowl -Why Cases Rise-NHRC
Body_Text:
Kaduna — Why do rape cases seem so much on the rise? Why are rapists now targeting mostly three-year-olds? There are no easy answers to these questions. But what is gaining unanimity is that the law punishing rapists is too lax and most of the victims hail from poor homes.
