Deputy director and head of legal and investigation of the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC), an agency at the forefront of the fight against rape, Mr. Z.O Sebanjo told Weekly Trust: "Poverty is at the centre of rape. The law punishing rape must also be reformed. There must be a law reform on the crime of rape. There is no way a rapist should get less than life jail."

Original_Search_Title: Mob Justice Thrives
Headline: Uganda: Mob Justice Thrives
Body_Text:
Kampala — JANE ROSE Nassuuna was stoned to death. Her crime? A brother in law accused her of witchcraft. Then local council leaders called a village meeting, during which Nassuuna tried in vain to defend herself. As the gathering passed a 'death sentence,' Nassuuna took to her heels. But soon the mob of about 50 villagers caught up with her at a family friend's doorway as she tried to dive into the house for protection.
Nassuuna's daughter, Mary Nagawa, tried to shield her but she too fled for her own safety as the stones rained. So did Nassuuna's husband and all their children.

Original_Search_Title: Macos Stands in Kololo Bid
Headline: Uganda: Macos Stands in Kololo Bid
Body_Text:
Kampala — KOLOLO SSS must tread past potential banana skins, Makerere College School (MACOS) to shrug off the "one-man team" tag that detractors have affixed onto them going into the first semifinal today.
The reigning champions have breezed through the Coca-Cola Schools Cricket Week without defeat due to the run-hungry, aggressive batting of opener Roger Mukasa.

Original_Search_Title: Marketplace Where Makutano Series is Shot
Headline: Kenya: Marketplace Where Makutano Series is Shot
Body_Text:
The loud signpost at the entrance of Greenacres School in Tigoni silences you as you venture into the 'dead silence' zone. This is where Makutano/Junction, the popular television series that has been running on Kenya Broadcasting Corporation television, is shot.
"We don't cough here, all mobile phones must be put on the silent mode," whispers Angie Musira, who is the third assistant director here. This is after one of our phones rings.

Original_Search_Title: Country, DRC Sign Multi-Sectoral Agreements
Headline: South Africa: Country, DRC Sign Multi-Sectoral Agreements
Body_Text:
Kinshasa — South Africa and the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) have signed agreements of co-operation in the fields of health and transport, amongst many others.
This happened on Tuesday during the fourth session of the South Africa - DRC Binational Commission, co-chaired by President Thabo Mbeki and his DRC counterpart President Joseph Kabila.

Original_Search_Title: Omuombo - Icon of Olympism
Headline: Kenya: Omuombo - Icon of Olympism
Body_Text:
Nairobi — When the National Olympic Committee Kenya (Nock) describes fallen sports administrator Tom Omuombo as a dedicated servant, they know they will truly miss the man widely referred to as the Mr Fix it of Kenyan sports.
For twenty years, Omuombo served as a Nock official and even at the time of his death last Wednesday in Beijing, he was on Olympic duty.

Original_Search_Title: Africa Wages War on Scourge of Plastic Bags
Headline: Africa Wages War on Scourge of Plastic Bags
Body_Text:
Port Louis — Many environment-conscious African countries are taking new measures to curb the plastic bag frenzy that seems to have overtaken the continent these past years.
Many animals, including endangered species like this African Spurred Tortoise, choke to death after swallowing plastic bags.

Original_Search_Title: All Set for Heroes Day Celebrations
Headline: Namibia: All Set for Heroes Day Celebrations
Body_Text:
Oshikango — Preparations for the Heroes Day celebrations at Eenhana on 26 August are progressing according to plan and are at an advanced stage.
The Governor of Ohangwena Region, Usko Nghaamwa, made the announcement when he received donations from individuals, organisations and businesses in Ohangwena Region last Friday.

Original_Search_Title: Kasama Roads Get Facelift
Headline: Zambia: Kasama Roads Get Facelift
Body_Text:
Ndola — MAMMOTH road works have been initiated by the Government through the Road Development Agency (RDA) countrywide, a sign that the nation is on path to economic recovery.
While the works may be clearly evident especially in the urban areas, the bulk of the works have been commissioned in rural provincial centres linking distant outlaying areas and the hinterland.

Original_Search_Title: Dandy Cartoon Magazine Hits Nigerian Newsstand
Headline: Nigeria: Dandy Cartoon Magazine Hits Nigerian Newsstand
Body_Text:
Lagos — A British household name in cartoon magazine, "Dandy" has hit the Nigerian newsstand.
The 24-page monthly full colour publication is to be found in supermarkets, popular eateries and bookshops to put to an end the problem of one shuttling the shores of the great country Nigeria and Britain just for a monthly copy of it.

Original_Search_Title: Why Kibaale Man Murdered Family
Headline: Uganda: Why Kibaale Man Murdered Family
Body_Text:
Kibaale — Friday started like any other normal day in Kagadi town in Kibaale District. The sun was bright and business went on as usual. Little did residents know that a tragedy had occurred in one of the wards of the town council.
Abdallah Byekwaso, a 28-year-old man, had beheaded his entire family and hanged himself. Police only identified the deceased children as Nakyanzi, 4, Tugume, 6, Byekwaso,9, Basemera,11, Nabukirwa and Namagembe. Not even did he spare his wife Annet Namu who also lay beside her children.

Original_Search_Title: Scoring Business Points Using Music
Headline: Africa: Scoring Business Points Using Music
Body_Text:
The recent live 8 music concerts were a clear manifestation of how powerful music is in effectively sending messages.
Global audiences have been brought to the attention of social responsibility activities by the magic of a lyrical whip.

Original_Search_Title: Govs, Deputies to Attend Anti-Corruption Retreat
Headline: Nigeria: Govs, Deputies to Attend Anti-Corruption Retreat
Body_Text:
Lagos — Governors and their deputies, and Speakers of Houses of Assembly are to attend a national retreat on ethics and good governance.
The two-day retreat, which would begin on August 24 in Abuja, would be facilitated by EFCC, ICPC, Code of Conduct Bureau, Due Process and National Planning Commission (NPC).

Original_Search_Title: Orange Rebuilds As Raila Says Kalonzo is Welcome
Headline: Kenya: Orange Rebuilds As Raila Says Kalonzo is Welcome
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Orange Democratic Party begins to rebuild today from the region it enjoys massive support but where it is also likely to face strong resistance.
Presidential aspirants Mr Raila Odinga, Mr Musalia Mudavadi, Mr William Ruto, Mr Najib Balala and Mr Joe Nyagah are expected at the 64 Stadium, Eldoret, for the party's first rally after it was reclaimed early last week.

Original_Search_Title: Minister Throws Out NRZ Report
Headline: Zimbabwe: Minister Throws Out NRZ Report
Body_Text:
Harare — The  Minister of Transport and Communications, Cde Christopher Mushohwe, has rejected a report prepared by National Railways of Zimbabwe officials explaining the cause of last week's train collision.
In an interview yesterday, Cde Mushohwe said the report, delivered to him this week, was not comprehensive and fell short of what he had asked for.

Original_Search_Title: I Do Not Care Who Develops Nakasero
Headline: Uganda: I Do Not Care Who Develops Nakasero
Body_Text:
Kampala — Interview of the week,  Maj. Gen. Kahinda Otafiire
Our press is irresponsible," sighed Maj. Gen. Kahinda Otafiire as he greeted us in his office on Thursday evening. "You tell a story and they add their imagination." Nevertheless, the minister for local government spared two hours to talk to Sunday Vision about the problems with KCC, land allocations, the creation of new districts, intrigue in NRM and the media.

Original_Search_Title: How Raila Group Acquired New Party
Headline: Kenya: How Raila Group Acquired New Party
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Fear of a protracted court case over the ownership of ODM-Kenya was the reason a group of presidential aspirants initiated moves to acquire a new party.
Langata MP Raila Odinga and ODM presidential aspirants William Ruto, Musalia Mudavadi and Najib Balala set out to convince lawyer Mugambi Imanyara to hand over the ODM party when it became apparent that they did not have sufficient time to mount campaigns if the case over ODM-K's ownership dragged on in court.

Original_Search_Title: Shareholder Mutiny Rocks Trust AGM
Headline: Zimbabwe: Shareholder Mutiny Rocks Trust AGM
Body_Text:
Harare — TRUST Holdings Limited (THL), suspended from the Zimbabwe Stock Exchange (ZSE) in 2004 following a liquidity crunch, last week stumbled on its path to a come-back after shareholders rejected special resolutions tabled at its fifth annual general meeting (AGM).
The move effectively means that the disposal of key assets by the group last year is null and void, something likely to force management and directors into a spot of bother as they are likely to face serious obstacles in trying to wrestle back the assets from current holders.

Original_Search_Title: Flood Victims Relocation Begins
Headline: Namibia: Flood Victims Relocation Begins
Body_Text:
Windhoek — The relocation of thousands of villagers in the Caprivi Region, who were displaced by floods a few months ago, is expected to start today.
The Acting Director of the Directorate Emergency Management in the Office of the Prime Minister, Gabriel Kangowa, told New Era yesterday that the transportation of flood victims from temporary camps to their permanent dwellings starts today at 06h00.

Original_Search_Title: Ford Asili Joins Scramble for Kigumo in a Large Field of 18
Headline: Kenya: Ford Asili Joins Scramble for Kigumo in a Large Field of 18
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Veteran politician Kenneth Matiba's former Ford Asili party has joined the scramble for the control of Kigumo politics where President Kibaki's friendly parties are already on the ground.
And what has greatly changed the politics of the constituency held by Mr Onesmus Kihara Mwangi is the latest entry of Ms Ruth Mwaniki.

Original_Search_Title: Aspirants Jostle for ODM Ticket in Machage's Kuria
Headline: Kenya: Aspirants Jostle for ODM Ticket in Machage's Kuria
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Kuria MP Wilfred Machage has ditched Narc-Kenya to join the Democratic Party - both friendly to President Mwai Kibaki - in a move seen as calculated at giving him an easy nomination.
Dr Machage, an assistant minister for Health, quit at a time when his opponent in Narc-K, lawyer Kerario Marwa, had gone to court to challenge the party elections the MP had conducted.

Original_Search_Title: Sugar War Brews Up
Headline: Uganda: Sugar War Brews Up
Body_Text:
Jinja — The Ministry of Tourism, Trade and Industry has drafted a bill to cool the simmering bad relationship between sugar companies and sugar cane out growers.
The acrimony is two pronged.

Original_Search_Title: Genesis of State War on the Media
Headline: Kenya: Genesis of State War on the Media
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As the controversial Media Bill 2007- which aims at scaring away crucial news sources that would like to remain anonymous - awaits President Kibaki's assent , spirited attempts by the current administration to gag the Press can be traced back to embarrassing scandals exposed by the Fourth Estate.
They include the multi-billion shillings Anglo Leasing and Goldenberg scams, in which top Government officials were allegedly involved. The fictitious Artur brothers' saga also ranks highly among embarrassing exposes.

Original_Search_Title: The Premiership At Zero Price
Headline: Uganda: The Premiership At Zero Price
Body_Text:
Kampala — The English Barclays Premier League is back with an interesting twist. NTV has joined the broadcast rights fray and is quite contented with the one match a week allocation it acquired. That may pale in comparison to what GTV and DStv got but NTV is set to redefine the free-to-air broadcasting yet again.
NTV went all out with a media blitz that sought to tell viewers they do not have to pay a penny to enjoy the Premiership. That counts for something if watching from a kibanda (makeshift video hall) or a noisy bar is not your cup of tea. NTV has left its competition in the dust yet again.

Original_Search_Title: Train Collision - State Summons Top NRZ Officials
Headline: Zimbabwe: Train Collision - State Summons Top NRZ Officials
Body_Text:
Harare — TWO National Railways of Zimbabwe senior officials were yesterday summoned by Government to explain the cause of the train collision that occurred on Thursday morning in Harare while the driver of the goods train has since been suspended.
The two senior NRZ officials - the Eastern Region area manager Mr Stanley Murenje and operations and marketing director Mr Mazi Banana - were summoned by the Minister of Transport and Communications Cde Christopher Mushohwe to his offices in Harare as investigations continued.

Original_Search_Title: The Ban On Buveera Must Be Enforced
Headline: Uganda: The Ban On Buveera Must Be Enforced
Body_Text:
Kampala — JESCA Eriyo is THE Minister of State for the Environment and Woman MP for Adjumani District. She discussed with John Odyek the salient issues regarding the recent ban of polythene bags (buveera) of 30 microns density and below.
While environmental groups have welcomed the ban, the public has expressed concern over the absence of alternatives, and also questioned the Government's commitment to enforcing the ban.

Original_Search_Title: Cane Growing Main Issue As Kombo Seeks Another Term
Headline: Kenya: Cane Growing Main Issue As Kombo Seeks Another Term
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Campaigns in the constituency are intensifying as the incumbent, Local Government minister Musikari Kombo, contemplates joining the presidential race.
And violence has set in as his possible rivals' supporters clash. Mr Kombo, the Ford Kenya chairman, has often said he in the presidential race, although his actions of late do not show it.

Original_Search_Title: Dream to Promised Land and MPs Who Avoid the House Business
Headline: Kenya: Dream to Promised Land and MPs Who Avoid the House Business
Body_Text:
Nairobi — I am still dreaming of being made a Mheshimiwa. I want to be given a giant wallet that suffers from obesity.
For, when you are a Mheshimiwa with a fat wallet, you bamboozle the world with the belief that you are a lawmaker, working for wananchi.

Original_Search_Title: Stepmother Murders Two Year-Old Boy
Headline: Sierra Leone: Stepmother Murders Two Year-Old Boy
Body_Text:
Freetown — Regional Crime Officer East, Samuel Sani Sesay Monday confirmed the murder of a two-year-old boy Fayia Hallies by his stepmother Tenneh Hallie in Yallamba village, Kissi Tongi chiefdom, Kailahun District.
Sesay said the ugly incident took place on Friday and the suspect has admitted to have fed her step-son with caustic soda which resulted in his death.

Original_Search_Title: Bobi's Sh400m Mansion
Headline: Uganda: Bobi's Sh400m Mansion
Body_Text:
Kampala — WE can confirm to you that Solomon Kampala, Bobi Wine's son's second birthday party did not cost anything near the sh28m, he claimed it would cost last week. However, what we can tell you about the Ghetto President's home is that, after four years of construction, it is ready for tenancy.
Guests, who attended the the house warming on Wednesday night, can attest to that. They comprised mainly musicians, actors, radio personalities, scribes and Omulangira Junju, Kabaka Ronald Mutebi's son. Estimated at about sh400m, the storeyed house seats on a sprawling 10-acre piece of land (which includes a sh18m forest) in Magere village off Gayaza Road.

Original_Search_Title: Union Demands Film Investigation
Headline: Namibia: Union Demands Film Investigation
Body_Text:
Windhoek — The country's only registered artists union has demanded an investigation by the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting into the worsening state of affairs in the local film industry.
The Oruuano Artists Union, self-proclaimed protector of the Namibian film industry in direct opposition to the government-recognized Filmmakers Association of Namibia, claims in the petition that Namibians are getting a raw deal with regard to employment opportunities.

Original_Search_Title: Use of Unique Material That Spells Fashion
Headline: Kenya: Use of Unique Material That Spells Fashion
Body_Text:
Nairobi — With increasing demand for unique jewellery, artists have embraced the use of rare raw materials to keep up with the thirst of consumers who want to wear a fashion mark.
US-trained Njee Muturi, who learnt part of the craft from his grandfather is one of them.

Original_Search_Title: Cabinet Must Come Clean On Road Contracts
Headline: Mauritius: Is Beebeejaun a Highway Man?
Body_Text:
Port Louis — What would be the outcome of the decision of the ministry of Finance to block at the last hour the award of a contract for the construction of a new bridge at Macondé to the tune of Rs 232 million. In any country, except in banana republics, enquiries and resignations would have immediately followed and disciplinary measures taken. But, as things stand in Mauritius, we can bet that nothing will be done.
To start with, the minister will make no comment. Neither him, nor the prime minister, nor those public officials responsible for drafting the tender documents think that they are answerable to the public. No one will dare ask them why they did not go for an international bid as advised by Luxconsult.

Original_Search_Title: Mbarara to Mix Uganda Waragi
Headline: Uganda: Mbarara to Mix Uganda Waragi
Body_Text:
Kampala — MBARARA will be the first beneficiary of the Uganda Waragi (UG) Mix a Million promotion when they host the first draw later this week. The promotion, which was launched at Centenary Park in July and later at Kamwokya Centre for the "In Bar" activities, was last week rolled out to Hotel Equatoria.
Oscar Mulira, Uganda Breweries' brand manager for spirits, says the promotion is an opportunity for UG drinkers to win sh1m. At Hotel Equatoria, the Uganda Breweries' sales manager, Michael Walekwa, explained to hotel and bar personnel the mechanisms of the promo, which they would later divulge to their customers.

Original_Search_Title: America's Family Jewels!
Headline: Nigeria: America's Family Jewels!
Body_Text:
"Our scientific power has outrun our spiritual power. We have guided missiles and misguided men"- Martin Luther King, Jr.
When the late Martin Luther King, Jr. uttered those words at the peak of the civil rights crises in America, the present occupant of the White House, George W. Bush, was coasting through college as an average student of history. Available records provided in abundance by the likes of Michael Moore also certainly suggest that the man craftily avoided the draft which saw him serve as an obscure airman in the National Guards [despite scoring only 25% on the written pilot's aptitude test] at a time his peers were being shot to smithereens in the jungles and paddy fields of Vietnam. He was helped no doubt by some rednecks in high places, and a conspiring father who lived to become the Director of the American Central Intelligence Agency [CIA in abbreviation] and also the 41st president of the United States.

Original_Search_Title: Regional Artistes Get Cold Shoulder At PAM Central Launch
Headline: Uganda: Regional Artistes Get Cold Shoulder At PAM Central Launch
Body_Text:
Kampala — AS the 2007 Pearl of Africa Music (PAM), Awards regional shows came to a close, the central launch at Colline Hotel in Mukono, showed that upcountry artistes still have a long way to go if they are to appeal to the central market.
Most of them were ignored by the crowd because their music was not familiar.

Original_Search_Title: Passing of Media Bill a Major Tragedy
Headline: Kenya: Passing of Media Bill a Major Tragedy
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A quote attributed to German philosopher and man of letters Johann Wolfgang Goethe summarises the crushing disappointment at the passage of the Media Bill by 29 MPs this past week: "Nothing is more disgusting than the majority: because it consists of a few powerful predecessors, of rogues who adapt themselves, of weak who assimilate themselves, and the masses who imitate without knowing at all what they want."
There was no enlightened debate, no philosophical interrogation of the concept of a free press and the universal freedom of all human beings to express themselves. MPs were happy to recount cases where they were criticised by the media, and this was for them enough reason impose curbs on freedom. In some liberal traditions, the freedom of the press is regarded as arising from the very humanity of man; it is claimed by the individual by virtue of his or her being human. In that (controversial) sense, it is a human right.

Original_Search_Title: President Hits Campaign Trail in West
Headline: Kenya: GNU Ministers Too Go West But for Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki heads to Western Province this Wednesday. This is at a time when political party leaders allied to him are cobbling up a new outfit for his re-election bid, while disagreements erupt in Narc-Kenya on how best to support him.
And while the leading opposition, ODM-Kenya, is fighting to stem an imminent fallout among its top presidential contenders, President Kibaki is sitting pretty and appears spoilt for choice of a re-election vehicle.

Original_Search_Title: More 'Beaucoup' Than Bo-Kaap, Ek Se
Headline: Zimbabwe: More 'Beaucoup' Than Bo-Kaap, Ek Se
Body_Text:
TUESDAY was a heavy night for members of Meikles Grapevine wine tasting club...especially those going on from knowledgeably sampling two South African whites and four reds to try the hotel's Cape Malay special week menu in La Fontaine.
We had been offered tiny samples of appetisers and soups to be served later, at the Cape wine tasting. Maybe not a bad thing as the guest chef-created dishes proved spectacularly non Bo-Kaapian in terms of helpings and - I, for at least one, thought -- not very Bo-Kaapian in flavours, aromas and textures.

Original_Search_Title: Need to Monitor Campaign Funds
Headline: Kenya: Need to Monitor Campaign Funds
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Should Kenya Introduce checks on campaign financing?
Since last year, leading ODM Kenya presidential candidates, Mr Raila Odinga and Mr Kalonzo Musyoka, have made whirlwind tours of Western capitals in what is believed to be fundraising efforts. What Kenyans don't know is the total amount of money they may have raised, and from which sources.

Original_Search_Title: Senate Committees - Mark Scaled the Hurdle, But...
Headline: Nigeria: Senate Committees - Mark Scaled the Hurdle, But...
Body_Text:
Lagos — Senate President, Senator David Mark penultimate Wednesday announced the composition of the 54 Special and Standing Committees of the Senate, thus setting the stage for possible discontent and quiet politicking in the upper legislative chamber, reports Sufuyan Ojeifo.
David Bonaventure Alechenu Mark, President of the Nigerian Senate, penultimate Wednesday, announced the chairmanship, vice chairmanship and membership of the fifty-four special and standing committees of the Senate. The retired Brigadier General with a whole lot of guts, understandably, took his time in dealing with the issue which had made tested politicians who occupied the position before him become tentative because of the negative reactions that had always followed it (committeesí composition). Mark and his leadership were not very confident that their selections were perfectly in order; hence, they had chosen to tarry in order to ensure that areas of friction and/or conflict were minimized.

Original_Search_Title: Govt to Send Oil Experts to Trinidad
Headline: Uganda: Govt to Send Oil Experts to Trinidad
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni will send a team of Ugandan oil experts to Trinidad and Tobago to study how the Caribbean country runs its oil sector.
Mr Museveni announced this yesterday on the first leg of his three-day visit to the country, a statement from the President's Office said, adding that Uganda stands to learn a lot from the Trinidad and Tobago experience.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni to Send Oil Experts to Tobago
Headline: Uganda: Museveni to Send Oil Experts to Tobago
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni wants to send Ugandan experts to Trinidad and Tobago to learn from the country's experience with oil production.
Museveni, who is on a three-day State visit to the Caribbean country, commended President George Maxwell Richards for their efforts to ensure value addition to petroleum resource.

Original_Search_Title: Does Your Home Town Deserve City Status?
Headline: Uganda: Does Your Home Town Deserve City Status?
Body_Text:
Kampala — There has been widespread talk and demands by municipalities to be accorded city status. Joshua Kato analyses why some towns do and others do not deserve the status
Uganda is the only country in East Africa with one city. Kenya has four: Nairobi, Mombasa, Kisumu and Eldoret, while Tanzania has Dar es Salaam, Dodoma and Arusha.

Original_Search_Title: Besigye Tours Butambala County
Headline: Uganda: Besigye Tours Butambala County
Body_Text:
Kampala — FORUM for Democratic Change (FDC) leader Dr. Kizza Besigye on Saturday toured Butambala county in Mpigi district to launch the sale of FDC party cards.
In spite of a truck with policemen being sighted during a stop-over in Mpigi, his journey was not impeded by the Police or any other authorities.

Original_Search_Title: Thugs Have Taken Over Oyo Politics and Turned Govt Purse to Theirs - Ajimobi
Headline: Nigeria: Thugs Have Taken Over Oyo Politics and Turned Govt Purse to Theirs - Ajimobi
Body_Text:
Lagos — Senator Abiola Ajimobi is a core technocrat who veered into politics after serving meritoriously as the Managing Director of National Oil and Chemical Company (now Conoil) for many years. He was the gubernatorial candidate of All Nigerian Peoples Party (ANPP) in Oyo state in the April poll. In this encounter with SINA FADARE he spoke on Oyo politics, his petition at the tribunals, the Nigerian state and the way forward. Excerpts:
You had a successful career in the oil industry, what else are you looking for, in life? And why politics?

Original_Search_Title: Will Mwakwere Sell Shirikisho?
Headline: Kenya: Will Mwakwere Sell Shirikisho?
Body_Text:
Nairobi — "Dzipapa! Jipapa! (big shark, big shark)!" shouts a Shirikisho Party of Kenya supporter waving a clenched fist, to work up the crowd.
"Lamkeni kudzecha (wake up, it is dawn)!" the crowd responds, with clenched fists raised in the air.

Original_Search_Title: To Shop Or Pray On Sunday
Headline: Kenya: To Shop Or Pray On Sunday
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It is on a Sunday. Ululations and hymnal chants rent the air as Christian faithful throng Suneka streets in South Kisii District. No, they are not heading to church.
They want the town council to change the market day to enable them worship peacefully and without competition from merchants.

Original_Search_Title: Is Hon. Kakooza Really for Museveni's 4th Term?
Headline: Uganda: Is Hon. Kakooza Really for Museveni's 4th Term?
Body_Text:
In a banana republic of 30 million people and over 330 members of Parliament, some of the legislators are bound to make intellectuals a little impatient. But sometimes a poorly regarded legislator turns out to be much smarter than had been assumed. The case of Hon. James Kakooza, who represents Kabula County, is intriguing.
About a month ago, Daily Monitor ran a front-page story that Kakooza was mobilising other MPs and local government officials in a crusade to keep Gen. Yoweri Museveni in power beyond 2011.

Original_Search_Title: United Nations Concerned By Security Situation in East
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: United Nations Concerned By Security Situation in East
Publisher: United Nations Mission in the Democratic Republic of Congo (Kinshasa)
Body_Text:
At its weekly conference this Wednesday July 25, 2007, MONUC stressed the United Nations concern over the security situation, conflict and humanitarian crisis in the east of the country, particularly in the Kivu provinces where 700,000 people have been internally displaced.
In a statement made in New York on Friday 20 July 2007, UN Secretary General Mr. Ban Ki-moon said he was deeply concerned by the deterioration in the security situation in the provinces of North and South Kivu in eastern DRC.

Original_Search_Title: Atta-Mills Knows What He is Talking About
Headline: Ghana: Atta-Mills Knows What He is Talking About
Body_Text:
Accra — Make no mistake, nobody is pleased with the high spate of gangland-style murders taking place in Ghana. Still, the classification or designation by which prominent non-law-enforcement Ghanaians, particularly politicians, label such heinous acts may unnecessarily prejudice and, indeed, actually hinder the crucial and legitimate detective work of law-enforcement agents and agencies.
And the latter observation appears to have been what Dr. Kofi Kesse, the Deputy Inspector-General of the Ghana Police Force, had in mind when he recently warned against such patently irresponsible characterization by those whom the general public expects to know better (Ghanaweb.com 7/19/07).

Original_Search_Title: Police Hold Mityana Accident Lorry Driver
Headline: Uganda: Police Hold Mityana Accident Lorry Driver
Body_Text:
Mityana — POLICE have arrested the driver of the truck that was involved in an accident and claimed 16 lives in Mityana town over the weekend. Simon Nadiope, who was driving the Mitsubishi Fuso registered UAF 102W, was arrested on Monday morning from Kisenyi, a Kampala suburb.
He was briefly detained at St Balikuddembe (Owino) Market Police Post and later transferred to Mityana Police Station where he allegedly committed the crime.

Original_Search_Title: Churches Reject New Trade Pacts With Europe
Headline: Africa: Churches Reject New Trade Pacts With Europe
Body_Text:
Nairobi — New trade agreements being negotiated between Europe and developing countries are harmful to Africa, All Africa Conference of Churches said.
In a letter urging African trade ministers not to sign the Economic Partnership Agreements by Dec. 31, AACC General Secretary, Rev. Dr. Mvume Dandala, said developing countries risked losing much-needed revenues if the new trade agreements came into force in January as planned.

Original_Search_Title: Lovely Winds, Bends to Bujumbura
Headline: East Africa: Lovely Winds, Bends to Bujumbura
Body_Text:
My trip traversing two countries in East Africa helped to bust a few myths. That the majority of Rwandans are light skinned and tall, that Rwanda and Burundi are largely flat with a few hilly areas, that the hills in Rwanda and Burundi are lush and green, that most of the trunk road network is poor, that due to the record population density, the people of Rwanda and Burundi are so abundant and visible and that there are more than a dozen gun totting personnel every couple of hundred meters.
It has been said that the difference in countries can be seen from the entry point vis the border crossing. I have always taken this statement with a pinch of salt, but my crossing at the Katuna border post into Rwanda changed all that. Not only was the area cleaner, the lines were more orderly and there was less noise! I had been rather frantic, with people pushing and shoving to get their papers cleared on the Uganda side. This was a welcome relief. The officials were polite, if a bit aloof, and I remember only seeing one armed soldier in the entire road trip across Rwanda. Not so in Burundi, however, where there are still numerous military roadblocks and several armed foot patrolmen every couple of kilometers.

Original_Search_Title: U.S. Ambassador's Fund Avails U.S.$76,000 for Small Enterprises
Headline: Rwanda: U.S. Ambassador's Fund Avails U.S.$76,000 for Small Enterprises
Body_Text:
Kigali — The US Ambassador's self help fund dished out small grants worth US$76,000 to different small business associations in Rwanda in 2006.
According to the public affairs officer, public diplomacy at the US Embassy in Kigali Mr. Brian George, the small associations are given one time grants and not revolving funds.

Original_Search_Title: U.S. Congratulates Elders On Pardon of CUD Leaders
Headline: Ethiopia: U.S. Congratulates Elders On Pardon of CUD Leaders
Body_Text:
Addis Ababa — The United States said it appreciates the efforts of Ethiopian elders and civil society leaders that resulted in Friday's release of CUD party leaders and said it hoped the elders would remain engaged in the promotion of national reconciliation and political reform.
The Ethiopian government on Friday released 38 opposition leaders and journalists detained in the wake of political violence in 2005.

Original_Search_Title: Disabled People Join 'Boda Boda' Business
Headline: Kenya: Disabled People Join 'Boda Boda' Business
Body_Text:
Nairobi — They have defied physical challenges to flourish in the boda boda (bicycle taxi) business in Busia town.
The 60 men and women have applied a stroke of genius to fix carriers on their wheel chairs and today, they are trusted transporters in the border town.

Original_Search_Title: Banyana Banyana Banana Peel for Falcons
Headline: Nigeria: Banyana Banyana Banana Peel for Falcons
Body_Text:
Lagos — Nigeria's Super Falcons and South Africa's Banyana Banyana are no strangers to each other, they have met several times on different turfs, but the Falcons had the most of their opponents.
As the 9th All Africa Games winds up today, it remains to be seen, who will win the gold medal.

Original_Search_Title: Violence Kills Three Soldiers, Four Civilians in Somali Capital.
Headline: Somalia: Violence Kills Three Soldiers, Four Civilians in Somali Capital.
Body_Text:
Mogadishu — Four civilians and three government soldiers have been killed in huge explosion followed shootouts which this afternoon happened Black-Sea junction in Hodan district, south of the capital Mogadishu.
The explosion that caused by a hand grenade bomb aimed at the government soldiers passing there, witnesses told Shabelle Radio.

Original_Search_Title: 20 Killed in Two Accidents
Headline: Uganda: 20 Killed in Two Accidents
Body_Text:
Mityana — SIXTEEN traders dealing in kasese, a local gin, died on the spot when their lorry overturned near Mityana town on Saturday night.
Eleven others were rushed to Mityana hospital in critical condition.

Original_Search_Title: Life of Chairman of Kitoro
Headline: Uganda: Life of Chairman of Kitoro
Body_Text:
Kampala — RESIDENTS threatened to expel him from the village if he didn't stand for LC1 elections again. Gladys Kalibbala sat down with the 93-year-old Livingstone Sebuliba
The day the 93-year-old Livingstone Sebuliba refused to stand re-election as LC1 chairman for Kitoro in Entebbe, about three quarters of the electorate in the area boycotted the poll.

Original_Search_Title: Jailed Opposition Leaders Freed Opposition Granted Total Pardon - PM Meles
Headline: Ethiopia: Jailed Opposition Leaders Freed Opposition Granted Total Pardon - PM Meles
Body_Text:
Addis Ababa — Leaders of the Coalition for Unity and Democracy sentenced to life in prison this week were released on Friday.
They were driven out of prison in minibuses to the cheers of dozens of relatives and supporters waiting outside.

Original_Search_Title: Win - Win for Coalition of Elders
Headline: Ethiopia: Win - Win for Coalition of Elders
Body_Text:
The release of more than 35 leadership and members of the main opposition party Coalition for Unity and Democracy (CUD) yesterday was a triumph for the Coalition of Ethiopian Elders who have been working for the last eighteen months to secure a pardon by the government.
Professor Ephraim Issac, chairman of the Elders, told journalists and diplomatic representatives that they are all very happy to know by now that numerous people have left the prison and they are on their way home.

Original_Search_Title: Fake Statutory Instrument
Headline: Zimbabwe: Fake Statutory Instrument
Body_Text:
Harare — Some unknown people in Harare have crafted a statutory instrument -- which is being widely circulated on e-mails -- claiming that Government intends to ban imports and exports of certain basic foodstuffs when the State has not gazetted such a regulation.
Distributors of the supposed Statutory Instrument 137 of 2007 Control of Goods (Import and Export) Regulations 2007 claim it will come into force on August 1 to deal with black market traders. But Government says it has not gazetted such a statutory instrument although it is considering introducing controls on the import and export of certain goods.

Original_Search_Title: Top U.S. Lawmaker Welcomes Prisoner Release
Headline: Ethiopia: Chairman Donald M. Payne Welcomes Release of Prisoners
Body_Text:
Washington, DC — Representative Donald M. Payne, Chairman of the House Foreign Affairs' Subcommittee on Africa, issued the following statement today:
"I very much welcome the release of these political prisoners and call for all political prisoners to be released without further delay.   I congratulate the many advocates of democracy, journalists, and human rights activists who have been imprisoned for standing up for what they believe.

Original_Search_Title: 38 CUD leaders, Journalists Released
Headline: Ethiopia: 38 CUD leaders, Journalists Released
Body_Text:
An almost two-year long political saga in Ethiopia seems to be wrapping up, as 38 senior leaders and members of Coalition for Democracy and Unity party (CUD), and journalists were released from prison yesterday after their request for pardon was approved by the president of the Federal Republic of Ethiopia (FDRE) Girma Woldegiorgis on Thursday.
"This sorry saga of the so-called Orange Revolution is now fully behind us. This is dead and buried now. This is a new chapter for everyone. I hope it also conveys that there is no sense of revenge and vendetta on the part of the government as long as people recognize that the rules of the game are to be respected by everyone, everyone is given a fair chance to participate," Prime Minister Meles Zenawi, who called a press conference at his office on Friday to announce the news, said.

Original_Search_Title: 38 Political Prisoners Released
Headline: Ethiopia: 38 Political Prisoners Released
Body_Text:
The Ethiopian government released 38 members of the opposition from jail on Friday.
They were granted full pardons just four days after a court sentenced 30 of them - members of the opposition Coalition for Unity (CUD) - to life imprisonment after convicting them of trying to overthrow the government.
Reuters news agency reported that at a news conference announcing the releases, Ethiopian Prime Minister Meles Zenawi said that the decision "conveys that there is no sense of revenge by the government."
The agency said the prisoners were driven from prison in Addis Ababa in mini-buses, to the cheers of relatives and supporters. The capital is a stronghold of the CUD, and the city's mayor-elect elect and CUD member Berhanu Nega was among the prisoners.
Keep up with the latest headlines on WhatsApp | LinkedIn
The released opposition members were also granted the right to contest elections and the right to vote, rights that had been taken away when they were sentenced on Monday.
The Ethiopian government has come under international pressure to release the prisoners, who were arrested and charged after unrest following elections in 2005 which the international community widely condemned.
On Wednesday, the United States House of Representatives Subcommittee on Africa and Global Health approved a bill calling for human rights reform in Ethiopia. The bill specifically called on President Bush to withhold assistance to Ethiopia until the prisoners were freed.
"No one has been held liable for those crimes and political prisoners have been languishing in jail for two years," committee chairperson Donald M. Payne said on Wednesday. "If the government is serious about democracy, rule of law and accountability, it would hold accountable the security forces responsible for killing citizens and it would free the political prisoners who are only guilty of running from office and winning."
Meles strongly denied that pressure from Washington, and in particular Congress, had caused him to release and pardon the prisoners. "The Ethiopian government isn't willing and is unable to be run like a banana republic from Capitol Hill. Some individuals appear to be entertaining such illusions," he said.
Meles also made public a letter signed by the prisoners admitting they were guilty. The BBC reported that opposition leader Hailu Shawel said after his release that he had signed the document "under duress".
Four journalists were among the prisoners released. Reporters Without Borders voiced relief at their release, while also calling for two journalists - Zelalem Guebre and Abey Gizaw – who were tried and convicted to a life sentence in absentia, to be cleared of the charges against them.
Two Action Aid officials were not released today. According to the organization, they will continue their legal battle.

Original_Search_Title: Africa Insight - Can Nigeria Cure its Oil 'Disease?'
Headline: Africa Insight - Can Nigeria Cure its Oil 'Disease?'
Body_Text:
Nairobi — After underdeveloping the Niger Delta, the Nigerian government is building roads and bridges in the region - which is too late - as the people have learnt that kidnapping foreigners produces rapid results.
And, to end the problem once and for all, some Nigerians say corrupt Delta business people, council chairmen, MPs and contractors should be kidnapped as well.

Original_Search_Title: Biotech is the Future - We Dare Not Be Left Behind
Headline: Kenya: Biotech is the Future - We Dare Not Be Left Behind
Body_Text:
Nairobi — THE UNITED NATIONS University and the UN Education, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (Unesco) last week appointed Nairobi a regional centre of expertise on education and sustainable development.
Nairobi becomes the first city in Africa to get such an award, joining 35 other global cities.

Original_Search_Title: Spoilt for Choice At Deli-Cious Emporium
Headline: Zimbabwe: Spoilt for Choice At Deli-Cious Emporium
Body_Text:
Harare — Spurred into action by images in the Sunday papers of housewives cramming their shopping trolleys with foodstuffs, I was up betimes on Monday morning to visit a supermarket and buy at least a packet of Chimombe and a loaf of bread.
Although my personal SatNav was directed towards a supermarket in Sam Levy's Village, a tinkling fountain in the far reaches of the shopping mall was distracting and George and I altered course to arrive at Deli-cious Fine Food Emporium just in time for breakfast.

Original_Search_Title: The Ban on Buveera Not Necessary
Headline: Uganda: The Ban on Buveera Not Necessary
Body_Text:
Kampala — I think the issue of buveera (polythene bags) has been rushed. These polythene bags are only a small portion of the non-degradable plastic materials we use in form of plates, cups, shoes and chairs.
I am told plastic gadgets can even be used in correcting life-threatening heart disorders. Buveera are convenient particularly in packing foodstuffs.

Original_Search_Title: Consumer Protection and the New Ghana Cedi
Headline: Ghana: Consumer Protection and the New Ghana Cedi
Body_Text:
Accra — Like the demonstration of the use of condoms at the market using Casfordian students of the University of Cape Coast with bananas, the practicals of the New Ghana Cedi changeover are definitely going to be wrought with problems. Either the hungry students eat the bananas and use their penises in the process or there will be no condom demonstration at all. Should the real life situational problems have been foreseen or otherwise been more explicit in the adverts like the 'USE A CONDOM' billboard which incorporates actual sexual Kama-Sutra positions as alphabetical characters for the sake of clarity?
The change is on and little has been considered in terms of how much real life impact the change over is going to have on confused Ghanaian consumers including myself.

Original_Search_Title: Use of Government Property Common
Headline: Kenya: Use of Government Property Common
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The 2006 by-elections after the Marsabit plane crash evoked complaints of voter inducement and misuse of State resources in the campaigns.
Government was accused of using State resources such as Government vehicles and military helicopters in the campaigns.

Original_Search_Title: Culture And Sports Have Huge Potential
Headline: Kenya: Culture And Sports Have Huge Potential
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In the fog and cold of last Sunday, Addis Ababa's Bole International Airport was jampacked with Ethiopian athletes travelling to Algiers, Algeria, for the All Africa Games and their relatives and friends.
Bedecked in the national colours of red, yellow and green, the athletes, some in their early teens, exuded the confidence and self-importance that may be gained from only a feeling of real security.

Original_Search_Title: Cricket Champs Africa Opt for Caution
Headline: Africa: Cricket Champs Africa Opt for Caution
Body_Text:
Kampala — CAUTION will be the watch-word as champions Africa Cricket Club get their Castle Cricket League title defence underway with a potential banana-skin fixture against Nile at Lugogo today.
Both sides reside in the ultra-competitive Premier League - one of two divisions in the newly-formatted cricket league. Last weekend's gripping encounter between Tornado and Tornado 'B' showed that clubs in the Premier division can ill-afford complacency.

Original_Search_Title: 'Midwives of the Dead'
Headline: Nigeria: 'Midwives of the Dead'
Body_Text:
Just like all living organisms, there is a time for man to be born and a time for him to die. However the way and manner and the place where we celebrate these rites of passage differ from place to place, from class to class and from one cultural location to the other. This explains the fact that some people are born in expensive well equipped hospitals while some others are born under banana leaves several miles away from any known hospitals.
However when it comes to death, the circumstances, social and cultural milieu and class notwithstanding, mortal man passes through same route, the morgue which we could call the mortuary interchangeably, where every one is leveled up. Dead. Dumb.

Original_Search_Title: A Critical Look At the Film, Namibia, the Struggle for Liberation
Headline: Namibia: A Critical Look At the Film, Namibia, the Struggle for Liberation
Body_Text:
Los Angeles — A Namibian Film Commission/Pan Afrikan Center of Namibia presentation.
Produced by Abius Akwaake, Steve Gukas.

Original_Search_Title: Love in Agony - Movie Exposes France And Restores Hope
Headline: Rwanda: Love in Agony - Movie Exposes France And Restores Hope
Body_Text:
Kigali — France is Jean Claude Habiyakare's villain in his new film, LOVE IN AGONY. The film, which will be launched officially in September, gives insight into how France acted in Rwanda's 1994 Genocide in which one million Tutsis and moderate Hutus perished.The film depicts the cruelty inflicted upon the Tutsis who are perceived to be in the league with the advancing members of Rwanda Patriotic Front (RPF).
Through the use of strong characters, France's part is clearly portrayed. The French at first have been able to deceive many people, including the international community that they have come to rescue innocent civilians.

Original_Search_Title: No Prayers, Eulogies for Mungiki Suspects
Headline: Kenya: No Prayers, Eulogies for Mungiki Suspects
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Hurried funerals without ceremony under instructions from the administration are the new kind of burials in Nyakahura village, Murang'a North District.
Since seven suspected members of the proscribed mungiki sect were shot dead in an oathing ceremony recently, villagers have followed that script during their funerals.

Original_Search_Title: Students Riot Over Sharia
Headline: Uganda: Students Riot Over Sharia
Body_Text:
Jinja — Twelve students of the Islamic Institute in Bugembe, Jinja have been released on police bond pending further investigation into several charges filed against them.
The students were arrested on June 29 after they engaged riot police officers in running battles following a strike they staged to protest the introduction of tough Sharia laws in the school.

Original_Search_Title: U.S. is About to Pull Out of Country Again- a Mistake
Headline: Somalia: U.S. is About to Pull Out of Country Again- a Mistake
Body_Text:
Nairobi — THE UNITED STATES INTENDS to give up on Somalia - yet again. In an interview with BBC last week, US assistant secretary of state for African Affairs, Ms Jendayi Fraser, just stopped short of admitting that the US now feels helpless over the goings-on in the troubled Horn of Africa country.
In her own words, it is hard to tell whether the situation is now better or worse than it was six months ago when Ethiopian troops - with the full backing from Washington - stormed Mogadishu to end the eight-month control of Somalia by the Union of Islamic Courts (ICU).

Original_Search_Title: Ugandan Coffee On Sale in Sweden
Headline: Ugandan Coffee On Sale in Sweden
Body_Text:
Kampala — ONE Café, a Swedish-Danish company, has began marketing Ugandan coffee on the Internet.
The coffee, which is packed in a 10-gramme plastic bag like a tea bag, is being sold on www.kaffe-agenterne.dk in three coffee shops in the Danish capital, Copenhagen. The bag costs $1.5 (about sh2, 445).

Original_Search_Title: Narc Prepares for Major Comeback
Headline: Kenya: Narc Prepares for Major Comeback
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A major political development is expected on Monday with the revival of the original National Alliance Rainbow Coalition (Narc).
Ford-Kenya chairman Mr Musikari Kombo, said his party, and the National Party of Kenya, Democratic Party of Kenya, Sisi Kwa Sisi and Shirikisho Party of Kenya, will spearhead the revival, when the party's co-ordinating committee meets in Nairobi.

Original_Search_Title: ODM-K - Unite Or Forget Presidency
Headline: Kenya: ODM-K - Unite Or Forget Presidency
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Short of unity in diversity, the ODM-Kenya leaders are likely to disappoint Kenyans as petty personality and tribal wrangles ensue.
Instead of plotting out campaign strategies, presidential aspirants and their tribal handlers are fighting over non-issues and method of choosing a flag bearer.

Original_Search_Title: Miles in Motorola Endorsment Deal
Headline: Uganda: Miles in Motorola Endorsment Deal
Body_Text:
While big brands look for international celebrities like David Beckham to endorse their brands, Ugandan celebrities have been more or less pleading with big companies to sponsor their events like album launches. But now the tide is changing and the big companies are looking at celebrities to endorse their products.
Mobile phone makers Motorola have identified three East African artistes to endorse their W series handsets in the region. Dancehall singer Peter Miles was selected to endorse phones in Uganda while Jua Kali was selcetd from Kenya and Banana Zoro from Tanzania.

Original_Search_Title: Good Customer Care Will Move Rwanda Higher
Headline: Rwanda: Good Customer Care Will Move Rwanda Higher
Body_Text:
Kigali — What do we mean by succeed? It means setting goals and attaining them. As far as that goes, Rwanda has a tall order to fulfill.
And as far as that goes-being a regional telecommunications centre; becoming an air-traffic hub and major film locale-Rwanda has been rightly touting all the progress it has made.But all of these tasks require that Rwandans are on top of their game.

Original_Search_Title: Social Distinctions Fade in the Face of Survival
Headline: Zimbabwe: Social Distinctions Fade in the Face of Survival
Body_Text:
Harare — A senior Zimbabwean police official has employed five young men to sell foreign currency on the streets of the capital, Harare, but while the police play cat and mouse with the illegal dealers, his "employees" conduct their business undisturbed because they cannot be arrested.
"We sell the foreign currency openly because we are untouchable. Some constables arrested me at one time, even though I had informed them that I was working for their boss. They have been transferred from Harare and after that no-one dares touch us," said one of the dealers, who identified himself as Peter.

Original_Search_Title: Bidandi Criticises Govt On New Taxes
Headline: Uganda: Bidandi Criticises Govt On New Taxes
Body_Text:
Kampala — FORMER Local Government minister Jaberi Bidandi Ssali has said the government is impoverishing Ugandans by imposing new taxes like the Local Service Tax.
"We have lessons in this new unfortunate government tax regime. The problems have been largely brought by bisanja (dry banana leaves) politics. Those leaders whom we tie bisanja on have made us suffer because they don't pay taxes," he said.

Original_Search_Title: Cleaning Up Entebbe - Kampala Road, Sort of
Headline: Uganda: Cleaning Up Entebbe - Kampala Road, Sort of
Body_Text:
Kampala — In 4 months time, Uganda will be hosting the long awaited Chogm though a section of the public is doubting our readiness. Glenna Gordon assesses the beautification project along Entebbe Kampala road.
THE campaign to beautify Kampala and the surrounding environs aims to "create the image of a major international city." The works have begun, and all along the road between Kampala and Entebbe, visitors' entry way to the city, progress is evident.

Original_Search_Title: Oaths That Water Growth of Sect
Headline: Kenya: Oaths That Water Growth of Sect
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Proscribed group continues to recruit and bind members even as State moves to stem violence.
As the Government steps up its crackdown on Mungiki sect members, the group continues to recruit and bind new members.

Original_Search_Title: KCC Waste Disposal Plan Has Loopholes
Headline: Uganda: KCC Waste Disposal Plan Has Loopholes
Body_Text:
Kampala — KAMPALA City Council (KCC) intends to implement the Waste Management Ordinance law in order to keep the city clean ahead of the Chogm summit in November. The ordinance which requires everyone to properly store solid waste until it is collected by a licensed disposal firm, came into effect in 2000 but has hitherto not been implemented.
Under the new measures, all households shall be checked to ensure that they have toilets, proper garbage and waste water disposal areas. In order to do this, KCC has divided the five divisions of Kampala City into zones and a private firm shall move from house to house collecting the garbage. In principle this is a welcome move because as the capital city of Uganda, the sanitation level of Kampala is appalling.

Original_Search_Title: Ban On Plastic Bags is Good, But What's the Alternative?
Headline: Kenya: Ban On Plastic Bags is Good, But What's the Alternative?
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In the Budget, Finance minister Mr Amos Kimunya banned polythene bags but forgot to give consumers an alternative.
He imposed a hefty 120 per cent excise duty on plastic polythene bags and banned the flimsy ones. The drastic move seems to have won accolades from many quarters, as plastic bag pollution has been a contentious issue for many years. The bags, many of which are so thin that they are simply thrown away after one trip from the shops, are a familiar eyesore in urban and rural areas.

Original_Search_Title: Seven Mungiki Members Shot Dead By Police
Headline: Kenya: Seven Mungiki Members Shot Dead By Police
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Murang'a was turned into a battleground Sunday when police shot dead seven suspected Mungiki members they allegedly found taking an illegal oath.
About 40 others fled into the bush with gunshot wounds during the raid at Gikui Village in Iyego location, Kangema constituency.

Original_Search_Title: He Had Planned to Return Home And Wed
Headline: Uganda: He Had Planned to Return Home And Wed
Body_Text:
Kampala — THEY saw him off with ululations and merry-making. He was setting off for a mission that would change his family fortune. But Osbert Tugume, 24, would not get the same treatment on his return.
He left with a dream to wed on his return back home. But the dream was never to pass. He returned in a coffin, living his family with a very unpredictable future. Raymond Baguma narrates Tugume's story.

Original_Search_Title: When Education is Not So Sweet
Headline: Malawi: When Education is Not So Sweet
Body_Text:
Brussels — A major Belgian retail firm has been accused of misleading its customers by implying that its sales of Malawian sugar will benefit education projects in that country.
Colruyt, a supermarket chain operating in Belgium and France, sells sugar and several other food and drink items from poor countries as part of a range of products called 'Collibri for Education'. The company states that 5 percent of the price of Collibri food and 3 percent of non-food products (not including value added tax) go to schooling projects run by non-governmental organisations.

Original_Search_Title: Obligatto's Romantic Aura
Headline: Uganda: Obligatto's Romantic Aura
Body_Text:
Kampala — LIFE begins at 40 so they say. True to this saying, people in this age bracket swing, dine and wine like there is no tomorrow.
Sneak into Club Obligatto on Friday or Saturday and you will see them guzzling beer, feasting on whole fish and chips before jumping on the floor to shake their bodies vigorously.

Original_Search_Title: Banana's Could Be New Source of Flour
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Gives Makerere University Scientists Shs8 Billion
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE government is giving a team of Makerere University scientists Shs8billion to set up a factory in Mbarara that will produce flour from bananas, President Yoweri Museveni has said.
Speaking in Masaka on Tuesday on his tour of the district, Mr Museveni said he has invited Sameer Group, an Asian company that bought Dairy Corporation to build a fruit-processing factory in Masaka to tap into the abundant pineapples.

Original_Search_Title: Mandazi Syndrome Attacks Legislators
Headline: Uganda: Mandazi Syndrome Attacks Legislators
Body_Text:
Kampala — A VERY dangerous vice has attacked our "esteemed" Parliament. Its signs and symptoms are only manifest in the mornings and shortly before lunch time. It attacks its prey when committee meetings are in session or whenever a committee meeting is meant to sit. Its victims are Members of Parliament and some "fortunate" reporters.
But the effects are more devastating on the MPs than they are on the reporters, who, moments after covering proceedings in the House, usually burn calories by walking back to their newsrooms. They are "privileged" not to own cars unlike the usually burly MPs who are the prime targets.

Original_Search_Title: What is Behind the New War On Corruption?
Headline: Uganda: What is Behind the New War On Corruption?
Body_Text:
Kampala — In less than a year since President Museveni announced zero tolerance for corruption in his third term, results are beginning to show. The last two months have shown a different Museveni from the one who in the earlier years of his rule consoled himself that unlike the corrupt of former regimes who stashed their loot away in foreign bank accounts, the new corrupt were investing theirs locally in buildings in places like Muyenga. Political pundits believe never before has the President demonstrated such a level of determination in the fight against corruption as he has done now.
Between May and June, Mr Museveni has ordered the jailing of three former ministers, two of whom are senior members in his ruling National Resistance Movement party. Mike Mukula is the NRM Vice Chairperson for eastern region while Brig. Jim Muhwezi is not only the leader and representative of the veterans league in the NRM's central executive committee, a top party organ, but also one of the few bush war historicals. Dr Alex Kamugisha is equally a key figure in the local politics of Rukungiri District.

Original_Search_Title: 2007 Could Be the Best Year We Have Ever Seen!
Headline: Uganda: 2007 Could Be the Best Year We Have Ever Seen!
Body_Text:
Kampala — You can tell a good year from a mile away, and from what many have seen so far, 2007 is as good a year as any they ever saw.
In fact, from the look of things this may be even better than many a year we have ever seen. It is one of those that take off on a tricky note, with some unpleasant foreboding, then gradually begin to get better and better.

Original_Search_Title: Mwakwere's Dalliance With Narc-Kenya is Finally Over
Headline: Kenya: Mwakwere's Dalliance With Narc-Kenya is Finally Over
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The end of the game is on for the Government of National Unity (GNU). It is now obvious the union is bursting at the seams.
Transport minister and Matuga MP, Mr Ali Chirau Mwakwere, could go down in history as the first Cabinet member to quit Narc-Kenya.

Original_Search_Title: Waiting for Oluchi's Kind of Music
Headline: Nigeria: Waiting for Oluchi's Kind of Music
Body_Text:
Lagos — These pictures have let the cat out of the bag. Nigeria's supermodel, Oluchi Onweagba is not only modelling these musical instruments, there are strong indications that she has brought out her song book and that one of the best producers in the industry has been contracted to work with her.
It won't be long now before the world is treated to Oluchi's kind of music.

Original_Search_Title: Police Kill Eight Mungiki Suspects
Headline: Kenya: Police Kill Eight Mungiki Suspects
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Police on Friday night killed eight suspected members of the outlawed Mungiki sect in Nairobi and Murang'a and recovered pangas and a pistol as the crackdown on the gang continued.
For the second consecutive day, police recovered a severed head and genitals dumped on a city street only a day after another head was found along Kirinyaga Road near the Globe Cinema roundabout.

Original_Search_Title: Govt to Create New Constituencies
Headline: Kenya: AG Orders Plan for New Constituencies
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The clamour for the creation of new constituencies is expected to reach fever pitch this week following news that the Attorney-General had directed the drafting of a Constitutional Bill to that effect.
Attorney-General Amos Wako told the Sunday Nation he had instructed drafters in his office to work on the Bill for the new constituencies that number anywhere from 21 to 60.

Original_Search_Title: Cold Weather Hardships
Headline: Uganda: Cold Weather Hardships
Body_Text:
I'm one of those people that prefer the rainy season to the dry and hot weather, the cold, mud and inconvenience for example terrible traffic jams not withstanding; I am yet to fully understand though why Kampala streets jam up so badly when it rains. Given the constituency we stay in, the norm is extremely dusty and bumpy roads, not to mention the excessive heat.
As is the case usually, the cold weather comes with its challenges and for any mother of young children need I say more?

Original_Search_Title: Mungiki Strike Leaves 12 Dead
Headline: Kenya: Mungiki Strike Leaves 12 Dead
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Four people were beheaded and 17 others shot dead in separate incidents on Thursday night and yesterday morning in a wave of terror perpetrated by heavily armed gangs and suspected Mungiki followers in Nairobi and Kiambu.
Three of the beheadings happened at Banana Hill in Kiambu District, a village which has borne the brunt of killings by suspected sect members in the recent past.

Original_Search_Title: Leave Encroachment Fight to Politicians - Museveni
Headline: Uganda: Leave Encroachment Fight to Politicians - Museveni
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has said curbing encroachment on forest reserves and national parks should be done by politicians and not the National Forestry Authority (NFA).
"Investors want encroachment of forest reserves to stop but I assured them that it will be done by us the politicians who know how to handle the people affected and not the National Forestry Authority, who can't handle people, or the game rangers who confuse people with warthogs."

Original_Search_Title: Dongfang - Seaweed Never Tasted So Good
Headline: Zimbabwe: Dongfang - Seaweed Never Tasted So Good
Body_Text:
Harare — It's taken me a couple of years to visit the Dongfang Chinese restaurant in Chisipite.
This may be something to do with the fact that the name Dongfang conjures up images in the Anglo Saxon compartment of my brain of loud noises and sharp teeth. In point of fact, this name has many impressive associations, and literally means "the dawn is breaking in the east". Hence we have the Dongfang space satellite programme, Han Dongfang, a renowned Chinese labour advocate, and the four star Dongfang hotel in Guangzhou.

Original_Search_Title: 25 to Wrestle With Former Energy Minister for Imenti Seat
Headline: Kenya: 25 to Wrestle With Former Energy Minister for Imenti Seat
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Constituency Profiles: Imenti Central
More than 25 people have declared interest in the Central Imenti parliamentary seat held by former Energy minister Kirugi M'Mukindia.

Original_Search_Title: Father Brought to Book for Keeping Son Out of School
Headline: Kenya: Father Brought to Book for Keeping Son Out of School
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Saidi Ngira, 49, was shocked when policemen arrested him for failing to take his son to school.
The officers had earlier arrested 12-year-old Fikiri Saidi, whom they found working in a quarry while in school uniform. Also arrested were Fikiri's two friends who were working with him.

Original_Search_Title: How Rwandans Are Minimising Business Costs
Headline: Rwanda: How Rwandans Are Minimising Business Costs
Body_Text:
Kigali — It is hard to find businesses operated in homes. But these days, because of urbanisation, there are a number of Rwandans who prefer to operate from home or on their verandas instead of renting office space.
The thought of minimizing costs and not being troubled for both house and office rent is becoming the norm today. Rwanda encourages job makers rather than job seekers. It is better to be self-employed sometimes.

Original_Search_Title: Our Budgets Are a Puzzle
Headline: Uganda: Our Budgets Are a Puzzle
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE budget is always intended to show the performance of the Government in the concluding financial year (FY) and what the Government intends to do in the coming FY.
When analysing the Government performance, we should ask ourselves whether the Government has fulfiled the promises it made in the previous budget.

Original_Search_Title: Matooke Can Be Used to Make Chapati - Study
Headline: Uganda: Matooke Can Be Used to Make Chapati - Study
Body_Text:
Kampala — Uganda's roving "banana" ambassador Florence Isabirye Muranga of Makerere University Department of Food Science and Technology and her team have some good news.
The team says their breakthrough research on turning banana, a staple diet in the central region, to flour makes it more desirable than wheat for confectionaries. The news is already exciting palates in Europe, they say.

Original_Search_Title: Monthly Human Rights Assessment - May 2007
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Monthly Human Rights Assessment - May 2007
Publisher: United Nations Mission in the Democratic Republic of Congo (Kinshasa)
Body_Text:
The United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights, Louise Arbour, paid a six-day working visit to the DRC; FDLR/RASTA rebels killed 17 villagers in South Kivu Province; Five civilians were arbitrarily executed by the 2nd Battalion of the Bravo Brigade in Rudehe-Katwiguru, North Kivu; FARDC and PNC elements were implicated in several violations of the rights to life, physical integrity, particularly rape, as well as the right to liberty and security of person ; The UN Special Rapporteur on the Independence of Judges and Lawyers, Leandro Despouy, presented a preliminary report on his visit to the DRC to the Human Rights Council; 252 inmates of the Kinshasa Central Prison (CPRK) were granted conditional release; The Kilwa massacre trial resumed before the Military Court in Lubumbashi, Katanga Province, following a suspension of several months; The Boma Military Tribunal (Bas-Congo Province) delivered its verdict in the case of seven members of the politico-religious group Bundu Dia Kongo (BDK).
Main Developments

Original_Search_Title: Opposition Strategy to Beat Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: ODM's Strategy to Beat Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Secret report prepared by the ODM-Kenya Council of Elders details an elaborate plan the party candidate will use to fight it out with President Kibaki.
The report, dubbed "Victory Mission 2007", predicts how votes would be cast in each region and tallies an estimate that shows Kibaki and the ODM-Kenya candidate going to the wire if the latter remain a united force. The report, prepared by the Chris Okemo-led sub-committee on power sharing, was tabled before the Council of Elders in May.

Original_Search_Title: Linking Compatriots
Headline: Cameroon: Linking Compatriots
Body_Text:
News of the sudden collapse of the Mungo bridge on July 1, 2004, was received throughout Cameroon with shock and consternation.
The famous "Reunification Bridge" linking the former French-speaking East Cameroon and English-speaking West Cameroon had collapsed! And even though the incident was traceable to a fire accident caused by a heavily-loaded petrol tanker that collided on the bridge, many were those who quickly came up with varied conspiracy theories. While some attributed the incident to an act of sabotage by Anglophone separatist movements, others pointed accusing fingers at international terrorist organizations whose nefarious activities stretch across international borders. Yet such theories could not be completely ignored or considered as far-fetched considering the political and socio-economic importance of the Mungo bridge in the development of Cameroon. In a world torn apart by conflict and violence, the incident evoked an apocalyptic image in many minds.

Original_Search_Title: Banana is Britain's Most Popular Foodstuff
Headline: Ghana: Banana is Britain's Most Popular Foodstuff
Body_Text:
Accra — Ghana could be making millions of pounds by exporting bananas to the United Kingdom. Recent news publications point to Ghana making frantic efforts to increase banana exports.
As if on cue, the Daily Telegraph of Britain on Thursday published an article that said the world's favourite fruit is now Britain's most popular foodstuff, uniquely portable and with apparently legion health benefits.

Original_Search_Title: Police Force Acts to Achieve Global Ratio
Headline: Kenya: Police Force Acts to Achieve Global Ratio
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government will embark on a crash programme of recruiting and training police officers to ensure they reach the recommended police-civilian ratio.
Internal Security minister John Michuki said that currently, Kiganjo Police Training College can only accommodate 3,000 police trainees at ago.

Original_Search_Title: Minister Pushes for Plastic Bag Ban
Headline: Rwanda: Minister Pushes for Plastic Bag Ban
Body_Text:
Kigali — The wish by Rwanda's minister of state in charge of lands and environment Ms. Patricia Hajabakiga for a unilateral ban on the use of polythene bags by the East African Community (EAC) came to pass last week.
In the EAC budgets read last Thursday, a ban was slapped on the importation of plastic bags and containers.

Original_Search_Title: Elders - Kalonzo ODM-K's Best Bet Against Kibaki
Headline: Kenya: Elders - Kalonzo ODM-K's Best Bet Against Kibaki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Mwingi North MP Kalonzo Musyoka is the Orange Democratic Party's best chance of beating President Mwai Kibaki in elections later this year, according to a committee appointed to help ODM nominate its presidential candidate.
The 22-member Council of Elders, which on May 21 was given the job of helping the coalition settle on one person to face Mr Kibaki, conducted an analysis of national voting patterns that led them to name Mr Musyoka as the best candidate.

Original_Search_Title: The Sleepless Market
Headline: Uganda: The Sleepless Market
Body_Text:
Kampala — JUST like the heart that never rests, so is Nakasero Market. Business is always booming 24 hours a day
While the rest of Kampala dozes off, Nakasero Market, crouching between the City House, the tall Tourist Hotel and the plush City Complex, hums.

Original_Search_Title: Strangled to Death Not Far From Home
Headline: Uganda: Strangled to Death Not Far From Home
Body_Text:
Kampala — She was 80 and wanted to settle down and enjoy the twilight of her life, but one Sunday, she was killed. Was the property dispute she had been involved in the reason for her murder? Harriet Onyalla asks
TERESA Nalumansi knew someone wanted her dead.

Original_Search_Title: Algon Petitions Yar'Adua, Okiro Over Oyo Hoodlums
Headline: Nigeria: Algon Petitions Yar'Adua, Okiro Over Oyo Hoodlums
Body_Text:
Ibadan — THE Association of Local Governments of Nigeria (ALGON), Oyo State chapter has petitioned President Umaru Musa Yar'Adua and the Acting Inspector General of Police, Mr. Mike Okiro over the incessant attacks of innocent people in Oyo State by political thugs who have continuously laid siege on Ibadan.
Action Congress (AC), in the state also despatched a similar save our soul (SOS) to the President in which the party condemned in strong terms the unbridled violence and lawlessness being perpetrated by hoodlums which not only endanger lives of innocent people in the state, but also portray Nigeria as a "banana republic."

Original_Search_Title: Mt. Cameroon FC President Returns to Team
Headline: Cameroon: Mt. Cameroon FC President Returns to Team
Body_Text:
Pioneer President General of Mt. Cameroon Football Club, Calvin Foinding, who retracted from the team a few months ago, Wednesday, June 13, told The Post that he had returned to take over the management of the club.
Foinding was speaking during a friendly encounter between Mt. Cameroon FC and Southwest professional footballers based in Europe, Asia and other parts of Africa.Foinding, who was given a standing ovation as he sauntered into the stadium, said "I am very happy to have met so many people in the stadium and I promise the general public that I have returned to the team.

Original_Search_Title: Budget Speech Financial Year 2007/08
Headline: Uganda: Budget Speech Financial Year 2007/08
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE REPUBLIC OF UGANDA
BUDGET SPEECH

Original_Search_Title: Algon Petitions Yar'Adua, Okiro Over Oyo Hoodlums
Headline: Nigeria: Algon Petitions Yar'Adua, Okiro Over Oyo Hoodlums
Body_Text:
Ibadan — THE Association of Local Governments of Nigeria (ALGON), Oyo State chapter has petitioned President Umaru Musa Yar'Adua and the Acting Inspector General of Police, Mr. Mike Okiro over the incessant attacks of innocent people in Oyo State by political thugs who have continuously laid siege on Ibadan.
Action Congress (AC), in the state also despatched a similar save our soul (SOS) to the President in which the party condemned in strong terms the unbridled violence and lawlessness being perpetrated by hoodlums which not only endanger lives of innocent people in the state, but also portray Nigeria as a "banana republic."

Original_Search_Title: Police Gets Tougher On Drug Usage
Headline: Liberia: Police Gets Tougher On Drug Usage
Body_Text:
As a means of reducing the illegal usage of harmful and dangerous drugs in the society, the Liberia National Police (LNP) have collected and destroyed a huge sum of marijuana and cocaine as a result of its operation "Sweeping Wave."
The Police, being cognizant of the numerous illicit usages of those harmful drugs and through its intensive security searches, brought under its subjection some large quantity of illegal drugs.

Original_Search_Title: Witnesses Often See What They Want to See
Headline: Kenya: Witnesses Often See What They Want to See
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Someone died but no one knows for certain whom it was.
Some say the dead man was a hawker. Some say he was a shoe shiner. Some say he was a sweeper at the hotel at whose front 'something' in a bag exploded. Some say it was the man who had the explosive who died. Some say it was a woman 'suicide bomber' who died.

Original_Search_Title: Rights Group Urges Probe of Police Killings
Headline: Kenya: Amnesty Wants Killings Probed
Body_Text:
Nairobi — An international organisation wants the Government to conduct an inquiry into the killing of 30 people in separate police operations last week.
The victims were gunned down by police in Nairobi's Mathare slum as the law enforcers hunted Mungiki followers who had shot dead two policemen on Monday night.

Original_Search_Title: Mark - What Agenda for the Sixth Senate?
Headline: Nigeria: Mark - What Agenda for the Sixth Senate?
Body_Text:
Lagos — Senator David Mark has taken over the administration of the Senate as the sixth President of the upper legislative chamber in this Fourth Republic; and, as the Senate resumes from a two-week recess on June 26, Sufuyan Ojeifo takes a look at the shape of the new Senate
On May 31, 2007, former Senate President, Senator Kenechukwu Nnamani hit the last gavel to close the fifth session of the Fifth Senate. When the sixth session was inaugurated on June 5, 2007, Senator David Mark emerged as the sixth President of the Senate of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. After the ceremony that marked the Senate inauguration and election that took place on the floor of the house, he assumed his seat and gave an acceptance speech that went down well with his colleagues. Consider for instance his professed commitment to leading a responsible Legislature: "The Senate, and indeed, the National Assembly, is a very strategic and important arm of government. No country can effectively operate a robust democracy without a viable and virile legislature. We have responsibility to make laws for the peace, order and good governance of our people and also act as a check on the other arms of government. The concept of oversight functions which the National Assembly is constitutionally empowered to carry out on the other arms of government must be sustained. " Also consider his confession on how he intends to succeed in his present enterprise: 'Surely, I cannot succeed in my new assignment without your unalloyed support and cooperation. I therefore wish to seize this opportunity to extend my hand of fellowship to you all and seek your support and usual genuine advice from time to time irrespective of our political and ideological leanings. The Senate of the Federal republic of Nigeria is one and shall remain one united family."

Original_Search_Title: EU Experts to Meet Amid Hot Criticicism of Trade Agreements
Headline: Africa: EU-Africa Experts to Meet Amid Hot Critics On EPAs
Body_Text:
European Union (EU) and African experts are scheduled to meet in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia this week to discuss key issues on the EU-Africa cooperation. Agendas for the meeting might include EU-ACP cooperation-related issues, progress on the Economic Partnership Agreements (EPAs) negotiations and African Union matters.
