The African Union has become a major player on the continent in the areas of politics, economics and trade, and in regional crisis management, mainly in Sudan, the Democratic Republic of Congo, Burundi and Somalia. This has led numerous states to strengthen their presence in Addis Ababa. The new representation will be specifically for the African Union, and will be distinct from the Ethiopia delegation, although reports indicate that there will be many synergies.

Original_Search_Title: Eastern Artistes Fall Short at PAM Awards in Mbale
Headline: Uganda: Eastern Artistes Fall Short at PAM Awards in Mbale
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Eastern regional launch of the Pearl of Africa Music (PAM) Awards 2007 at Mbale Resort Hotel's annex on Saturday, was successful per se.
However, compared to the inaugural regional launch in Fort Portal, it could have used some more punch. Though there was palpable energy in their performances, the regional artistes lacked charisma; that magnetism required to dominate their Kampala peers, who, with the failure to turn up of such noteworthy artistes as Ragga Dee and Mega Dee, were not much of a competition.

Original_Search_Title: Dogged By the P.3 Jinx!
Headline: Rwanda: Dogged By the P.3 Jinx!
Body_Text:
Kigali — The good news coming out of the weekly cabinet meetings lately was of the appointment two weeks ago of a number of individuals to different posts in varied institutions. Of course, there was nothing unusual in that as such, only that among those appointed was one from our immediate 'stable'. The exciting part was that one from the 'backroom' could be spotted and be thrust onto the arena of 'public-glare' challenges. So, go ye, jolly old chap of the Manchester accent, and have a go at it! Evil wishers will always be there, ignore them: diversion being determinant or not, fold your sleeves and get to work.
It won't be easy, but who said life was? Just keep your wits about you, and your feet on the ground. Seek wise counsel, but don't answer to the so-common deliberately destructive whims. Especially, work with humble but determined diligence, have a stand but don't be obstinate, and shun indulgence in impressions, the trade of our society.

Original_Search_Title: A Night Away in Fort Portal
Headline: Uganda: A Night Away in Fort Portal
Body_Text:
Gerald Portal, the British colonial general after whom the town was named, is immortalised by way of a weather-beaten statue depicting a moustachiod man holding a pre-AK47 gun.
Being in Fort Portal made me want to do two childlike things. I wanted to see if lying atop the tea shrubs felt better than sleeping on a foam mattress. I also wanted to scale the mist-covered Rwenzori ranges if anything to see if I could touch the sky on the horizon.

Original_Search_Title: Re-Living Those First Days in Independent Zimbabwe . . .
Headline: Zimbabwe: Re-Living Those First Days in Independent Zimbabwe . . .
Body_Text:
There were, of course, moments of sadness and regret, remembering those who deserved to be there but were not.
When Josiah Tungamirai, the ZANLA High Command's political commissar saw me, he took me aside and walked me round the garden, mournfully relating details of Josiah Tongogara's death in Mozambique on the night of 26 December 1979.

Original_Search_Title: Tell the Truth
Headline: Uganda: Tell the Truth
Body_Text:
Kampala — BONEY Katatumba is Pakistan's Honorary Consul in Uganda.
Are your achievements a fulfilment of your childhood dreams?

Original_Search_Title: Walking On the Trails of Mungiki
Headline: Kenya: Walking On the Trails of Mungiki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Is Mungiki a cultural group, a terror gang for hire or a group of organised extortionists?
University of Nairobi sociology lecturer, Mr Ken Ouko, says Mungiki is a product of unemployment and poverty, but which has been helped to thrive by crooked businessmen in the public transport sector. "Matatu owners are to blame because they entertained Mungiki," said Ouko.

Original_Search_Title: Stars of Our Time
Headline: Kenya: Stars of Our Time
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The year was 1995 and I was one very happy teenager as we watched the great pulsating sensation of the time, Five Alive, rock the Hard-rock CafÈ at Barclays Plaza in Nairobi. Kids were shouting the white roof of the plush and classy neon-ad dÈcor of the cafe down: "Five alive, five alive " they chanted.
The quintet, comprising Eric Wainaina, Victor Seii, Chris Kamau, Joe Kiragu and Bob Kioko were, at the time, a handful of only two handfuls of local celebrity musicians in the entire country - and just three years before, there had not been even that.

Original_Search_Title: Senators Defend President Over Scam Allegations
Headline: Nigeria: Â£6 Million Mess - Senators Defend Mark
Body_Text:
Lagos — PLOT to discredit Senate president, Senator David Mark yesterday thickened with allegation that he was involved in £6 million scam.
According to an agency report, Saharareport, a clue of Mark's alleged wealth was gleaned from a bitter divorce suit filed by his ex-wife, Mrs Victoria Preye Mark in 2000.

Original_Search_Title: Riding the Train to Namboole Victory
Headline: Uganda: Riding the Train to Namboole Victory
Body_Text:
It was over a decade since the brown colonial block, which hosts Uganda Railways, had seen many people. Thanks to the Uganda Cranes match versus Nigeria's Super Eagles, fans could use a train in Uganda after many years, most for the first time in their lives.
Rift Valley Railways (RVR), the new company that took over the Uganda Railways last November, was the brain behind this arrangement.

Original_Search_Title: Helicopters Welcome
Headline: Botswana: Helicopters Welcome
Body_Text:
Recent media reports that the Botswana Police Service is a step closer to establishing an Air Wing has been greeted with applause from various sectors of the society. "It is definitely a positive step. We have always advocated for the allocation of resources to the Police Service.
Helicopters would definitely give them the capacity to discharge their mandate effectively," said Baboloki Tlale, Executive Secretary, of Botswana Council of Non-Governmental Organisation. He said the public has always expressed concern that the Botswana Defence Force was being given more support by the government, while the Police Service seemed to be overlooked. "This will definitely help address those concerns. It would give police more resources and modernise them. It would give them more visibility thus helping prevent crime," he said.

Original_Search_Title: First Jobs of Kampala's Renowned Rich
Headline: Uganda: First Jobs of Kampala's Renowned Rich
Body_Text:
Kampala — MONEY seems to be at their beck and call. They cruise about in luxurious cars and sleep in lavish bungalows. Some of them own plush hotels, banks, restaurant chains and shopping arcades. But did you know that many of our prominent success stories were once cash-strapped and survived by doing odd jobs? Titus Serunjogi reveals how some of the successful people in Kampala started out
David Katumwa

Original_Search_Title: Danida Out to Improve People's Access to Abundant, Clean Water
Headline: Zambia: Danida Out to Improve People's Access to Abundant, Clean Water
Body_Text:
THE crisis in water and sanitation is above all, a crisis for the poor. Almost two in three people lack access to clean water and survive on less than US$ 2 a day, with one in three, living on less than $1 a day.
The distribution of access to adequate water and sanitation in many countries mirrors the distribution of wealth and access to piped water into the household which averages about 85 per cent for the wealthiest, 20 per cent of the population, compared with 25 per cent for the poorest 20 per cent.

Original_Search_Title: Do Not Shun People Living With Disabilities - Mujuru
Headline: Zimbabwe: Do Not Shun People Living With Disabilities - Mujuru
Body_Text:
Harare — ALL economic sectors should take on board people living with disabilities as in some cases they were being discriminated against, Vice President Joice Mujuru said yesterday.
People with disabilities, just like able-bodied persons, had a critical role to play in the development of Zimbabwe.

Original_Search_Title: The Colour of Poverty is the Same Everywhere
Headline: Kenya: The Colour of Poverty is the Same Everywhere
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The colour of poverty in Murang'a is like that of desperation in Migori. Poverty is pale-dark, deathly and ominous.
The shape of hunger in Magarini is like that in Mt Elgon, where thousands have been displaced. The dispossession has made desperate otherwise potentially hardworking individuals. You cannot tell these people the economy is growing when they rely on charity rations.

Original_Search_Title: Suspected Witches Survive Mob Action
Headline: Uganda: Suspected Witches Survive Mob Action
Body_Text:
Mukono — AS thousands gathered at Namugongo Shrine on June 3 to commemorate the murder of the faithful for the sake of Christianity, an angry mob stormed the residence of a suspected witch and destroyed his property.
After missing the suspect, George William Kiwanuka and his accomplice Godfrey Migadde, the mob vandalised his residential house, set ablaze a shrine, chicken house and latrine.

Original_Search_Title: Two Officers Shot Dead in Cold Blood
Headline: Kenya: Two Officers Shot Dead in Cold Blood
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Two policemen were last night shot dead in Nairobi's Mathare slums, in an attack that was immediately blamed on followers of the outlawed Mungiki sect.
The officers were also robbed of their AK-47 rifles in the 8pm attack.

Original_Search_Title: From Nnamdi to Nnamani
Headline: Nigeria: From Nnamdi to Nnamani
Body_Text:
Lagos — So far eleven individuals have served as Senate President in the country's history. Their story has been just as diverse as their backgrounds and political convictions.
- Dr. Nnamdi Azikiwe

Original_Search_Title: Man Who Killed Wife Over Utensils Vows to Use Them
Headline: Kenya: Man Who Killed Wife Over Utensils Vows to Use Them
Body_Text:
Nairobi — On January 15, a 70-year-old man walked into a police post in Murang'a South District to deliver gruesome news. He had killed his wife after a quarrel over kitchen utensils.
According to Mr Samson Karimi Ng'ang'a, his wife of 45 years had cursed him by saying he should never use some of their utensils after her death.

Original_Search_Title: Mining Company to Displace Thousands
Headline: Uganda: Mining Company to Displace Thousands
Body_Text:
Tororo — "How about this hill? Who is the owner of this hill?" asked Jimmy Kisara, a member of Nilefos Minerals team surveying Sukulu Valley residents. The team was assessing the value of the residents' assets before the company starts mining phosphates from the mineral rich area, displacing between 8,000 and 10,000 people.
"It's mine, from this side," Abdu Kakande replied, pointing in the distance to the rising slope behind the cluster of houses and patch of banana trees. "Where is madam? Sometimes she knows things as well," interjected Local Chairman David Okurut, present at the survey to make sure everything goes smoothly and his constituents' property is duly accounted for.

Original_Search_Title: Kudirat - Chairmanship Aspirant Calls for Justice
Headline: Nigeria: Kudirat - Chairmanship Aspirant Calls for Justice
Body_Text:
Lagos — As human rights activists and the entire nation today remember the slain wife of the acclaimed winner of the June 12, 1993 presidential election, late Mrs. Kudirat Abiola, a local government chairmanship hopeful, Comrade Ayodele Adewale has wondered why justice has not yet taken its cause on her killers, eleven years after.
It will be recalled that Kudirat Abiola was gunned down on June 4, 1996 at the 7Up junction, Alausa by the alleged killer squad of the late despot, Gen. Sani Abacha.

Original_Search_Title: June Reminds Us of Responsible Tourism
Headline: Tanzania: June Reminds Us of Responsible Tourism
Body_Text:
Arusha — Responsible tourism promises to be a reality in Arusha. Major environment and development events organized for early June in Arusha support the claim.
Based on the concept of sustainable development, Arusha Municipal Council conducts a five-day 'Environment Fair' every year, which culminates on the World Environment Day on 5th of June. The fair is held at Kijenge Town Park. Meanwhile, based on a similar perspective, TATO and the Department of Tourism conduct independently, the 'Karibu Travel and Tourism Fair' in Arusha every year during the same period. The Karibu Fair is staged at the prisons grounds. Common sense informs that the two fairs would make lasting impacts if they were coordinated under one umbrella since sustainable tourism depends on sound environmental management practices. Is it worrying or surprising that this common sense is not common to organizers of the two events? Yes and no.

Original_Search_Title: Where Pilgrims And Party Animals Mix
Headline: Uganda: Where Pilgrims And Party Animals Mix
Body_Text:
Kampala — Today, Namugongo Martyrs' Shrine is a centre of attraction to countless pilgrims. However, this is not the only martyrs' shrine in the country.
AS Christians from different nations pay homage to the Uganda Martyrs at the Namugongo shrines today, one can hardly ignore what goes on at Salaama, Munyonyo.

Original_Search_Title: Martyrs' Day - Jungula, the Well of Blessings
Headline: Uganda: Martyrs' Day - Jungula, the Well of Blessings
Body_Text:
Kampala — A girl after drawing 'holy water' from the well walks past two crosses marking the spot where Jean-Marie Muzeeyi was killed.
AS Christians throng Namugongo Martyrs Shrine today one may be forgiven if they forget the existence of places like Jungula. Situated in a valley opposite Winners Chapel off Rubaga Road, this is where Jean-Marie Muzeeyi Kiwanuka was murdered on January 27, 1887.

Original_Search_Title: Constituency Profiles - Imenti South - Murungi to Bank on Projects
Headline: Kenya: Constituency Profiles - Imenti South - Murungi to Bank on Projects
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Eyes from all corners of the country will be set on South Imenti where Energy Minister Kiraitu Murungi whose careless talk made him stumble in politics, then rise again, will be defending his seat.
The long tongue he developed after joining the Cabinet when Narc came to power, bashing every Government critic, tended to portray him nationally as a monster.

Original_Search_Title: Reject Puppet Senate President - Nnamani
Headline: Nigeria: Reject Puppet Senate President - Nnamani
Body_Text:
Abuja — Spirited plot to stop Senator David Mark as the next Senate president was further intensified yesterday as the outgoing Senate president, Chief Ken Nnamani asked the in-coming senators to resist Chief Olusegun Obasanjo's hand picked leadership.
Senator Nnamani in his valedictory speech yesterday urged in-coming senators to ignore the instructions and choice of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) for the plum job and go ahead to elect a Senate president of their choice next Tuesday.

Original_Search_Title: Senate President Dissolves National Assembly
Headline: Nigeria: President Dissolves National Assembly
Body_Text:
Abuja — The Senate President, Senator Ken Nnamani and the Speaker of the Federal House of Representatives Hon. Aminu Bello Masari yesterday hit the last gavel on the table to adjourn the two chambers, the Senate and House of Representatives sine die, thus bringing to an end the fourth session of the fifth parliament.
The adjournment was immediately followed by the circulation of a proclamation by the President, Alhaji Umar Yar'Adua, dissolving the National Assembly with effect from 12 midnight tomorrow (Saturday).

Original_Search_Title: Don't Impose My Successor, Nnamani Tells PDP
Headline: Nigeria: Don't Impose My Successor, Nnamani Tells PDP
Body_Text:
Abuja — THE 2003 Senate adjourned sine die yesterday with its President, Chief Ken Nnamani, on his last day as presiding officer warning the People's Democratic Party (PDP) against imposing its choice of his successor on the next Senate.
Chief Nnamani's predecessor, Chief Adolphus Wabara, at the valedictory session poured venom on the immediate past President of the country, Chief Olusegun Obasanjo, praising the Senate for frustrating what he described as "Obasanjo's devilish plans."

Original_Search_Title: Massa the Scoring Machine
Headline: Uganda: Massa the Scoring Machine
Body_Text:
Uganda faces off with Nigeria at Namboole stadium tomorrow and adrenaline is high in anticipation of the Cranes humbling the Eagles.
Cranes key striker 21-year-old Geoffrey Massa missed out on the first leg in Abeoukuta where the Cranes' goal was cancelled unfairly, allowing the Nigerian Eagles to win the game. The time for revenge is on and Massa thinks the Nigerians are in for a beating.

Original_Search_Title: Handicraft - Lifting Women From Poverty
Headline: Rwanda: Handicraft - Lifting Women From Poverty
Body_Text:
Kigali — There are a number of beautiful art items Rwanda can make by using local materials and hands. They are promoted through associations and trainings. Women can use the chance of the advanced skills and wide markets to advance in their standard of living. They have come together, organised themselves and formed associations. They are preaching the results.
The Gasabo Hand Craft Association (Apaga), a women's association that came into existence after its members realised that women can do something and get out of poverty, is one of these clusters.

Original_Search_Title: Collateral Damage
Headline: Kenya: Collateral Damage
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Muted at first, but now with increasing alacrity, the murderous spate of violence and beheadings visited upon Kenyan matatu (public service vehicles) fraternity in select routes within the city and some parts of Central and the Rift Valley provinces is finally driving both the fares and the cost of operating matatus to new heights as drivers and their touts relocate en masse to safer routes.
As the public comes to terms with the recent bizarre developments, matatu owners are pulling fleets out of some routes, putting to risk the future of public transportation in Kenya.

Original_Search_Title: Let's Get Serious About CHOGM
Headline: Uganda: Let's Get Serious About CHOGM
Body_Text:
Kampala — The November Commonwealth Heads of Government meeting (CHOGM) will be the highest profile event ever hosted in this country. If all goes according to plan not only will the Queen of England come to visit, but all 52 heads of state will turn up - except Zimbabwe's Robert Mugabe.
Successfully hosting CHOGM may change the stereo type of Uganda as a dysfunctional, banana republic run by a bloody thirsty dictator.

Original_Search_Title: Constituency Profiles: Chepalungu
Headline: Kenya: Constituency Profiles: Chepalungu
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Few people would have guessed why retired Army major Joseph Kiptanui Chepkwony sat the Kenya Certificate of Secondary Examination last year - long time after he left the disciplined forces and primary school.
However, it is now clear he had a burning ambition to take on Cabinet minister John Koech for the Chepalungu seat.

Original_Search_Title: Memorial Day is Freedom Day for Americans!
Headline: Zimbabwe: Memorial Day is Freedom Day for Americans!
Body_Text:
Harare — SUNDAY, May 27, was Memorial Day and preceeded the actual holiday, which falls on Monday.
It marks a day for remembering Americans killed in war, defending freedom. Americans come out best if the observer simply observes their way of life to illustrate their love for life and freedom.

Original_Search_Title: Electoral Commissions - Africa's Biased Refs
Headline: Africa: Electoral Commissions - Africa's Biased Refs
Body_Text:
Harare — The whole of Southern Africa is agog with excitement over the hosting of the Soccer World Cup in South Africa in 2010.
Every country in the region is doing its best to position itself to make as much mileage as possible in terms of tourist traffic and foreign currency earnings.

Original_Search_Title: Experts Advocate Enriched Flour
Headline: Uganda: Experts Advocate Enriched Flour
Body_Text:
Kampala — HEALTH experts have urged the Government to introduce mandatory enrichment of flour with vitamins and minerals to reduce the rate of birth defects.
The most common defects include hydrocephalus, an accumulation of fluid within the skull and spina bifida, resulting from incorrect development of the spinal cord.

Original_Search_Title: Time to Look Our 'Benefactors' Straight in the Eye
Headline: Uganda: Time to Look Our 'Benefactors' Straight in the Eye
Body_Text:
Isn't it time we looked "the donors" in the eye and told them that they are the problem of Africa? Many theories and schools of thought have been advanced in attempts to explain the problem of Africa. African leaders clinging to power is one such theory.
Too much good fortune, believe it or not, is another - you see, in Mother Africa, organic fruits, bananas, tubers and vegetables available for any passer-by, eagerly fall from trees, on their own, to feed her children and when they have had their fill. Africa's children can lie anyway on Mother's bossom and slumber without the danger of frost.

Original_Search_Title: AU Enjoys China's Dollar Diplomacy
Headline: Kenya: AU Enjoys China's Dollar Diplomacy
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ghanaian President John Kufuor's gratitude to China last Friday illustrated how off guard some African leaders are. Anyway, hindsight in advance remains illusive. That's why caution exists.
Mr Kufuor, the current chairman of the African Union, attended a ceremony to lay the foundation stone for the organisation's $150 million Chinese-funded conference hall in Addis Ababa. Currently, AU summits use the Economic Commission for Africa's hall.

Original_Search_Title: Message to President Jammeh - Improving the Country
Headline: Gambia: Message to President Jammeh - Improving the Country
Body_Text:
Berlin — Happy Birthday President Dr. Jammeh,
Now that you are 42 years younger, I feel like dropping our First Birthday Kid some thought-provoking lines. I would have loved to deliver these glad tidings personally but my schedule won't allow me. Nonetheless, the ultima ratio of this letter is to exchange ideas on how to make life in The Gambia beautiful and sexy for our people. I am personally tired of all these bad news from Banjul and I hope the following romantic arguments would help us find some sweet solutions to some of the bitterest shortcomings at home:

Original_Search_Title: 'Breast Ironing' in Cameroon
Headline: Cameroon: 'Breast Ironing' in Cameroon
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Imagine having your breasts pounded with a hot grinding stone for hours on end everyday, for a month or two, until they flatten out and stop growing?
That's a real possibility if you are a 12-year-old girl beginning to sprout breasts in Douala, Cameroon. It's estimated that close to 50 per cent of women and girls around this region have had their breasts ironed to make them disappear. The practice is said to exist in all the ten provinces of Cameroon.

Original_Search_Title: Gusii Elders Sign Mosocho Declaration to Support Raila
Headline: Kenya: Gusii Elders Sign Mosocho Declaration to Support Raila
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Lang'ata MP, Mr Raila Odinga, demonstrated his commitment towards consolidating the Nyanza vote during his three-day campaign in Kisii, which culminated in the Mosocho Declaration.
His stay in the area saw a change of heart among the electorate, who turned out in huge numbers wherever he made impromptu stopovers.

Original_Search_Title: Traditional Rivals Khaniri's Headache
Headline: Kenya: Traditional Rivals Khaniri's Headache
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The incumbent, Mr George Khaniri, faces his traditional rivals in his bid for a fourth term to perpetuate a dynasty founded by his father Nicodemus.
The younger Khaniri went to Parliament through a by-election following his father's Nicodemus death in 1994.

Original_Search_Title: Karume's Fate Hangs On Race to State House
Headline: Kenya: Karume's Fate Hangs On Race to State House
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Veteran politician James Njenga Karume, who has dominated the politics of the larger Kiambu district for more than four decades, risks becoming a victim of the shifting national political alliances.
Mr Karume faces a dilemma in his Kiambaa constituency as he is operating in a region where there are bound to be two key parties competing for State House, ODM-Kenya which Gatundu South MP Uhuru Kenyatta who helped him retain the seat in 2002 is allied to and Narc Kenya.

Original_Search_Title: Exploring Genesis of the Outlawed Mungiki Sect
Headline: Kenya: Exploring Genesis of the Outlawed Mungiki Sect
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The outlawed Mungiki sect started in Murang'a District way back in 1999.
Matatu touts up in arms against the proscribed Mungiki sect. They were protesting against a daily 'protection' fee Mungiki charges them.

Original_Search_Title: Boxers Resort to Honey to Supplement Diet
Headline: Uganda: Boxers Resort to Honey to Supplement Diet
Body_Text:
Kampala — UGANDAN boxers at the Africa Boxing Championships in Madagascar have resorted to taking honey so that they make up for the 'poor' diet on their menu.
The boxers have to brave the breakfast with dry tea and hard French bread, which team doctor Ntege Ssengendo says is not healthy and beneficial to the fighters.

Original_Search_Title: Buziga - The Hide-Out for the Affluent
Headline: Uganda: Buziga - The Hide-Out for the Affluent
Body_Text:
Kampala — HERE was once hearsay that no one was allowed to build a house on Buziga Hill unless it was tiled. The truth of the matter hangs in the balance, but all the new houses that sprung up here have tiled roofs. Today, many developers are going for this plush neighbourhood that real estate prices have shot up.
Today, a 25-decimal plot goes for about sh80m at Buziga, thrice what it would have gone for a decade ago! Famous residents here include members of the first family, businessman Hassan Basajjabalaba and returnees from kyeyo (odd jobs abroad).

Original_Search_Title: Rwanda, Uganda Clash Over Dead Soldier
Headline: East Africa: Rwanda, Uganda Clash Over Dead Soldier
Body_Text:
Kampala — RWANDA has refused to release the body of a soldier for burial in Uganda. Both countries insist the former soldier is their citizen.
The Rwandan authorities buried the soldier in Kanobe military cemetery last evening after blocking the widow, Annet Abaine, from bringing the body to Uganda.

Original_Search_Title: President's Party to Tip the Scales in Kigumo Fight
Headline: Kenya: President's Party to Tip the Scales in Kigumo Fight
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It is hardly mentioned that President Jomo Kenyatta once represented Kigumo constituency now held by Onesmus Mwangi Kihara alias 'Bishop'.
Mr. Kariuki Njiiri who represented the area in Parliament (Legco) gave up the seat for Mr Kenyatta after he was released from detention in 1961 and from here, he began his long journey to the presidency.

Original_Search_Title: Search for Mungiki Suspects Moves to Nairobi
Headline: Kenya: Suspects Search Moves to City
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A search for suspected Mungiki sect members who planned executions in Murang'a and Kiambu districts has been extended to Nairobi.
Detectives have established that the killers were ferried from the city's Dandora estate to villages in Murang'a North and Kiambu districts where they beheaded four people.

Original_Search_Title: What People Say About the Arrest of Ministers
Headline: Uganda: What People Say About the Arrest of Ministers
Body_Text:
Kampala — FORMER ministers of state for health Mike Mukula and Alex Kamugisha were arrested on Tuesday. They were later charged with abuse office over donor funds and remanded to Luzira Prison. Geresom Musamali sought the views of the public in Kampala
Swaib Mubajje (Businessman):

Original_Search_Title: Street Children May Be a Future Menace
Headline: Rwanda: Street Children May Be a Future Menace
Body_Text:
Kigali — I am inspired to write on the future that awaits street children in Kigali and other urban areas. Their current trend leaves one to wonder if they will not grow up to be professional highway robbers or even diehard criminals.
As one travels along Nyabugogo highway in Kigali City, the street children can be seen stealing banana and Irish potatoes from moving trucks, yet nobody seems to mind what type of fellows these kids will grow up to be.

Original_Search_Title: One Year Down the Drain?
Headline: Uganda: One Year Down the Drain?
Body_Text:
WE witnessed the first anniversary of the 8th Parliament this very day last week. For many, especially the debutant MPs, there was reason to celebrate. It is understandable. We saw many of you walk through the Parliament foyer on May 16 and 17, 2006; some sporting tired shoes and many more shabbily dressed, not because you lacked taste or that your stylists were incompetent, its just that you were broke.
But your fortunes have since changed after you swore allegiance to country, vowing to "give faithful service to this parliament and support and uphold the constitution."

Original_Search_Title: Wolfowitz, Leadership And Ethics
Headline: Nigeria: Wolfowitz, Leadership And Ethics
Body_Text:
Last week Thursday, 17 May 2007, Mr. Paul D Wolfowitz, the World Bank President, finally threw in the towel and negotiated a mildly worded reprimand with the executive board of the Bank to resign effective 30 June 2007. This brings to a closure, the scandal that has over the months engulfed the World Bank President over matters of ethics. What brought down Wolfowitz was a little matter of indiscretion, which any leader here would brush aside with impunity. And have they done worse things here! Mr. Wolfowitz has a girlfriend, Ms Shaha Riza, who was a long-standing staff of the World Bank.
When Wolfowitz arrived at the Bank as President, he wanted to resolve an ethical dilemma of supervising his girlfriend, but in the process slipped into a worse scandal. He had her transferred to the State Department on a pay rise personally authorized by him without following due process. Wolfowitz reasoned that the pay rise was a compensation for disrupting Riza's career. This is the ethical banana peel on which the World Bank President slipped and fell down from his top job.

Original_Search_Title: Six Beheaded
Headline: Kenya: Six Beheaded
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Six people have been beheaded in yet another horror attack by killers suspected to be Mungiki.
The bizarre killings took place in Murang'a and Kiambu districts and are only the latest in a string of murders which security forces appear powerless to stop.

Original_Search_Title: From Wabara to Nnamani - a Valediction By the Senate
Headline: Nigeria: From Wabara to Nnamani - a Valediction By the Senate
Body_Text:
Lagos — Sufuyan Ojeifo looks at the current Senate, which is in the twilight of its life, and presents a composite picture of events and achievements that defined the era of Adolphus Wabara and Kenechuckwu Nnamani, the two men that led the Upper legislative chambers as Senate President in this tenure
On June 3, 2003, Adolphus Ndunuweh Wabara stepped in the saddle as President of the Senate, having won his election to represent Abia South Senatorial District in circumstances that were largely controversial: his strongest contender for the seat and candidate of the All Nigeria Peoples Party (ANPP), Elder Chinyere Imo was reported to have won the election consequent upon which the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) Returning Officer announced and gave him a Certificate of Return.

Original_Search_Title: 'Why Goje Was Re-Elected'
Headline: Nigeria: 'Why Goje Was Re-Elected'
Body_Text:
Lagos — Dr. Danlami Arab Rukejei, a medical doctor is the Gombe State Water Corporation Chairman. But beyond this, Rukejei, who hold the traditional title of Zannan Tangale, served as the General Secretary, Goje 2007 Campaign Organisation. In this interveiw, Rukejei spoke on the re-election of his boss, Governor Danjuma Goje; the opposition's alliance as well as calls for the cancellation of the 2007 elections, among others. Excerpts
How would you assess the conduct of the April general election? The result didn't come as a surprise to us because if you remember when I spoke with journalists before the elections, the issue was raised on what my position would be and I said I was not going to make any predictions. My prediction was going to be based on the outcome of the ballot and the outcome of ballot has manifested our anticipation concerning the kind of support the people of Gombe State have for this administration. The election was clear, clean, credible, fair, peaceful and the whole process was conducted smoothly with massive participation of the people, particularly electorates in the state. Also, contrary to the speculations and rumours being peddled by some unpatriotic citizens that the general elections will be marred by violence and mayhem,everything went peacefully and smoothly.

Original_Search_Title: Advertising Boon
Headline: Kenya: Advertising Boon
Body_Text:
Nairobi — KENYA'S MEDIA AND ADVE-rtising industry is waiting with bated breath for the start of the official campaign period - to be declared any time now - as it salivates at the prospect of making an estimated Ksh1 billion ($15 million) from politicians and political parties in the next general election.
Advertising insiders who spoke to The EastAfrican predicted that the record number of aspirants expected and the proliferation of media outlets makes for an explosive mix that will see the industry reap record profits.

Original_Search_Title: Abuja - Migrant Traders Face Hard Times
Headline: Nigeria: Abuja - Migrant Traders Face Hard Times
Body_Text:
"They drove me out of school because of money" was Emmanuel's answer when asked why he was in the street hawking when his age mates were in school.
Solomon Morgan, in another instance, sells CDs in Utako, a neighbourhood immediately next to Wuse if you hit the road towards Jabi, one of Abuja's most famous districts.

Original_Search_Title: Mbeki Camp Accused of 'Intimidation'
Headline: South Africa: Mbeki Camp Accused of 'Intimidation'
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — WITH less than 40 days before the succession battle of the African National Congress (ANC) comes to a head at the party's December conference, factions supporting ANC deputy president Jacob Zuma's bid for control yesterday accused backers of President Thabo Mbeki of intimidation.
They claim branch delegates are being bullied into supporting the president for a third term.

Original_Search_Title: Devolution As Defined in Drafts
Headline: Kenya: Devolution As Defined in Drafts
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Both the Wako draft, which was rejected at the referendum and the Bomas draft, which was trashed by the Government have some similarities on the system of devolution, which has sparked controversy.
The two documents are in agreement about the principle of devolution which, to all, means transferring power from the central government to the people.

Original_Search_Title: Gowon in History
Headline: Nigeria: Gowon in History
Body_Text:
Abuja — I remember the incident as if it occurred today. The year was 1974. I happened to be a teenager in my first year in secondary school.
The head of state was not only due in town, but also scheduled to visit our school. It was action stations.

Original_Search_Title: Ettehgate - Impunity vs Integrity
Headline: Nigeria: Ettehgate - Impunity vs Integrity
Body_Text:
I remember that evening. With boisterousness, then President Olusegun Obasanjo, beaming with the pride of a policeman who has just caught a thief, hijacked the airwaves and announced to a bewildered nation and the world at large that Senator Adolphus Wabara had been caught in a N55 million bribe-for-budget scam. Uncle Shege went on to lampoon Wabara, calling him names before finally asking him to resign with immediate effect.
In Wabara's case, Obasanjo did not give the man fair hearing. He did not even allow the Senate to investigate, much more debate the N55 million saga. He usurped the powers of the police and became the chief judge in the case. As far as he was concerned, the Supreme Court of Obasanjo had spoken and, for Wabara, it was res judicata.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaki Rejects Bill to Finance Parties
Headline: Kenya: Kibaki Rejects Bill to Finance Parties
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki has declined to sign the Political Parties Bill and sent it back to Parliament for amendment.
Sources yesterday said the President proposed that the Bill should give a definition of a "political party" which would not be in conflict with that of the Constitution and the National Assembly and Presidential Elections Act.

Original_Search_Title: Traders Distraught Over Rentals
Headline: Botswana: Traders Distraught Over Rentals
Body_Text:
The imposition of Gaborone City Council levies on a public area at the Gaborone Railway Station payable to a private company, Market Plaza appears to be ruining the business of vendors. Some vendors have even been dragged to courts of law for failure to pay rent at the taxi rank. City Mayor, Harry Mothei declined to respond to telephone enquiries about the new rent charges.
Town Clerk, Kutlwano Matenge, said on Wednesday: "If they are trading at a place designated for the bus rank then that is illegal.

Original_Search_Title: Mark, Etteh And the Collateral Damage to Due Process
Headline: Nigeria: Mark, Etteh And the Collateral Damage to Due Process
Body_Text:
Abuja — It has almost become a mantra for Senate President David Mark to stress his determination to serve out his four-year stint as the nation's number three citizen.
In his recent engagements in the Senate building, Senator Mark has taken to the pathway of repeatedly professing his loyalty to his 108 fellow Senators by whose grace, he says now and again, he occupies the office of Senate President.

Original_Search_Title: President Launches 'Prosperity for All' Programme
Headline: Uganda: President Launches Bona Bagaggawale
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni yesterday launched the prosperity for all programme, popularly known as Bonna Bagaggawale.
The President did not indicate how much money has been earmarked for the programme or when the Savings and Credit Cooperatives start accessing the money.

Original_Search_Title: We Could Have Avoided This Humiliation
Headline: Botswana/South Africa: We Could Have Avoided This Humiliation
Body_Text:
Gaborone — I am still reeling from shock after our national team lost to South Africa's third grade team. I know we still have some way to go before reaching the standard of our neighbours.
But honestly, I did not expect us to be felled by a group of youngsters who had only been in camp for a day. This is one result that is never going to make us proud.

Original_Search_Title: Ngilu Sorry for Backing Banana
Headline: Kenya: Ngilu Sorry for Backing Banana
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Health minister Charity Ngilu has apologised to her constituents for misleading them to support the Government in the 2005 Constitution referendum.
But she thanked them for voting against the proposed Constitution.

Original_Search_Title: Policemen Evil, Says the Public
Headline: Kenya: Policemen Evil, Says the Public
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The police yesterday came under intense criticism during a public inquiry on security.
Witnesses at the Kenya National Commission on Human Rights inquiry's session in Banana Hill, Kiambu District, accused the police of extortion and extra-judicial killings.

Original_Search_Title: Children Are The Main Victims of Fighting
Headline: Congo-Kinshasa: 'I Just Felt Really Sorry For Them'
Body_Text:
Goma — She was just 16 years old but already in her final weeks of pregnancy when Pascaline's hometown, the pretty lakeside town of Sake, erupted in fighting. The hills around Lake Kivu crackled with the sound of gunfire and RPGs as Tutsi rebel leader Laurent Nkunda tried to take the strategic town from Congolese government forces (FARDC).
The only way out was to head for Goma, on the foothills of the still active Nyiragongo Volcano, just 20 kms away. With nothing but the clothes on their backs, Pascaline and ten members of her family fled on foot. They were not alone -- thousands from all around the Masisi territory had the same idea. On some days between 5–16 September up to 20,000 people were fleeing to Goma.

Original_Search_Title: Dear Ngwa
Headline: Cameroon: Dear Ngwa
Body_Text:
You asked for a comfortable majority, we gave you a crushing majority, we are waiting to see what you'll do with it.
Come to think of it, what do you expect to do today that you've been unable or unwilling to do in the last quarter of a century? You came to power, don't forget, in 1982 when there was the one party system. You ran a rubber stamp Parliament and only eight years later did we rattle your complacency.

Original_Search_Title: Slippery Banana Skins
Headline: South Africa: Slippery Banana Skins
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — THE Banana Skins Survey 2007 of risks facing insurers, released yesterday, is bound to ignite an interesting debate. The report, compiled by the New York-based Centre for the Study of Financial Innovation and PricewaterhouseCoopers, identified political and regulatory pressure as one of the main risks facing insurers in SA.
Pressure to do black economic empowerment deals, the need to meet affirmative action targets under the financial sector charter and the development of products targeted at the poor black populace were identified as being politically motivated.

Original_Search_Title: Balala's New Hurdle in Mvita
Headline: Kenya: Balala's New Hurdle in Mvita
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Mvita MP Mr Najib Balala's received a standing ovation for his last-minute withdrawal from ODM presidential nominations race at Kasarani last week.
But this move may just have given his political opponents at the Coast new "fire power".

Original_Search_Title: Catholic Bishops - Men in the Muddle?
Headline: Zimbabwe: Catholic Bishops - Men in the Muddle?
Body_Text:
Harare — THE foot of Catholicism is thought to have stepped this part of the world around the 18th Century. But it was an itinerant foot, and to that extent, one which left no lasting print. Real lasting footprint of Catholicism gets the notice of history towards the end of the 19th Century, in about May 1879, following a Decree of Erection signed by Pope Leo XIII on 16th February 1879, which defined a proselytising mission north of the Limpopo. All told, eleven missionaries came to Mashonaland, straight from Grahamstown in the Cape Colony.
They were four Belgians, two Germans, two Englishmen, two Italians and one from Liechtenstein. Eleven years later, in 1890, Rhodes' so-called Pioneer Column would follow, obliging the Catholic brag that the flag followed the frock.

Original_Search_Title: Bandits Rob Travellers On Noni Highway
Headline: Cameroon: Bandits Rob Travellers On Noni Highway
Body_Text:
Highway robbers recently attacked travellers on the Lassin-Nkor road in Noni Subdivision, Northwest Province, robbing them of over seven cell phones, various property and money estimated at over FCFA 250,000.
According to some of the robbery victims, about 10 bandits blocked the road with banana stems at about 9 pm. Then they began ordering passengers in approaching vehicles to lie down, searched them and stole their property and money.

Original_Search_Title: N628m Scandal - the Cloud Thickens for Etteh
Headline: Nigeria: N628m Scandal - the Cloud Thickens for Etteh
Body_Text:
Lagos — Unless something dramatic happens, the threat of removal from office is dangling over the Speaker of the House of Representatives Hon Patricia Olubunmi Folake Etteh over the alleged illegal award of contract worth N628million for the renovation of the speaker and her deputy's official residences.
In this report Ademola Adeyemo examines the issues involved and available options for the embattled Speaker.

Original_Search_Title: Tanzania Hindering Regional Trade Talks With Europe
Headline: East Africa: Tanzania Hindering Regional Trade Talks With Europe
Body_Text:
Kigali — The East African Community (EAC) is set to sign an economic pact with the European Union (EU) ahead of a December 31 deadline to avoid losing the lucrative export markets in Europe, but Tanzania is yet to agree to the plan, Kenyan official has disclosed.
Kenya's Permanent Secretary in Trade Ministry David Nalo said the region would sign the new trade pact with EU despite obstacles posed by other EAC states because time was running out, which might jeopardize the region's exports.

Original_Search_Title: Behind the Scenes At Kalungu East By-Elections
Headline: Uganda: Behind the Scenes At Kalungu East By-Elections
Body_Text:
The two main opposition parties in Central region were locked in a battle; the main ruling party was rooting for pride and more ground.
After 5p.m. on Thursday August 16, the ruling NRM had succeeded in maintaining a crucial parliamentary seat and the opposition benches remained with fewer MPs on their side. Michael J. Ssali reports on the intricate by-election.

Original_Search_Title: The Highs And Lows of Narc-Kenya
Headline: Kenya: The Highs And Lows of Narc-Kenya
Body_Text:
Nairobi — One year after Narc-Kenya was formed, the flower party has largely been a shy and cautious outfit without a flag bearer or a timeframe to produce one.
It has desperately courted President Kibaki to lead it in the General Election later this year.

Original_Search_Title: 'NRZ Still Probing Train Collision'
Headline: Zimbabwe: 'NRZ Still Probing Train Collision'
Body_Text:
Harare — NATIONAL Railways of Zimbabwe officials are still carrying out investigations to ascertain the cause of the train collision that killed one person and injured several others in Harare last Thursday morning.
NRZ public relations manager Mr Fanuel Masikati said they had also engaged the services of other "necessary" stakeholders and parties to assist them in carrying out the investigations, according to the trains working regulations.

Original_Search_Title: Country's Oldest Chimp Turns 43
Headline: Uganda: Country's Oldest Chimp Makes 43 Years
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE birthday party had been delayed for two weeks to teach him table manners and how to cut the cake. But surrounded by hundreds of guests, Zakayo would not be bothered.
The king of the chimps of Budongo Island, who turned 43, lifted his heavy body and walked slowly towards the fence where his cake was being held out for him, only to squat down and ignore all calls from his minders to perform his act.

Original_Search_Title: Our Land is Dying
Headline: Kenya: Our Land is Dying
Body_Text:
Nairobi — At 85, Mzee Kiuri Kimaru has seen many things. The portly man says he has witnessed sacred natural sites in Karima Hill forest near Othaya town desecrated and most of the streams from the hill disappear.
"We have lost 20 streams here, and this is not the end," he says.

Original_Search_Title: The North and Yar'Adua Presidency
Headline: Nigeria: The North and Yar'Adua Presidency
Body_Text:
Jimeta — In May 29, 2007, power symbolically shifted to northern Nigeria; however the region is still nursing the wounds it sustained in the last eight years.
The north was relegated to the back seat in the economy, the public service, oil sector and has also lost command positions in the military, security agencies and corridors of real political power. The north was badly sidelined in the privatization program, none of the lucrative national enterprises that were sold went to the north, and also the people in the north bitterly acknowledged that they suffered a fatal injury in the recapitalization of the banking industry which many northerners regarded as specifically targeted at the North. Out of the 25 banks that emerged, the north has a stake in only one bank. But the hardest hit were the political leaders of the north, they were economically strangled and politically castigated.

Original_Search_Title: Regional Administrator Conducts Tour in Gash-Barka Region
Headline: Eritrea: Regional Administrator Conducts Tour in Gash-Barka Region
Body_Text:
Barentu — The Administrator of the Southern Red Sea region, Ms. Tsegereda Woldegergish, conducted a tour in Gash-Barka region, during which she observed the current climatic condition and overall activities.
The Administrator of Gash-Barka region, Mr. Kahsai Gebrehiwet accompanied her in the course of the visit aimed at observing and exchanging experience in the on-going development activities and those set for implementation through the Warsay-Yikaalo Campaign.

Original_Search_Title: Bitter Water to Sweet
Headline: Uganda: Bitter Water to Sweet
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Ruhiira Millennium Village project is part of a global effort to help villages in African countries to become self-sustaining by 2015. John Odyek visited Ruhiira to see if lives were being changed.
Like a grandfather who has many ripe bananas and therefore attracts many young friends and relatives to his home, Ruhiira Village in Isingiro District is becoming popular.

Original_Search_Title: PDP Chairmanship Tussle - Who Wears the Cap?
Headline: Nigeria: PDP Chairmanship Tussle - Who Wears the Cap?
Body_Text:
Lagos — By December 2007 Ahmadu Ali's chairmanship of the ruling PDP will cease. A candidate from the South East geo-political area where the office has been zoned will step in. Ike Abonyi examines the names already being peddled for the plumb job from the area and their chances
Against the backdrop of being one of the tripods upon which the political foundation of this country was laid the ethnic Igbo of the South East zone can rightly claim that it has lost out in the emerging scheme of things in the nation's polity. Since the nationís independence never has South East been without having somebody among the topmost position in the country. In fact they have never been without being among the top three.

Original_Search_Title: Monday Blues
Headline: Uganda: Monday Blues
Body_Text:
I decided to do something illegal for a change; being a model citizen will bore anyone rigid and I am no different. Feeling slightly rebellious one Monday morning, I figured it was high time I committed a traffic offence; a tiny misdemeanour that wouldn't hurt anyone, especially if nobody noticed. I imagine one must start small after all, and it will be a while, hopefully, before I make it into the ranks of our most hardcore felons.
Tricky: Heavy traffic on one of Kampala's busy roads. Sometimes, the urge to break a law or two is irresistible especially when you feel that it is the best way around a difficult situation. File Photo

Original_Search_Title: Raila Holds Six Rallies This Weekend
Headline: Kenya: Raila Reveals Why He's the Best Man for ODM Ticket
Body_Text:
Nairobi — For Lang'ata MP Mr Raila Odinga, it was one weekend and six rallies - another signal it is all systems go and that he intends to be at the finishing line in December.
"When you set on a journey, you do it with the intention of finishing, otherwise there is no need starting in the first place. That is why I am not taking any half measure," he told The Sunday Standard last week. He added: "You must have proper logistics, especially if it is a long distance journey. You can't enter a race with a plan to stop at the heats. You go hoping to get to the finals. I am confident I will get to the finals. I am confident that the people will make the right decision."

Original_Search_Title: Wrapping Up a Political Saga?
Headline: Ethiopia: Wrapping Up a Political Saga?
Body_Text:
A new chapter: Meles
An almost two-year long political saga in Ethiopia seems to be wrapping up, as 38 senior leaders and members of Coalition for Democracy and Unity party (CUD), and journalists were released from prison yesterday after their request for pardon was approved by the president of the Federal Republic of Ethiopia (FDRE) Girma Woldegiorgis on Thursday.

Original_Search_Title: Government Dispels Fears of Looming Earthquake
Headline: East Africa: Government Dispels Fears of Looming Earthquake
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government has dismissed as rumors reports that a major earthquake was looming even as it requested Kenyans to be vigilant.
Internal Security minister Mr John Michuki said it is impossible to predict an earthquake. He termed as rumor mongers people who called their friends and relatives to sound an alarm of an impending earthquake.

Original_Search_Title: Motorola Changes Nansubuga's Life
Headline: Uganda: Motorola Changes Nansubuga's Life
Body_Text:
Sophie Nansubuga won a 'start up' package from Motorola that includes a free kiosk, with a solar powered battery that can charge up to 20 phones in one go, brand new mobile phones for sale, a landline pay phone and airtime cards for sale, including rent for a year, which changed her life forever
Thirty two-year-old Sophie Nansubuga's typical day didn't always have surprises. She woke up early every morning, went to the market, purchased banana bunches (matooke) that she and her mother would sell that day, and left in the evening with a meagre profit. But not that day.

Original_Search_Title: Fruit And Vegetable Issues Top List in Maragua Campaign
Headline: Kenya: Fruit And Vegetable Issues Top List in Maragua Campaign
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Constituency Profiles: Maragua
Maragua constituency which remains one of the least developed in Central Province is a fruits basket.

Original_Search_Title: Weighing the Cost of Going Green
Headline: Uganda: Weighing the Cost of Going Green
Body_Text:
Kampala — More than two weeks since it was announced, and despite a grace period till September, the ban on packaging polythene bags has sparked fears of how consumers will adjust and law enforces are fretting over how the ban will be implemented.
Finance Minister announced in his 2007/08 National Budget a ban on plastic bags - buveera - of less than 30 microns and imposed a 120 per cent tax on the rest.

Original_Search_Title: Mungiki Sect Killings Leave at Least 22 Dead
Headline: Kenya: It's a Night of Terror As Mungiki Strike
Body_Text:
Nairobi — It was a night of grisly Mungiki killings and action by police against suspects that left 22 people dead. In what police believe were revenge killings, three people were beheaded in Banana, Kiambu; five were shot dead in a Kariobangi bar and three while driving along Kangundo Road.
The killings came in the wake of the sentencing on Thursday to a five-year jail term of former Mungiki leader John Maina Njenga on gun and bhang possession charges.

Original_Search_Title: Govt Advises On Buveera
Headline: Uganda: Govt Advises On Buveera
Body_Text:
Kampala — The government is considering the use of locally made sisal and banana fibre bags known as bikapu as replacements for polythene bags.
While reading the 2007/08 National Budget last week, Finance Minister Ezra Suruma banned the production and importation of polythene bags commonly known as buveera.

Original_Search_Title: Like Balogun, Like Ehindero
Headline: Nigeria: Like Balogun, Like Ehindero
Body_Text:
Lagos — He came without a bang. He had a whimperish visage. Almost lacking in charm and charisma. But he, all the same ascended the hot and privileged seat of the number one cop of the nation. His name is Gabriel Sunday Ehindero. He studied Mathematics and eventually Law. That explains why most of his lectures never wandered from "paradigm shift" He hails from the northern part of Undo State. When he succeeded the heavily dirtied personality of Mr Tafa Balogun, many thought he would be far more careful in distinguishing between personal funds and Police funds. He made scathing remarks about the itchy fingers of Balogun, when he just came into office. He spoke like one anointed for saintly service. Now we know better. Perhaps former president Obasanjo knew better than we all did. He almost did not want to confirm his appointment as IG.
Although he was supposed to have been retired a few months ago, he was expediently retained in the force for the obvious reason of helping to execute the dirty brief of organizing a fraudulent election. It is not certain what brief he got, but the performance of the Police and other law enforcement agencies during the April polls, is self evident that whatever the brief was, it was not a righteous one. Many are even saying that the N21.6 million found with Mr John Obaniyi, the CP, Budget was a fall-out of the messy polls. Most of the N27 billion said to have been stolen by Tafa Balogun were said to have also come from the 2003 polls. So now we know the Police are heavily bribed during polls to aid and abet electoral fraud. That is why the trio of Obasanjo, Maurice Iwu and Ehindero must be core characters in the probe of the electoral misadventure of last April.

Original_Search_Title: Training Vital in Managing GMOs
Headline: Zimbabwe: Training Vital in Managing GMOs
Body_Text:
Harare — A TRAINING workshop on Biosafety Clearing House that was held recently by the Biotechnology Authority of Zimbabwe represented a significant step in scaling up efforts to safely manage genetically modified organisms and to fulfil Zimbabwe's obligations under the Cartagena Protocol on Biosafety.
Scientists, policy makers, regulators and researchers participated at the training workshop held in Harare from June 5-7.

Original_Search_Title: Pam Goes to Mbale
Headline: Uganda: Pam Goes to Mbale
Body_Text:
Grey skies have often been a fixture at the Pam awards North Eastern regional launches in Mbale. But the threat of the skies opening up has never dissuaded the town's music-loving folks from partaking of this annual Celtel/ Bell Lager treat that brings together the region's top musicians and some of the nation's big names.
Rather than have a concert that usually stretched into the wee hours of the morning, the performers' bill was whittled down significantly. The plan this year was to have only 10 regional artistes chosen through radio listener votes. The rules had to be bent last Saturday at the Mbale Resort Hotel Annex to accommodate twice the designated number, something that elicited murmurs from those who were denied access to the lofty stage.

Original_Search_Title: Tough Battle Looms in Shikuku's Former Base
Headline: Kenya: Tough Battle Looms in Shikuku's Former Base
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The constituency, made famous during the tenure of "people's watchman" Martin Shikuku, once again comes into focus. But Mr Shikuku whose name was synonymous with the area for four decades, is said to be shifting base to Nairobi's Kamukunji.
If this happens, he will leave the battlefield to incumbent Wycliffe Oparanya, his predecessor Amukowa Anangwe, nominated MP Ruth Oniang'o and newcomer Shabanji Opukah.

Original_Search_Title: Compliance Ranks Highest of Issues Facing Banks
Headline: South Africa: Compliance Ranks Highest of Issues Facing Banks
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — AS THE most influential bankers met in Cape Town early this week for their annual pilgrimage to the International Monetary Conference, one issue continued to nag them. It was not just the unregulated activities of private equity firms and hedge funds posing a risk to the buoyant global economy and soaring financial markets, but also the compliance cost of bank regulations.
They had just been handed a confidential report entitled The Banana Skin Report. It had an interesting, if not chilling, statistic. Out of the 200 issues identified, the cost of complying with bank regulations was the biggest facing the industry globally.

Original_Search_Title: There Must Be Fresh Elections - Isyaku Ibrahim
Headline: Nigeria: There Must Be Fresh Elections - Isyaku Ibrahim
Body_Text:
A member of the Board of Trustees (BOT) of the ruling People's Democratic Party (PDP), Alhaji Isyaku Ibrahim has said that notwithstanding Tues-day's inauguration of Alhaji Umaru Musa Yar'Adua as president, there must be fresh free and fair elections if Nige-ria is to have a genuine and lasting democracy. Anything short of that will consign the country to the status of a Banana Republic, he said.
Alhaji Ibrahim, who is a founding member of the PDP and one of its pioneer financiers, asserted that since the new administration is founded on the basis of flawed elections not only democracy but even the cor-porate existence of the coun-try is in serious danger of collapse. He said right from day one it is bedeviled by cri-sis of legitimacy adding that nothing positive should therefore be expected now or later.

Original_Search_Title: Must Botswana Tolerate Corruption?
Headline: Botswana: Must Botswana Tolerate Corruption?
Body_Text:
Gaborone — A finance minister during Sani Abacha's regime in Nigeria responded to questions about the levels of abuse of state assets.
"Koropshon is paat en passel of Afrikan kulture," he said.

Original_Search_Title: Latter-Day 'Children of Mau Mau' Have a Case
Headline: Kenya: Latter-Day 'Children of Mau Mau' Have a Case
Body_Text:
Nairobi — These are paradoxical times when a 'normal crime' tag takes the shape of the deviant and the pathological, with baffling business as usual casualness.
Security officers perpetually plan to counter as Mungiki strikes. And Mungiki reassures that the public has nothing to worry because the sect knows its targets: They are sellouts, defaulting sponsors and the propertied who have reneged on pledges.

Original_Search_Title: Nakaseero Vendors Reward Lukwago
Headline: Uganda: Nakaseero Vendors Reward Lukwago
Body_Text:
Kampala — MP Erias Lukwago was on Monday given a basketful of fruits and chicken for fathering twins. The chairperson of vendors, Ali Kayanja Rwere, amidst ululations said: "You did a great job when you fathered those twins. Not anyone can accomplish that."
Lukwago was part of an entourage of MPs on the local government accounts committee who were on a tour of Nakasero Market to assess the wrangles between contractor Sheila Investments Limited and the vendors. The other MPs looked on open-mouthed. Overwhelmed, Lukwago lifted two cocks high and bowed incessantly to the vendors as a sign of respect.

Original_Search_Title: Six Suspects Paraded By Police
Headline: Kenya: Six Suspects Paraded By Police
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Police paraded six suspects whom they say are behind the spate of insecurity in the parts of Central Province.
Detectives said the men aged between 30 and 50 are behind the beheading of four people in Kiambu and Muranga and distribution of leaflets inciting youths to violence.

Original_Search_Title: The Impending Deluge of Foreign Investments
Headline: Nigeria: The Impending Deluge of Foreign Investments
Body_Text:
Just as I sent in last week's article entitled 'The Vicious Cycle of Patronage', which was targeted at addressing the minds of Nigerians to our social culpability in the problem of corruption; a result of a sort of culture clash, where appointees and 'electees' are congratulated in newspapers for winning elections and for being appointed, and huge amounts of money is so expended in the hope of getting some back from such lucky winner, I ran into the report of one Alhaji Wali, one of the campaign coordinators/mobilisers for the mighty party PDP. The honorable Alhaji Wali had gone to visit the President-elect and made no mince of his intentions in reminding the Alhaji Yar'adua to remember those of them who slaved and fought to ensure the victory of PDP.
In very strong language which belie the sort of energy and desperation put into the assignments (ensuring the victory of the party), many times at great personal risk to the campaigners, he said he had to cry out as it seemed that 'the monkeys have worked and now some baboons are positioning to chop'. Alhaji Wali instructed President-elect Yar'adua to ignore the baboons and focus on assisting the monkeys get into position, apparently in order to chop. I have used Alhaji Wali's exact words and references regarding monkeys and baboons. No commentator, as far as I know, has bothered to comment on the implications of this kind of talk to our collective aspirations in Nigeria. Whereas the reference to monkeys and baboons is within the context of a colloquial proverb in Nigeria, the whole idea is about corruption, not service. God forbid that the affairs of this much-endowed country in the next four years and beyond, should be run by people who see themselves as the monkeys who are poised to devour the bananas.

Original_Search_Title: A Case of 'Two Fighting'?
Headline: Nigeria: A Case of 'Two Fighting'?
Body_Text:
Lagos — Surprise surprise: Nigerian politicians want to fight corruption. These days, I get very sceptical whenever politicians say they are interested in fighting corruption, especially after the PTDF experience. I'm sorry to say this, but it sounds quite oxymoronic to say a Nigerian politician is interested in probity.
Yet one politician I have come to respect is Chief Ken Nnamani, the President of the Senate. Ever since he took over the mantle of leadership from Chief Adolphus Wabara, he has brought some level of sanity and dignity to the Upper Chamber. Now we hardly hear of "Ghana Must Go" bags polluting the Senate again. The bribe-for-budget scandal appears to have become a thing of the past. Bribe-for-clearance, which used to dog the screening of ministerial nominees, is now hardly spoken of, either in whispers or on mountain top. The "banana peel" which, in the words of the late Dr. Chuba Okadigbo, trips every Senate president, has hopefully been swept into the dustbin of history by the Nnamani regime.

Original_Search_Title: Ford Kenya Tottering Towards Obscurity
Headline: Kenya: Ford Kenya Tottering Towards Obscurity
Body_Text:
Nairobi — By 2002, Ford-Kenya was the 'lion' party in Western Province. Sadly today, few people mention it although its leadership claim it is the party in the province.
After party elections last year, national chairman, Mr Musikari Kombo, who retained the position unopposed, promised that his immediate task would be to strengthen the party. Several months later, there is strong evidence that the party is slowly receding into oblivion.

Original_Search_Title: Police Killings Alarm the Force
Headline: Kenya: Police Killings Alarm the Force
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The killing of three police officers in Nairobi by armed gangsters on Thursday has alarmed the force.
Police suspect that former colleagues could have been involved in the daytime attacks. A detective who sought anonymity said the daring manner in which the attacks were executed point to the work of former police officers.

Original_Search_Title: Narc-K Pulls in Separate Ways
Headline: Kenya: Narc-K Pulls in Separate Ways
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Narc-Kenya leaders will meet on Monday under the aegis of the Government of National Unity Parliamentary Group to disabuse perception it is rudderless and has a bloated and ineffective Executive.
The meeting follows weeks of backstabbing and second-guessing, which have turned the party into the Tower of Babel. It was the week in which assistant ministers disrespected ministers and party leaders, throwing the nascent party into a new spin.

Original_Search_Title: Stereotypes, Where Do You Fall?
Headline: Uganda: Stereotypes, Where Do You Fall?
Body_Text:
Kampala — Eh! Those Bakiga women, you better be careful with them, they beat the daylights out of their men!" "And what about those Baganda men with their larger than life noses?"
These are just some of the many stereotypical remarks doing the rounds around Kampala, most of which are actually extreme and totally off the mark.

Original_Search_Title: Blair: Gordon Brown's Gordian Knot
Headline: Zimbabwe: Blair: Gordon Brown's Gordian Knot
Body_Text:
Harare — THE British people are a baffling lot. Much as they fulminate about other peoples' constitutions, they themselves have none. And they do not allow lesser peoples to emulate their constitution-free democracy, never mind that both the illegal and unjust war against Iraq, and the stinking "cash-for-honours" scandal have clearly shown that even in the land of Albion --itself home to Lord Acton -- absolute power still corrupts absolutely
THE British people are a baffling lot. Much as they fulminate about other peoples' constitutions, they themselves have none. And they do not allow lesser peoples to emulate their constitution-free democracy, never mind that both the illegal and unjust war against Iraq, and the stinking "cash-for-honours" scandal have clearly shown that even in the land of Albion --itself home to Lord Acton -- absolute power still corrupts absolutely.

Original_Search_Title: The More Things Change, the More They Remain the Same
Headline: Kenya: The More Things Change, the More They Remain the Same
Body_Text:
Nairobi — THERE WAS A TIME WHEN it was dead easy to tell who stood where in Kenyan politics. It was a straightforward "either/or" situation.
You were either playing sycophant or rotting in detention. At best, you would be playing cat-and-mouse games with the secret service.

Original_Search_Title: Police Alert as Sect Drops Scare Leaflets
Headline: Kenya: Police Alert as Sect Drops Scare Leaflets
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Leaflets calling on people believed to be adherents of the outlawed Mungiki sect to 'arise' sent panic across three provinces where they were circulated, putting police on red alert.
The leaflets also ask, "Does (President) Kibaki really know the old guard around him are lying to him?"

Original_Search_Title: Tourism Board Eyes the Western Kenya Circuit
Headline: Kenya: Tourism Board Eyes the Western Kenya Circuit
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Kenya Tourist Board has turned its attention to promoting the country's Western region as a tourist circuit, with the little-known Saiwa Swamp, which is the country's smallest National Park as the entry point.
This is part of a campaign to diversify tourists' itineraries away from the well-beaten Masaai-Mara - Tsavo - Coast loop.

Original_Search_Title: Make Or Break for Kombo in the West
Headline: Kenya: Make Or Break for Kombo in the West
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Mr Musikari Kombo will be in the spotlight this year as he fights to retain the Webuye parliamentary seat. It will be a pivotal election where his clout, and that of his party Ford Kenya, will be put to test.
The political backyard he is defending extends to the rest of Bungoma district, onto the neighbouring Trans Nzoia district - in the Rift Valley Province - which is also inhabited by the Bukusu sub-ethnic group of the Luhya, and onto the rest of Luhyaland. The latter has become a major battleground for the main political parties.

Original_Search_Title: Crew in Lucky Escape as Fire Chopper Crashes in Flames
Headline: South Africa: Crew in Lucky Escape as Fire Chopper Crashes in Flames
Body_Text:
Cape Town — A SUPER FRELON helicopter fighting a veld fire in Table Mountain National Park in gale-force winds burst into flames after the pilot had to make an emergency landing near the Silvermine dam yesterday.
All four occupants escaped with minor burns and bruising, while one of them broke an ankle.

Original_Search_Title: Taking Help to the Grassroots
Headline: Uganda: Taking Help to the Grassroots
Body_Text:
Once upon a time, prosperity flourished in Pallisa. The district was known for producing some of the best cotton in the region and probably in the world.
"We lived in harmony with cotton, we thrived on cotton.

Original_Search_Title: We'll Continue to Campaign Till Polls are Cancelled - ACE
Headline: Nigeria: We'll Continue to Campaign Till Polls are Cancelled - ACE
Body_Text:
Emma Ezeazu is chairman of the Alliance for Credible Elections (ACE), one of the civil society organisations urging for the cancellation of the April polls. He says in this interview that campaigns would only stop when the elections are nullified and credible ones are held.
Weekly Trust: What further action is the Alliance for Credible Elections taking concerning the April polls?

Original_Search_Title: A Group of Butchers in Obudu.
Headline: Nigeria: A Group of Butchers in Obudu.
Body_Text:
Obudu — A famous hunter from Gobir hunted Elephants around Obudu, and he had a magical jacket which made him invisible while on the hunt. Then people used to sing'Baba Akwashe mai-rigar-layu, 'owner of the magical jacket.' Today, his offspring have Obudu wives, and they transport Bananas to the north, rather than Elephant meat or Tusks.
They are the Hausas of Obudu, and when you ask after their actual roots, they admit that today they feel out of place whenever they have to travel up north. To them hilly Obudu with its dual face of charming mists and gentle sunshine, is home. Obudu is Gida, (home) they say. When they speak so joyfully about Obudu, one can sense an umbilical bond with the Obudu area, and this is much deeper than any biological link.

Original_Search_Title: Leaders Must Seek Rose of Truth in This Thorny Tangle of Names
Headline: South Africa: Leaders Must Seek Rose of Truth in This Thorny Tangle of Names
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — WILLIAM Shakespeare, with his deep insight into human nature, would have made a good political psychologist had he been here to see what is increasingly becoming a silly season in South African politics.
He would have regaled us with psychoanalytical tales of cackling witches cajoling the Macbeths of the ruling party's leadership race to have a go at the throne. He would have spoken of the ambitious flatterers waiting to plunge their knives into the back of Caesar. We would have been enthralled by stories of two-faced Iagos and of a Hamlet oscillating between feigning madness and engaging in acts of political insanity.

Original_Search_Title: Entertainment News
Headline: Uganda: Entertainment News
Body_Text:
These days, no Ugandan nightclub or radio station is complete without playing P-Square's songs Temptations and Bizzy Body.
The identical Nigerian twin brothers Paul and Peter Okoye (P-Square) will perform live in Kampala and Entebbe this weekend.

Original_Search_Title: Legal Questions Over  Plan to Give Away Mabira Forest
Headline: Uganda: Legal Questions Over  Plan to Give Away Mabira Forest
Body_Text:
Kampala — Uganda has been awash with land giveaway wrangles, all in the name of promoting investment. That is no problem but the manner in which this strategy is designed and implemented casts a dark shadow and has caused a stir.
The International Water Law Project of the United Nations International Law Commission calls for an international right to the equitable and reasonable utilisation of transboundary water resources whether directly as open water bodies or indirectly as in aquifer systems that discharge water into a transboundary water source.

Original_Search_Title: City Road Reserves Abused
Headline: Uganda: City Road Reserves Abused
Body_Text:
A road reserve is space reserved by the roadside to accommodate at a later stage, aspects like underground telephone cables, water pipes, drainage channels and pedestrian walkways.
At a later stage, the road reserve can be used for expansion of the road when traffic density increases and the road cannot handle the capacity anymore.

Original_Search_Title: Kenyan Facility Frustrates Athlete
Headline: Botswana: Kenyan Facility Frustrates Athlete
Body_Text:
He is in his second year at an international training centre in Kenya but 800m runner, Onalenna Oabona says what was supposed to be a blessing has turned into a disaster. Since joining the International Amateur Athletics Federations (IAAF) facility last year, the track star says his form has slumped because of the poor conditions.
He was speaking to Mmegi Sport over the weekend after winning the 800m at the African Permit Meeting in Gaborone. He said the authorities in charge of his training programme in Kenya, have not afforded him opportunities to compete.

Original_Search_Title: Bagaggawale Funds Hit With Procedural Hiccup
Headline: Uganda: Bagaggawale Funds Hit With Procedural Hiccup
Body_Text:
Kampala — Recently, parliament declined to approve the Shs9 billion proposed to bankroll the "Prosperity for All" plan and demanded for a clear policy to guide utilisation of the fund
The prospect of a wealthy future for many whose hopes have been raised by the promise of the tongue twisting Bonna Bagaggawale scheme could turn out as twisted.

Original_Search_Title: Between Realism And Fatalism
Headline: Nigeria: Between Realism And Fatalism
Body_Text:
There was no compulsion to assuage the feelings of the greater majority of Nigerians who felt betrayed by INEC's midwifery of the polls, which left many among us wishing they had a parallel country to escape to. From his arrogant demeanor and choice of words, there was even a lesser inclination to assuage the feelings of mass frustration in the land, let alone the urge to reach out to millions who were crudely disenfranchised by the first, the non-arrival of ballot papers which were left marooned in a South African warehouse; crass incompetence in the management of logistics for the polls; and the most brazen collusion with security agents to perpetrate the infamy we experienced last April.
All Nigerians ever demanded from INEC was a level playing field and the freedom of choice, the very pillars of any enduring democracy; but even at that the agency according to local and international observers, was a woeful failure. It failed in its openly partisan conduct even before the polls. Instead of dedicating all its energies to tackle the logistic nightmare of preparing for the elections in a nation of over 140 million people, it spent greater time in the law courts trying to get candidates opposed to the ruling party disqualified.

Original_Search_Title: A Matter of Honour
Headline: Kenya: A Matter of Honour
Body_Text:
Nairobi — AFTER FOUR DECADES OF procrastination, Kenya has finally woken up to recognise its heroes and heroines. The government through the Ministry of National Heritage, has constituted a national task force - currently going round the country - to seek the views of Kenyans on who they consider their heroes and heroines and why.
The task force is also required to develop models for the construction of the heroes square.

Original_Search_Title: Pack of Formidable Aspirants Out to Wrest Seat From Minister Githae
Headline: Kenya: Pack of Formidable Aspirants Out to Wrest Seat From Minister Githae
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ndia normally witnesses low-key campaigns, compared to the other three constituencies in Kirinyaga district.
The giants who used to make the area an arena for bruising political battles ended up being in Kerugoya/Kutus, which was carved from the larger Ndia constituency before the 1997 polls.

Original_Search_Title: Showdown Looms in Kuria District
Headline: Kenya: Showdown Looms in Kuria District
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Assistant minister Wilfred Machage will have a tough time defending the Kuria seat on a Narc Kenya ticket, as the tide seems to favour ODM Kenya.
But party matters occasionally play second fiddle to clan considerations. Good interaction with the four major clans - Abakira, Abagumbe, Abairege and Abanyabasi - is a big boost to any aspirant. All the clans have candidates this time round. Kuria was a Kanu stronghold until 2002, when the Narc euphoria swept aside Mr John Magaiwa (Kanu candidate).

Original_Search_Title: Raila Launches His Vision for Country As President
Headline: Kenya: Raila Launches His Vision for Country As President
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In trailblazing style Lang'ata MP, Mr Raila Odinga, launches his presidential bid in what is seen as making a statement.
