All is set for what is expected to be a groundbreaking event where Lang'ata MP, Mr Raila Odinga will present his manifesto and vision at the Kenyatta International Conference Centre, on Sunday.

Original_Search_Title: Most Detained Man Has a Chequered Political Career
Headline: Kenya: Most Detained Man Has a Chequered Political Career
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As he launches his presidential campaign and vision in Nairobi today, Mr Raila Odinga expects among his invited guests to include President Kibaki, whom he helped get power, as well as former President Moi, the man whose planned rule-by-proxy he helped scuttle in 2002.
Following the failed August 1, 1982 coup staged by elements of the Kenya Air Force, Mr Odinga was charged with treason in connection with the coup, but the trial was terminated and he was detained for six years until 1988 when President Moi pardoned him.

Original_Search_Title: Raila Launches His Vision for Kenya as President
Headline: Kenya: Raila Launches His Vision for Kenya as President
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In trailblazing style Lang'ata MP, Mr Raila Odinga, launches his presidential bid in what is seen as making a statement, writes Dennis Onyango.
All is set for what is expected to be a groundbreaking event where Lang'ata MP, Mr Raila Odinga will present his manifesto and vision at the Kenyatta International Conference Centre, on Sunday.

Original_Search_Title: Animal Friends in Mombasa's Haller Park
Headline: Uganda: Animal Friends in Mombasa's Haller Park
Body_Text:
They say the world is a book and those who do not travel read only one page. I read another of my pages recently when I visited the coastal town of Mombasa. After two flights to Kenyatta International Airport in Nairobi and later on to Moi International Airport in Mombasa, jetlag started wearing down on me.
Lucky for me, there was a surprise in the bag. Together with a group of journalists from Uganda, we were to shake off the fatigue at the splendid Sarova Whitesands Beach Resort and Spa.

Original_Search_Title: Cobbler Regrets 'The Cut'
Headline: Kenya: Cobbler Regrets 'The Cut'
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The cobbler who chopped off his genitals in Ikolomani division, Kakamega North District, now regrets his action.
Mr Joseph Ingongo, 45, blamed the devil for his action. "I see the devil's hand in the act. There is nobody else to blame," he said.

Original_Search_Title: Who Will Be Delta's Next Speaker?
Headline: Nigeria: Who Will Be Delta's Next Speaker?
Body_Text:
Lagos — With the conclusion of repeat elections and the subsequent setting up of transition committees by the governor-elect, Dr. Emmanuel Uduaghan on the executive sphere, attention shifts to the legislature where plans are afoot to determine who becomes the next speaker of Delta State. Kingsley Nwezeh x-rays the factors at play
With the consummation of transition processes across the polity gradually winding up, part of the issues at play in Delta State 's political firmament is the issue of who becomes the next speaker of the State House of Assembly.

Original_Search_Title: Mukono Moves to Become Uganda's Leading Food Village
Headline: Uganda: Mukono Moves to Become Uganda's Leading Food Village
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE leadership of Mukono district has designed a development project which will necessitate each village to act as a production centre for a specific product.
Speaking toThe New Vision recently, the district chairman, Francis Lukooya, revealed that although they are still lobbying for more financial support from various donors, the district has already earmarked sh600m to implement the project. The funds have been generated from the local revenue and the unconditional grant.

Original_Search_Title: Gold Fever Infects Young And Old
Headline: Mozambique: Gold Fever Infects Young And Old
Body_Text:
Manica — Three children living outside Manica town in western Mozambique, bordering Zimbabwe, spent the Easter holidays digging a hole. Manica Province is the scene of a gold rush, and has all the problems associated with resource exploitation in a desperately poor country: dangerous working conditions, environmental degradation and violence.
Taking turns with a blunt-edged shovel, the young work crew excavated a shaft as wide as a coffin and about five metres deep. After a week of labour, the trio had collected gold flakes worth about US$2. They need to come up with about US$8 each to buy school uniforms, said Josefa Joao, 13, oldest of the three.

Original_Search_Title: Police Moved From District
Headline: Kenya: Police Moved From District
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Police officers in crime-prone Kiambu District have been moved in the fight against the Mungiki sect.
More than 250 regular and Administration police officers as well as their CID counterparts have been transferred from the area to other parts of the country.

Original_Search_Title: Don't Defend Mungiki, PCs Warn Politicians
Headline: Kenya: Don't Defend Mungiki, PCs Warn Politicians
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government has warned politicians against defending the outlawed Mungiki sect.
Nairobi and Central PCs also alluded that some politicians could be summoned to make statements on what they know of the sect.

Original_Search_Title: Politicians Blamed Over Mungiki Sect Menace
Headline: Kenya: Politicians Blamed Over Mungiki Sect Menace
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Politicians are frustrating the fight against Mungiki members, a top security meeting in Nairobi was told yesterday.
The discussion, attended by police chiefs, intelligence officers and Provincial Administration officials from Nairobi and Central provinces, called for the support of political leaders to win the fight against the outlawed group.

Original_Search_Title: Access to Clean Water Critical to Nepad Development Goals
Headline: Tanzania: Access to Clean Water Critical to Nepad Development Goals
Body_Text:
United Nations — No one in Lusala needs to walk more than 400 metres in search of water anymore. Fresh water gushes from taps at 11 drawing-points right within the Tanzanian community. For years, shortages sent women and children, the main collectors, several kilometres away each day. The drudgery was worsened by the hard-rock terrain they had to climb carrying heavy pots back to their hilltop village, located about 700 kilometres southwest of Dar es Salaam, the Tanzanian capital.
"Life is much better now that I have clean water near my house," Elizabeth Mtweve, a villager and mother of four, told Africa Renewal. "I don't walk all day in the heat to find water. In three to five minutes you fill your bucket by turning a tap. The water project has saved every woman in Lusala a lot of hardship and time."

Original_Search_Title: Government is Fanning Skirmishes, Says ODM-K
Headline: Kenya: Government is Fanning Skirmishes, Says ODM-K
Body_Text:
Nairobi — ODM-Kenya claims the Government is fanning clashes as a strategy to postpone the General Elections.
The party secretary general Prof Anyang Nyong'o said the current incidents in Mt Elgon, Kiambu and the Coast Province were deliberate tactics by the Government to get an excuse to cancel the polls due in December.

Original_Search_Title: War On Sect Robs Officer's Family of Bread Winner
Headline: Kenya: War On Sect Robs Officer's Family of Bread Winner
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Relatives of a police officer who was killed during a crackdown on Mungiki last week are now anxious over the fate of his immediate family for which he was the bread winner.
They want to know if the Government will compensate them for the death of Mr Jimmy Kabue Gitahi.

Original_Search_Title: Man Burns Child Over Shs300
Headline: Uganda: Man Burns Child Over Shs300
Body_Text:
Mbale — Police in Mbale are hunting a man who allegedly burnt his nephew's hand for stealing Shs300. According to police, Mr Jamada Bukuwa committed the crime two months ago in Bumbobi. He allegedly locked the child in a house under instruction not to tell anybody.
The victim, Karim Masaba, 8, a pupil at Bumbobi Primary School told Daily Monitor last week he requested for some money from his uncle, Mr Bukuwa, to buy a book and a pen but that he merely kept quiet.

Original_Search_Title: Wakaman - Where Do We Go From Here?
Headline: Nigeria: Wakaman - Where Do We Go From Here?
Body_Text:
So much has been written about the gargantuan sham that was passed on as elections in the past two weeks that writing about it pales into such symbolic insignificance it looks like adding a teaspoon to the ocean. The media, the opposition and indeed the raped people of Nigeria have been numbed into soporific silence by the way the entire process was scripted, acted and directed by Maurice Iwu, a man to which Nigeria reposed the confidence of midwifing the first attempt at civilian-to-civilian transition but who delivered nothing but a monumental fraud and shamefacedly faced the nation with an evil grin to say he has given himself a pass mark.
Iwu has scored an ace - in the history of examinations, this professor of pharmacology has shown examiners that it is possible to teach yourself, write your own questions, sit for your own examinations at a venue chosen by you, mark your own script and score yourself an 'A'. Never in the history of any examination has that been done, so in effect, Nigeria has scored another ace. What is painful about this is that many of us had begun to think that Nigeria is turning its image as a nation of fraudsters around - but how can we look anyone in the face after all this sham and say that we are no fraudsters. Right in the full glare of international observers, Maurice Iwu and his 37 commissioners have dared the people and shown to the world that Nigeria is indeed a fraud.

Original_Search_Title: The Largest Fruits Market in West Africa
Headline: Nigeria: The Largest Fruits Market in West Africa
Body_Text:
Kano — The famous 'Yanlemo Fruits Market located along Zaria Road at the Southern part of Kano state is said to be the largest fruits market in West Africa. It came into existence in 1982 and currently occupies over 3000 hectares of land.
It is argued that fruits of different varieties worth over N20 million are sold every week in the market. Furthermore, it is said that more than 50 truck-loads of fruits arrive the market on a weekly basis from the South-west alone.

Original_Search_Title: Nnamani's Bitter Pills for Path of Honour
Headline: Nigeria: Nnamani's Bitter Pills for Path of Honour
Body_Text:
Lagos — The recent altercation between the office of the Senate President, Chief Ken Nnamani, and the Presidency has called for a sober reflection as the nation in a matter of days, transits into another four years of democratic governance.
It was very unfortunate that a simple and truthful comment by the Senate President on the real situation report on the governorship and State House of Assembly elections conducted in the country three weeks ago, was misinterpreted.

Original_Search_Title: Mungiki Burn Three Vehicles in Daring Dawn Attack
Headline: Kenya: Mungiki Burn Three Vehicles in Daring Dawn Attack
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Mungiki menace reared its ugly head again as gunmen suspected to belong to the sect burnt three public transport vehicles at a petrol station in Nairobi.
The arsonists wounded a night watchman who tried to raise an alarm.

Original_Search_Title: Suspected Sect Men Burn Five Matatus
Headline: Kenya: Suspected Sect Men Burn Five Matatus
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A gang suspected to be members of the outlawed Mungiki sect yesterday struck again, this time in the city against owners of public service vehicles opposed to their activities.
They slipped through several roadblocks and officers on foot patrol and drove to the city centre where they set five matatus ablaze.

Original_Search_Title: Odinga to Launch Presidential Bid
Headline: Kenya: Raila Lays the Road for 'Spectacular' Launch of Bid
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Lang'ata MP, Mr Raila Odinga, breaks fresh ground in his quest for State House next week when he formally launches his presidential bid.
The political grapevine is abuzz with accounts of his so-called well laid out plans for the day, during which he is expected to finally put away the mask of kingmaker.

Original_Search_Title: Unwanted Attention!
Headline: Uganda: Unwanted Attention!
Body_Text:
Kampala — THEY will steal touches whenever they can, sniff at you at every opportunity, con you wherever they meet you and abuse you if you protest. Some just stare at you without a word. Others will start with a name or an old line like "Your face looks familiar," before their hands weave a path to your thighs.
Gently, you pick the offending hand with two fingers and throw it back at its owner. Disgust is written all over your face, but the offending fellow just doesn't seem to get the message. It has been said that men have problems understanding women's hints, but all will agree that the act of throwing another's hands back at him is far from subtle.

Original_Search_Title: I Lost My Childhood At 12
Headline: Uganda: I Lost My Childhood At 12
Body_Text:
Kampala — Living without parents is very hard. I am 18 years old. I missed my childhood. I have been the head of my family in Kasojo/Muholi village in Fort Portal town for nearly nine years.
When my parents died in 1998, I was left with the responsibility of looking after my two brothers and sister because I was the eldest.

Original_Search_Title: Outlawed Sect Ambushes Commuter Vans
Headline: Kenya: Arsonists Set Vehicles Ablaze
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Suspected members of the outlawed Mungiki sect struck again, this time a dawn ambush against owners of public service vehicles opposed to their activities.
In a daring attack, entered the city centre, slipped past several roadblocks and police patrol officers and set five matatus (commuter vans) ablaze, three of them reduced to shells.

Original_Search_Title: Raila Stands for Meaningful Change
Headline: Kenya: Raila Stands for Meaningful Change
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A discussion on the contemporary Kenyan politics is not be complete without mentioning Lang'ata MP Raila Odinga's name. As a leading lawmaker his name draws positive and negative commentaries, showing that he attracts affection and rebellion at the same time. This can mean only one thing: that you cannot ignore him in the Kenyan politics.
A son of the father figure of opposition politics in Kenya, Jaramogi Oginga Odinga, the former Roads minister has continued his father's clamour for meaningful change in Kenya, but has cut a niche for himself that is separate from his father's tribulations and achievements.

Original_Search_Title: Malaria the Most Endemic Disease of the Rural Community - Dr. Patrick Chang
Headline: Gambia: Malaria the Most Endemic Disease of the Rural Community - Dr. Patrick Chang
Body_Text:
Dr Patrick Chang, Taiwanese Ambassador to The Gambia, has said that malaria, the most debilitating endemic disease of the rural community was eradicated in Taiwan, in the early 1960s.
According to him, there is no record to indictate when the disease came into existence in Taiwan, but it is reasonable to assume that Malaria had existed among the indegenous population for many centuries, perhaps since the arrival of the first settlers from the Western Pacific Islands.

Original_Search_Title: Slow Service At Under-Patronised Mokador
Headline: Zimbabwe: Slow Service At Under-Patronised Mokador
Body_Text:
IT'S a sad, sobering fact that often the less well patronised a restaurant is, the slower the service offered. Of course, the one's probably at least partially responsible for the other!
Nowadays, I visit Mokador once or twice a year, in order to update this column and also to witness how the march of time influences this once hugely popular outlet.

Original_Search_Title: Govt Will Not Negotiatie With Criminal Gangs
Headline: Kenya: Talk to Criminal Gangs? No Way, Says Munya
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Government will not negotiate with criminal gangs like Mungiki and Sabaot Land Defence Forces.
Defence Assistant minister Peter Munya said 328 suspects had already been arrested in connection with the Mungiki mayhem in Kiambu District alone and would soon be charged in court.

Original_Search_Title: Call for Kisima Submissions On
Headline: Uganda: Call for Kisima Submissions On
Body_Text:
The seventh edition of the Kisima Music Awards process kicked off last week with a call for musicians from the East African region to submit their works.
Kisima, Kenya's premier music awards seek to embrace a diversity of talent from the three East African countries of Uganda, Kenya and Tanzania.

Original_Search_Title: Captivated By a Gorilla
Headline: Uganda: Captivated By a Gorilla
Body_Text:
Kampala — WE always boast to the world about what we have in our backyard through such campaigns as the 'Gifted By Nature' on CNN. But how many Ugandans have seen the amazing natural features our country offers?
Yet we listen in apparent boredom as an excited tourist tells of his encounter with a mountain gorilla.

Original_Search_Title: Calm As Police Dislodge Gangs
Headline: Kenya: Calm As Police Dislodge Gangs
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Normal business resumed amid tension in Kiambu District's Banana area after police took control of bases previously occupied by Mungiki gangs.
Yesterday, officials from the town council maintained order at the main matatu stage and charged levies after members of the outlawed sect were dislodged.

Original_Search_Title: Bumpy Stretch for Opore And Other Ford-P MPs Should Nyachae Retire
Headline: Kenya: Bumpy Stretch for Opore And Other Ford-P MPs Should Nyachae Retire
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Like nearly all MPs in Kisii region, Bonchari representative Zebedeo Opore might approach the elections with Ford People's ticket safely in his hands.
But that might be as far as it goes. There are a host of challengers, particularly for ODM Kenya nomination. They feel they have an excellent chance of ending Ford People's domination of Gusiiland.

Original_Search_Title: If Not for the Courts
Headline: Nigeria: If Not for the Courts
Body_Text:
Lagos — HAD the courts of superior record in Nigeria not risen above the fray, the survival of democracy as a system of governance would have long collapsed. The reign of lawlessness and impunity, which began in 2003, if not much earlier was elevated to statecraft under President Olusegun Obasanjo.
The country not much better than most banana republics, an aphorism for failed states, even if she fancies herself the giant of Africa, was saved from sinking deeper into the mire by the courts.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Wants More Industrial Parks
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Wants More Industrial Parks
Body_Text:
Kampala — PRESIDENT Yoweri Museveni has directed the trade ministry to identify and demarcate more industrial parks.
"President Museveni has written a letter to me directing the ministry to identify industrial parks in order to establish them with the required infrastructure as a way of facilitating more investment," the industry state minister, Prof. Ephraim Kamuntu, said.

Original_Search_Title: Cement Reconciliation and Unity
Headline: Rwanda: Cement Reconciliation and Unity
Body_Text:
Kigali — As we go into the third week, commemorating, for the 13th year, the Genocide that decimated a 10th of the population, the memory remains fresh of Rwandans' blood that continued to flow, unchecked, down the beautiful hills and into the valleys, rivers and lakes of Rwanda and, even, across the borders.
For the following 70 days or so after April 7, 1994, the maniacal cries of Interahamwe and ex-FAR would continue to fill the land as they mobilised the populace and together hunted and hounded children (without sparing foetuses), women and men in a killing and raping orgy that shocked the civilised world speechless and left it numb and dazed.

Original_Search_Title: Policeman Dies in Fight with Gunmen
Headline: Kenya: Policeman Shot Dead in Fight With Mungiki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A police officer was shot dead in a clash with Mungiki gunmen in Kiambu District's Banana area when fresh chaos erupted yesterday.
One of the sect members was also gunned down as they fled a home in which they had been hiding when a team of police officers surrounded it at 7 am.

Original_Search_Title: Eight Killed in New Wave of Violence
Headline: Kenya: Eight Killed in New Orgy of Violence
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Emboldened by the apparent Government inability to dismantle it, the Mungiki sect has now transformed itself into an organised gang armed with AK-47 rifles, which they used to kill a police officer in another dawn of bloodletting in Kiambu.
Corporal James Gitahi was shot twice in the chest and hand as he, together with his squad members, pursued the attackers who had overran several homes in the neighbourhood of Banana Hill at about 7am. He was pronounced dead on arrival at the Nazareth Hospital.

Original_Search_Title: KCC Regrets Lack of Pre-Season Training
Headline: Uganda: KCC Regrets Lack of Pre-Season Training
Body_Text:
Kampala — KCC coach Jackson Mayanja has blamed the lack of pre-season training for the club's poor run in the second round of the soccer Super league. The club lost to Victor 2-1 on Sunday, their third defeat in four encounters of the second round that started three weeks ago.
They lost to Iganga and Heroes away and their only win was against their junior side City Lads at home.

Original_Search_Title: As I See It
Headline: Botswana: As I See It
Body_Text:
The Government is in a potential crisis Parliamentary democracy is based on the following four principles: Free and fair elections, freedom of the press, the rule of law and the separation of powers. Absence of any of these four principles puts government in jeopardy with a crisis potential.
After forty years of democratic rule in Botswana, it is time we interrogated the accolade from outside observers that we are a "shining example of democracy." Students of democracy agree that democracy is a process, not a finished product or a historic event. In that context no country in the world can be "a shining example of democracy," let alone Botswana. In that light, the accolade is false, delusive, perhaps mischievous because it can induce complacency.

Original_Search_Title: Narc-K Man Ditches Party
Headline: Kenya: Narc-K Man Ditches Party
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Magarini parliamentary seat aspirant Franco Esposito of Narc-Kenya who was among three people prevailed upon to stand down for a government-supported candidate in the by-election, has defected to another party.
His announcement yesterday came as the Electoral Commission cleared three candidates to take part in the May 14 by-election.

Original_Search_Title: $1.3m Lodge Changes Country Safaris
Headline: Rwanda: $1.3m Lodge Changes Country Safaris
Body_Text:
Tourists visiting Rwanda stand to enjoy the ultimate safari experience at a new eco-tourism lodge.
Holed in the northern part of Rwanda some 96km from Kigali city, the Ikoro Tented Camp in the middle of a eucalyptus forest will leave visitors to Rwanda spellbound.

Original_Search_Title: Iwu an Embarrassment to Academia - Ajayi
Headline: Nigeria: Iwu an Embarrassment to Academia - Ajayi
Body_Text:
Lagos — The April 14 Gubernatorial and State Houses of Assembly elections were trailed with controversy and outrage nationwide as there were widespread allegations of rigging and attendant violence. Many openly challenged and doubted the integrity and ability of the Independent National Electoral Commission, such that both the April 14 and 21 elections have posed very serious issues about their legitimacy.
There have been strident calls for their cancellation. This Day went out to seek the views of leading lawyers on the issues the elections have posed. He spoke with Dr. Konyinsola Ajayi SAN, former General Secretary of the Nigerian Bar Association Deacon Dele Adesina and constitutional lawyer Mr. Fred Agbaje.

Original_Search_Title: Succession Law - Can Local Women Bask in Glory?
Headline: Uganda: Succession Law - Can Local Women Bask in Glory?
Body_Text:
Kampala — Is it time for women to count this just another of their achievements?
BECKY was widowed at the tender age of 28. Her husband, Anthony, with whom she had one child died in a car crash, two years after their wedding.

Original_Search_Title: Was There a Jailbreak From CPS Kampala Last Week?
Headline: Uganda: Was There a Jailbreak From CPS Kampala Last Week?
Body_Text:
Last week we were reminded of the biblical stone that the builders refused which eventually became the head cornerstone.
A group of stick-wielding shabbily dressed youths, the type that are periodically arrested indiscriminately by the police for being idle and disorderly, "miraculously" emerged from the environs of the Central Police Station in Kampala, beat up protesters and "restored" law and order.

Original_Search_Title: Jinja Residents Eager to Have Bujagali Power Dam
Headline: Uganda: Jinja Residents Eager to Have Bujagali Power Dam
Body_Text:
Jinja — When he received news that he was among the families to vacate Kikubamutwe village along River Nile and pave way for the construction of the Bujagali Hydro Power Project, Thomas Musumba like many other affected thought that his future had hit a snag.
Mr Musumba, a peasant, could not imagine himself leaving his only home and an acre of land, the only asset he had depended to fend for his family.

Original_Search_Title: Tension in Kiambu As Another Man is Killed
Headline: Kenya: Tension in Kiambu As Another Man is Killed
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Tension is high in Githunguri, Kiambu, after a transport operator was shot dead by gunmen believed to be members of the outlawed Mungiki sect.
Jeremia Gitau Ng'ang'a, 56, was killed near his home on Saturday night by gunmen who had trailed him from a local shopping centre.

Original_Search_Title: Crooks Under Police Cover?
Headline: Kenya: Crooks Under Police Cover?
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Public transport operators and residents of Kiambu point the finger at police, accusing some officers of collaborating with Mungiki gangs to fleece businessmen
Crooked police officers have been linked to an audacious Mungiki-run extortion ring that unleashed terror of an unprecedented scale in Kiambu District last week, The Big Issue can now report.

Original_Search_Title: State Split Over War On Sect
Headline: Kenya: State Split Over War On Sect
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Mungiki is a proscribed sect, having been declared a dangerous society in 2002.
The sect was banned on March 15, 2002, alongside 17 others in a special issue of the Kenya Gazzette signed by the then Minister for State in charge of Internal Security, Mr Julius ole Sunkuli.

Original_Search_Title: Voters Quiz FDC's Musumba Over Husband
Headline: Uganda: Voters Quiz FDC's Musumba Over Husband
Body_Text:
Kampala — KAMULI LC5 candidate Proscovia Salaamu Musumba cannot be with her husband Isaac Musumba at her campaign rallies despite strong calls from supporters.
"Nobody will see me with Isaac at any political rally and that is the way it is," she told a campaign rally in Bugulumbya trading centre, about 12km from Kamuli town, on Saturday. She added that she was principled and would not move with her husband just for the sake of winning votes.Salaamu Musumba is the vice-president of the opposition Forum for Democratic Change.

Original_Search_Title: The Anatomy of Rigging
Headline: Nigeria: The Anatomy of Rigging
Body_Text:
We have our decorum to thank for the survival of the Nigerian economy. A serious gambling nation would be counting its losses if it had placed bets on April 14 and 21 elections. We all knew that it would be called an election for want of a better decoy and we also knew that it would have no element of fairness in it. And by God, were we proved right? Well, on one score, it was not an election; it was the coronation of a selected few. In such an exercise, fairness and freedom are two attributes that are absolutely unnecessary.
In all but two states where it is absolutely obvious that a torpedo of the people's will might plunge Nigeria into unparallel chaos - every other state won by the PDP cannot be said to have been 'won' by the dictionary definition of that word. But what do these pathological riggers care? Nothing! They ride roughshod on the people and trample on their aspirations the way an elephant trample on grass. In Lagos, ab initio, it was certain that any attempt to wrestle power from the Action Congress would lead to anarchy of monumental proportions, the like of which not even the mighty army would be able to curtail and the repercussion of which nobody, not even the marabous of Aso Rock can predict. So, AC was allowed to have its way in the gubernatorial elections.

Original_Search_Title: Twin Goals the Police Must Aim to Achieve
Headline: Kenya: Twin Goals the Police Must Aim to Achieve
Body_Text:
Nairobi — After the events of the last few weeks when ordinary citizens put their collective foot down, declared enough is enough and started fighting against the Mungiki after feeling abandoned by the police, it was bitterly ironic to note that once upon a time, the outlawed gang/quasi-religious sect by the name of Mungiki was referred to as a vigilante group.
It would appear that while the police have seemed hapless and quite paralysed, in effect being reduced to mere bystanders, the worm has turned and the once defenceless citizenry have risen against their oppressors. Indeed the hunter has become the hunted.

Original_Search_Title: Step Aside, Kamotho Tells Michuki Over Menace
Headline: Kenya: Step Aside, Kamotho Tells Michuki Over Menace
Body_Text:
Nairobi — An MP yesterday asked Internal Security minister John Michuki to step aside or ask President Kibaki to transfer him, accusing him of failing to deal decisively with the Mungiki menace.
Mathioya's Mr Joseph Kamotho reminded the minister that groups like the Mungiki had been used in other African countries to causes instability.

Original_Search_Title: Tide Turns Against Mungiki as Welling Public Anger is Let Out
Headline: Kenya: Tide Turns Against Mungiki as Welling Public Anger is Let Out
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Could this possibly be the beginning of a rebellion by Mungiki members who are tired of harassment?
Villagers and matatu operators have in the past three weeks protested against harassment by members of the outlawed sect who have for some time been a government unto themselves.

Original_Search_Title: Musila is Still the Man to Beat
Headline: Kenya: Musila is Still the Man to Beat
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Deputy House Speaker David Musila might seem almost certain to retain the Mwingi South parliamentary seat when voters go to the polls for the third time since creation of the constituency in 1997.
But his opponents will beg to differ, and a major battle is likely to be fought which will bring in elements of the national politics. Mr Musila has become a key pointman for Mwingi South MP Kalonzo Musyoka.

Original_Search_Title: Kalonzo's State House Bid Excites Mwingi
Headline: Kenya: Kalonzo's State House Bid Excites Mwingi
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Mwingi district was once considered a backwater in Ukambani politics. Not any more. With the excitement generated by the presidential candidacy of Mwingi North MP Stephen Kalonzo Musyoka, the district is firmly in the spotlight of the region's politics.
Mwingi eclipsed neighbouring Kitui, the base of Health minister Charity Ngilu, Mr Musyoka's main rival for Ukambani leadership, as well as Machakos and Makueni districts as the place to watch for political direction in the region.

Original_Search_Title: Stop Clashes, Says U.S. Envoy
Headline: Kenya: Stop Clashes, Says U.S. Envoy
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The United States Ambassador Michael Ranneberger has called for the end of ongoing clashes.
The ambassador said the clashes are not acceptable and should be ended.

Original_Search_Title: April 2007 - End of the Beginning Or Beginning of the End
Headline: Nigeria: April 2007 - End of the Beginning Or Beginning of the End
Body_Text:
Lagos — Nigeria-observers worldwide should wake up on April 15 to an altered political reality. The long simmering battle for control of the levers of power in Africa's most populous - and certainly one of the most troubled, yet vastly promising - nation states, would have come and gone. Starting from that same date, the events that may end up either saving the Fourth Republic, or possibly laying the grounds for a 5th Republic, would have been laid. The die is cast. The Rubicon we spoke of early last year, would have been crossed.
Now, let's be frank that in more ways than one, this may be Nigeria's first true test of open democracy since the military withdrew in 1998. That year too was in its own box as a traumatized nation sought to put an end to an ugly past riddled with gun-totting soldiers ever so ready to 'kill and go'. The despondency mixed with relief at the end of the long nightmare, had been such that the incumbent head of state, Gen. A. A. Abubakar and his circle, including General Ibrahim Babangida, had free rein in anointing Obasanjo fresh out of one of Abacha's gulags.

Original_Search_Title: Dutch Experts to Assist Small Scale Entrepreneurs
Headline: Tanzania: Dutch Experts to Assist Small Scale Entrepreneurs
Body_Text:
Arusha — 'Skills and technical assistance is better than financial support'
Dutch 'Senior experts,' through their so called PUM organization, have expressed interest in assisting local small and upcoming business enterprises, through providing them with 'on site' free technical support.

Original_Search_Title: Clashes - Bishops Offer to Mediate
Headline: Kenya: Clashes - Bishops Offer to Mediate
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Catholic Bishops have condemned the Government over the spiral of violence as ODM-Kenya says the clashes are related to the General Election.
The 27 bishops said they were willing to arbitrate between the opposing clans in Mt Elgon District.

Original_Search_Title: Caught in the Riots of Uganda
Headline: Uganda: Caught in the Riots of Uganda
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ms Manju Dhiri, a British tourist caught up in the violence that rocked Kampala, tells our writer about her ordeal. Picture by Rebecca Nduku
The terrified motorbike rider stared at the enraged mob that surged towards him.

Original_Search_Title: Clash of Class, Kitsch, Culture And Colour
Headline: Zimbabwe: Clash of Class, Kitsch, Culture And Colour
Body_Text:
UTANO Junction blasted onto the local hospitality scene over Easter, seemingly set to make a major impact on dining out in the capital.
Utano ("health" in Shona) is in Avondale where Art Café and Steak Out Restaurants were... but there the similarity ends.

Original_Search_Title: Once Hunger-Ravaged Villages Now Have Plenty of Food
Headline: Kenya: The Once Hunger-Ravaged Villages That Have Plenty of Food And Glory
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The lunch time bell rings, provoking a loud noise from pupils who rush out from their classes at Nyamninia Primary School in Yala Division within Siaya District.
All their faces radiate with joy because of one thing - their lunch is guaranteed. While in most primary day schools pupil would be rushing home for lunch, if any, those at Nyamninia are a privileged lot.

Original_Search_Title: Government Concedes Crisis is Threat
Headline: Kenya: Top Govt Officials Differ On Security As Michuki Admits Mt Elgon Crisis
Body_Text:
Nairobi — On a day the Government conceded the crisis in Mt Elgon posed a threat to national security, its top brass was once again caught reading from different scripts, sparking off confusion over exactly who is in charge.
The Defence minister, Mr Njenga Karume, Police Commissioner Maj-Gen Hussein Ali, Government Spokesman Dr Alfred Mutua and a Provincial Commissioner gave the impression that the Government was not speaking in one voice on the widespread insecurity in the country.

Original_Search_Title: Seven More Killed in Three Hotspots
Headline: Kenya: Seven More Killed As Fresh Fighting Erupts in Hotspots
Body_Text:
Bura — The country appears to be slowly slipping into a five-year cycle of violence that has become synonymous with General Elections, after seven people were killed within 24 hours in three hotspots.
In Bura, four people were killed in cold blood in a dawn attack that saw over 300 houses burnt as Tana River exploded - to join Mt Elgon and Mungiki ravaged Kiambu - as the Government threw in its might.

Original_Search_Title: Country Faces Worst Ever Food Crisis
Headline: Eswatini: Stoicism in the Face of the Worst Ever Food Crisis
Body_Text:
Mbabane — Squeezed between Swaziland's worst-ever food crisis and the world's highest HIV/AIDS prevalence rate, people are struggling to survive.
Abdoulaye Balde, Country Director for the World Food Programme (WFP) in Swaziland, reported to government officials this week that the signs indicated the worst food crisis in the small kingdom's modern history.

Original_Search_Title: Over 200 Chinese Job Seekers Stranded
Headline: Liberia: Over 200 Chinese Job Seekers Stranded
Body_Text:
Over 200 Chinese citizens who came to Liberia to seek job with a private Chinese investor are now stranded in the country.
According to information, these Chinese citizens came into the country by way of plane or ship and were promised that upon arrival to Liberia they would be given jobs.

Original_Search_Title: Death As Hunt for Mungiki Spreads
Headline: Kenya: Death As Hunt for Mungiki Spreads
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A man was yesterday slashed to death as the violence pitting Mungiki thugs against villagers and matatu operators in parts of Kiambu District continued for the second day.
Mr Samuel Mbugua Ngige, 27, was slashed with machetes by a gang of 10 men who attacked him as he walked from his home at Kiharo village near Banana Hill, shortly before mid-day.

Original_Search_Title: Blues Out to Avoid Hammers' Banana Peel
Headline: Nigeria: Blues Out to Avoid Hammers' Banana Peel
Body_Text:
Lagos — Fresh from booking his place in his first ever FA Cup final, the attention of Chelsea boss Jose Mourinho returns to the Premiership tonight with a very tricky away fixture at Upton Park for a showdown with West Ham United.
West Ham may just be a place above the bottom of the table in 19th spot, but a fortnight ago they hugged the limelight when they did what more illustrious and better placed teams than them have failed to do - end Arsenal's unbeaten run at their new ground.

Original_Search_Title: No to 'Religion' of Murder And Destruction
Headline: Nigeria: No to 'Religion' of Murder And Destruction
Body_Text:
It is a pity that some of my Muslim brothers have refused to learn any lesson. They have chosen to remain the same way they were centuries ago. In this age and time when people are thinking of how to invent things and come up with ideas that will make their lives better and the society a better place to dwell in, some misguided extremists still engage in unjustifiable killings and destructions in the name of religion. That is why anywhere in Nigeria or outside when non-Muslims see that you are attractive, they would appreciate and welcome you thinking that you are a Christian (to them, all good looking and responsible people are Christians while the opposite are always Muslims). They would love you more as soon as you say something and you are found to be of sound mind. But immediately you mention your name at their request and it sounds like Abdulrahman, Abdulazeez or Suleiman, they begin to change their minds; they begin to withdraw from you while whispering to one another that this one is a terrorist!
Before beating around the bush too much, I refer to the pathetic case of one Mrs Temiloluwa Oluwaseesin, a contract schoolteacher, housewife, and mother of two (one of whom is a ten-month-old baby) in Gombe State who was recently slaughtered by students who claim to be Muslims (Are they? We are coming to that).

Original_Search_Title: Confusion Breeds Difficult Customers
Headline: Botswana: Confusion Breeds Difficult Customers
Body_Text:
There are different types of customers out there. What kind of customer are you?
Most of the time when sales people are stuck in providing the service they should provide they label us 'difficult customers'. There is no difficult customer, but rather a customer with a different opinion and who will not necessarily agree with what you are presenting. As a sales person you need to prove clearly and beyond reasonable doubt that your product and service live up to your business values and goals. The difficult customer can be sorted out very fast. Let me give you a few tips:

Original_Search_Title: It's War On Mungiki
Headline: Kenya: It's War On Mungiki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Angry villagers joined by matatu drivers and touts yesterday burnt down several homes as they hunted down Mungiki suspects in Banana Hill, Kiambu District. Scores of people suffered minor injuries during the four-hour orgy of violence in the busy little township, about 20 kilometres from Nairobi.
Seven houses were torched. It was the second time in three weeks that matatu men were protesting against what they described as illegal levies by Mungiki sect members.

Original_Search_Title: Europe's Free Trade Offer is a Mixed Bag for SA
Headline: South Africa: Europe's Free Trade Offer is a Mixed Bag for SA
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — THE European Commission's offer last week to lift duties and quotas on all products from African, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) countries -- with phase-in periods for bananas and rice -- has cast the economic partnership agreements (EPAs) being negotiated with these countries in a rosy light.
SA will, however, not fully benefit from the generosity, with indications from the European Union (EU) that it would retain import duties on a number of products from SA. Even so, SA will obtain greater market access to European markets than it now enjoys. But goods are only part of the agenda and the EU offer has detracted somewhat from other, more contentious, issues.

Original_Search_Title: Earning More From Matooke
Headline: Uganda: Earning More From Matooke
Body_Text:
Kampala — The market is as busy as a beehive. Traders on bicycles loaded with five or more bunches of matooke arrive, vehicles that have just loaded speed off and others park in waiting. People enter hotels and shops for meals while others rest from the pool game in a tent next to the market.
This is the situation you will find each time you visit Ruti matooke market on the Mbarara -Kabale high way, about 7km off Mbarara town. Traders, who by 7.00a.m. have sold off their first carry, are by 4.00p.m. still making endless trips to the market from villages to supply the insatiable demand for matooke by the Kampala city traders whose vehicles come in and out until twilight.

Original_Search_Title: President Kagame Warns Local Leaders Over Survivors And Witnesses' Security
Headline: Rwanda: President Kagame Warns Local Leaders Over Survivors And Witnesses' Security
Body_Text:
Rwamagana — President Paul Kagame has said that the government will not tolerate leaders who are supposed to provide security to the Genocide survivors and Gacaca witnesses but instead leave them vulnerable and susceptible to the wrath of lawbreakers.
Addressing local leaders, religious leaders, business persons and opinion leaders from the Eastern Province at AVEGA Complex in Rwamagana, Friday, the President expressed dissatisfaction over leaders who do not impact change amongst the people they lead, to benefit all people they lead.

Original_Search_Title: Rough Ride for Ford-P Man As Nyachae's Influence Wanes
Headline: Kenya: Rough Ride for Ford-P Man As Nyachae's Influence Wanes
Body_Text:
Nairobi — If Ford People leader Simeon Nyachae retires from politics has he indicated last month, he will leave behind a platoon of political orphans.
Bobasi MP Stephen Manoti will be one of them.

Original_Search_Title: ODM Factor May Give Magara a Headstart in the Crowded Race
Headline: Kenya: ODM Factor May Give Magara a Headstart in the Crowded Race
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The political temperature in South Mugirango constituency is rising as various aspirants strategise to unseat the incumbent Mr Omingo Magara.
Mr Magara will be fighting tooth and nail to retain the seat for a third term.

Original_Search_Title: Why FDC's Musumba Lost Kamuli Polls
Headline: Uganda: Why FDC's Musumba Lost Kamuli Polls
Body_Text:
Kamuli — The new Kamuli LC5 chairman is Mr Stephen Mubiru who raised 46,322 votes to beat FDC woman Proscovia Salaamu Musumba who polled 44,322.
Two weeks before the elections, Musumba seemed to be in the driver's seat. Basoga had been having their grievances and wanted to punish the NRM. The President made several pledges during the previous election campaigns including reconstructing the Kyabazinga's palace at Igenge hill, reconstruction of Jinja central market, turning Jinja into an Export Processing Zone and providing the Busoga Kingdom with $1.5m to establish a call centre business in Jinja. But none of these has been fulfilled.

Original_Search_Title: Executive Presidency Protects Morality
Headline: Zimbabwe: Executive Presidency Protects Morality
Body_Text:
Harare — IN an earlier instalment, titled "Executive presidency protects national interests" published in The Herald (April 6 2007), this writer put forward the thesis that an executive president is the steward of a nation, responsible for the welfare of the nation.
It is the duty of the electorate to endorse to the position of executive president, a person who exhibits the qualities of a good steward.

Original_Search_Title: Murder in the City 'Sells Out' at Cineplex, New Simogerere Film Shooting
Headline: Uganda: Murder in the City 'Sells Out' at Cineplex, New Simogerere Film Shooting
Body_Text:
The producer of the controversial Ugandan film Murder in the City has claimed the film sold out and outshined Hollywood movies during its first week at Cineplex Cinema, Garden City last week. Ronnie Kyeyune said the film attracted all classes of people during the Easter weekend.
Cineplex Cinema Marketing Executive Natasha Epenu corroborated Kyeyune's claim, however, she stated, "Only Dream Girls did better than Murder in the City as far as last week's box office sales are concerned."

Original_Search_Title: Passionate About Coffee
Headline: Uganda: Passionate About Coffee
Body_Text:
Kampala — Steven Banya, the proprietor of Ban Cafe has a deep passion for coffee. Starting up the café, which is one of the longest surviving coffee shops in Kampala was God's making
Why Ban café?

Original_Search_Title: Ibadan Elders, Ladoja Back Ajimobi
Headline: Nigeria: Ibadan Elders, Ladoja Back Ajimobi
Body_Text:
Ibadan — Senator Isiaka Abiola Ajimobi, the All Nigeria Peoples Party (ANPP) governorship candidate in Oyo State was Tuesday in Ibadan unanimously endorsed by the Ibadan Elders Forum, a frontline socio-political group comprising eminent sons and daughters of Ibadanland, as the group openly canvassed for mass votes for the gubernatorial hopeful in Saturday's general elections.
Shortly after the adoption, members of the Ibadan Elders Forum rode in convoy to the Oyo State Government Secretariat, Agodi, where they paid a courtesy visit to the sitting Governor, Senator Rashidi Adewolu Ladoja, who similarly embraced the idea and gave it his blessing.

Original_Search_Title: ISO Airtime Diverted to Public Booths
Headline: Uganda: ISO Airtime Diverted to Public Booths
Body_Text:
A MAN who claims to be a hardworking but frustrated security operative has spoken out to expose what he calls corrupt tendencies within the Internal Security Organisation.
Robert Kwesiga Rwampororo's account, which has been independently verified, suggests that some intelligence officers have conspired to enrich themselves in the guise of state duty.

Original_Search_Title: Passionate About Coffee
Headline: Uganda: Passionate About Coffee
Body_Text:
Steven Banya, the proprietor of Ban Cafe has a deep passion for coffee. Starting up the café, which is one of the longest surviving coffee shops in Kampala was God's making
Why Ban café? I believe it was God that started this in the first place. As a believer, I do not believe in chance and coincidence. I believe it was God's idea. All I did was bring it to pass," he remarks, adding, "More so, I am not one who can stand a job whereby I have to wait anxiously for the end of the month hoping my employer will be kind enough to pay me."

Original_Search_Title: The Beauty of the Adowa Dance
Headline: Ghana: The Beauty of the Adowa Dance
Body_Text:
ANYTIME A treating doctor is requested to attend the funeral of a patient, it's an embarrassment of sorts, and an honour, all rolled into one. You take it, whichever way you prefer, but dishonoring the invitation is the bigger of the two evils. I choose the lesser, whenever the onus falls on me. You don't have to wait long, if you are in a rare field, such as mine, (and I would like to keep my prospective readers guessing). This one was about twenty miles away from Oseikrom and in that old city with plenty of Ashanti and Akan history, Efiduasi, (also written Afigyuase).
Owing to the fervid rate of road construction the last couple of years between Ejisu and Kumasi, weekend travel on that piece of road, - Kumasi to Ejisu could be a nightmare. So avoid it. My driver, born and bred in the Ashanti Region, had a hilarious surprise for me.

Original_Search_Title: Nation Records CFA 248 billion Trade Balance
Headline: Cameroon: Nation Records CFA 248 billion Trade Balance
Body_Text:
BEAC report says hikes in crude oil prices in the world market is responsible for the positive balance.
The first nine months of 2006 witnessed an interesting trade balance in Cameroon, a report published by the Bank of Central African States (BEAC), has said. According to the report updated on 26 March, 2007, the balance of trade stood at CFA 248 billion. The figures which were obtained from the National Institute of Statistics forecast, include oil revenue. "The excess can be explained by hikes in crude oil prices in the world market", the report said.

Original_Search_Title: Informal Exports Fetch $200m
Headline: Uganda: Informal Exports Fetch $200m
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE informal sector earned $200.3m (about sh350b) from exports during 2005, the Uganda Exports Promotions Board's executive director, Florence Kata, has said.
"An economic survey by the Uganda Bureau of Statistics, Uganda Revenue Authority and Bank of Uganda has indicated that Uganda earned sh350b from the sector out of the official export earnings of $810.9m (sh1,427b)," Kata said.

Original_Search_Title: Lutaaya Earns Sh10m Per Year From Pineapples
Headline: Uganda: Lutaaya Earns Sh10m Per Year From Pineapples
Body_Text:
Kampala — WHEN Francis Lutaaya, 60, retired from Prisons many years ago, it seemed all hope was lost. Now Lutaaya is all smiles as he recounts how his life has changed.
"Ten years ago, I decided to take on pineapple cultivation just to remain busy. Little did I know it was an income earner," he says.

Original_Search_Title: U.S. Sees Terrorists Under the Bed as People Die in Mogadishu
Headline: Somalia: U.S. Sees Terrorists Under the Bed as People Die in Mogadishu
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Given the iron fist with which the Ethiopian army put down the protests in Addis Ababa two years ago following disputed elections, it was perhaps only to be expected that it would be only more vicious in its latest campaign against alleged Islamist extremist strongholds in Mogadishu.
Up to 400 people have died, and over 100,000 have fled Mogadishu in what the International Committee of the Red Cross has described as the worst fighting for 15 years in the Somali capital.

Original_Search_Title: Where to Kapfupi?
Headline: Zimbabwe: Where to Kapfupi?
Body_Text:
Harare — He has vast talents -- a big voice, stage presence, humour and meaningful lyrics. Unlike Joseph Garakara of Idya Banana fame, Kapfupi (real name Fred Manjalima) knows how to handle his music.
Although he was a curtain-raiser yesterday afternoon at the Ghetto Fabulous family show held in the Harare Gardens, Kapfupi -- who, sadly -- cannot match his dancers, showed that given time and with more practice, his act could get close to that of the late Safirio "Mukadota" Madzikatire's antics.

Original_Search_Title: ODM May Pick Uhuru Or Kalonzo
Headline: Kenya: ODM May Pick Uhuru Or Kalonzo
Body_Text:
Nairobi — ODM Kenya may opt for either Kalonzo Musyoka or Uhuru Kenyatta to face President Kibaki in the December General Election.
The revelations came as controversy stocked the recent move by MPs allied to the party to form a council of elders, which would produce a formula to pick the ODM-K presidential candidate.

Original_Search_Title: The State vs. Fatou Jaw Manneh; I Wash My Hands!
Headline: Gambia: The State vs. Fatou Jaw Manneh; I Wash My Hands!
Body_Text:
Berlin — I was shocked when a former classmate of mine from Nusrat High School called me to ask if it is true that I have been "asked to testify in the trial of Fatou Jow Manneh!" I dismissed the insinuations as sheer nonsense with a remark that all those wishing to see discord between Fatou, The Gambian Government and myself should "go hang" if Robert Mugabe would permit me!
After cracking my brain later on, I rediscovered that some years back, the wind of fate made my career path cross with that of Fatou in an unceremonious way. I bear no grudge since both of us were in our formative years then. She was around 21 and I, 16 - the usual phase in human development in which young adults feel they can change the world. I subsequently sighted Fatou only twice from a distance and I therefore, see no reason why I should now be trigger-happy like a Wild West (cowboy) when she is in distress!

Original_Search_Title: Busitema Baboons Raid Taxis, Gardens
Headline: Uganda: Busitema Baboons Raid Taxis, Gardens
Body_Text:
Kampala — THEY raided the taxi for bananas and bread. Mission over, they retreated with their booty. The fact that the raiders were baboons and the victims human beings - passengers on a commuter taxi - is causing a lot of concern among residents of Busitema Sub-county in Busia District.
It was not a one-off. According to the locals, incidents of baboons raiding vehicles moving through their "territory" are becoming frequent.

Original_Search_Title: Sipi Falls - Nature's Beauty
Headline: Uganda: Sipi Falls - Nature's Beauty
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE interesting difference between Sipi and Murchison Falls, in my opinion, is that of the two Sipi is more accurately named. The thing about Murchison Falls is that the water there just doesn't fall. It fights, froths, and foams its way through the rocks.
At Sipi, however, the water really does fall. The flow never touches the cliff at all, but simply drops, in a lacy, diaphanous veil, to crash into the pool at bottom, creating a constant fine mist in the process. It's a beautiful, elegant ballerina, stretching top to toe a slender 100 metres; Murchison by comparison is a stocky, meaty beast, more awe-inspiring than mesmerising.

Original_Search_Title: World Artists' Campaign to Aid Slums Schools
Headline: Kenya: World Artists' Campaign to Aid Slums Schools
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Several schools in Nairobi's Kawangware and Kibera slums are to benefit from an initiative launched by artists in Kenya and abroad.
Joseph Kamau Njuguna who is popularly known as Katuni and whose recent works are being exhibited at the Rahimtulla Museum of Modern Art (Ramona) at Upper Hill in the city, spearheaded the campaign that raised Sh3 million.

Original_Search_Title: Revellers in for a Treat
Headline: Zimbabwe: Revellers in for a Treat
Body_Text:
Harare — LAST week was a wet weekend resulting in a number of shows being washed away.
If the rains spare us this weekend then fans are certainly going to have lots of fun when Cephas Mashakada, whose show was disrupted by a heavy downpour in Kadoma, teams up with Hosiah Chipanga at the Club Plaza de Castilla in Greencroft tonight in Harare.

Original_Search_Title: Clock Ticks for ODM
Headline: Kenya: Clock Ticks for ODM
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Moments after top ODM-Kenya leaders announced the party had retained the Orange symbol, the Electoral Commission of Kenya (ECK) cut short the party through a technicality.
"We have told them they cannot use the orange on the map of Kenya. We said they can take the orange, but alter it to look different from the other orange party called ODM," ECK deputy chairman, Mr Gabriel Mukele, clarified.

Original_Search_Title: Why I'm Not Over Ambitious - Atiku
Headline: Nigeria: Why I'm Not Over Ambitious - Atiku
Body_Text:
Lagos — My dear compatriots, I have decided to address you today on the recent sad and unfortunate developments in our polity which constitute not only a clear and present danger to the continued survival of our young democracy but pose a serious threat to peace and stability in Nigeria .
I want to first express my gratitude to all Nigerians for their sympathy and prayers over a minor domestic accident I had recently during which I injured my right knee. It is a sad commentary on the state of health-care delivery in this country that I had to travel all the way to the United Kingdom for treatment. The specialist who could have treated me in Abuja lacked the necessary equipment and materials to carry out the surgery and dressing. The delivery of a world-class quality health-care will surely be one of my priorities as President of Nigeria.

Original_Search_Title: Making Politicians Look Silly, Ridiculous
Headline: Uganda: Making Politicians Look Silly, Ridiculous
Body_Text:
To the visiting Kenyan Redykyulass comic trio, the power glitches that marred their highly billed Ugandan debut last Saturday represented yet another of the ridiculous situations the wacky threesome parodies.
"Power cuts are typical of banana republics," group member Tony Njuguna complained moments before he was due to walk up onto the stage and do his skit portraying flamboyant Kenyan televangelist Margaret Wanjiru. The other members, John Kiarie and Walter Mongare were still enroute from their hotel to Kyadondo Rugby Club when the generator went silent.

Original_Search_Title: Supermarkets Ease Your Easter Shopping
Headline: Uganda: Supermarkets Ease Your Easter Shopping
Body_Text:
Kampala — MANY people might be wondering where to buy genuine Easter products worth their money. Supermarkets in Kampala are stocked with a variety of items ranging from groceries, toiletries to garments.
Make use of the calm shopping atmosphere in the supermarkets and make a good choice. Millennium Supermarket at Kisementi, Kamwokya has varied items.

Original_Search_Title: Express Your Love with Gifts
Headline: Uganda: Express Your Love with Gifts
Body_Text:
Kampala — WHAT will you include in your Easter basket this year? Gift baskets provide an opportunity for you to select items that your spouse, children and friends will enjoy.
According to a source at Banana Boat, a leading gift store in Kampala, a gift basket is opened on Easter Sunday to reveal goodies for everyone in the home.

Original_Search_Title: World Music Tour On the Cards
Headline: Namibia: World Music Tour On the Cards
Body_Text:
Windhoek — A Namibian musician has his eyes solidly set on a world tour to boost local music on a global scale due to the current international impact and interest in the country's music around the world.
This creative and enterprising idea is the brainchild of musician and College of the Arts lecturer, Banana Shekupe, after his recent return from Los Angeles where he made solid contacts for such a music tour.

Original_Search_Title: ODM-K Officials And Supporters Storm ECK Offices
Headline: Kenya: ODM-K Officials And Supporters Storm ECK Offices
Body_Text:
Nairobi — ODM-Kenya officials and supporters stormed the Electoral Commission of Kenya (ECK) offices, demanding the registration of the party's Orange symbol, which is at the centre of an ownership row.
The ECK has up to Thursday to comply, the party said.

Original_Search_Title: Opening Up Western
Headline: Kenya: Opening Up Western
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The completion of road projects in Western Kenya this year will usher in a new visage to the economic development of a region whose potential largely remains untapped.
Five roads, whose construction is ongoing, will open up the area for domestic and international tourists, businessmen and the local communities by providing a faster means of transport to Kisumu and other outside markets.

Original_Search_Title: Squatter And Ethnic Issues to Shape the Race
Headline: Kenya: Squatter And Ethnic Issues to Shape the Race
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Taveta MP Dr Naomi Shaban would have to fight for her survival in the event that the big Kamba community decides to back their own candidate.
The constituency is one of the areas in Taita-Taveta District whose election outcome has been unpredictable since the departure of former MP Basil Criticos in 2001.

Original_Search_Title: Save Country From Tyranny
Headline: Nigeria: Save Country From Tyranny
Body_Text:
My dear compatriots, I have decided to address you today on the recent sad and unfortunate developments in our polity which constitute not only a clear and present danger to the continued survival of our young democracy but pose a serious threat to peace and stability in Nigeria .
I want to first express my gratitude to all Nigerians for their sympathy and prayers over a minor domestic accident I had recently during which I injured my right knee. It is a sad commentary on the state of health-care delivery in this country that I had to travel all the way to the United Kingdom for treatment. The specialist who could have treated me in Abuja lacked the necessary equipment and materials to carry out the surgery and dressing. The delivery of a world-class quality health-care will surely be one of my priorities as President of Nigeria.

Original_Search_Title: Nyachae the Man
Headline: Kenya: Nyachae the Man
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The inclusion of Mr Simeon Nyachae in Government on June 30, 2004, was perhaps one of the boldest moves President Kibaki made since taking power in December 2002.
With a stroke of the pen, the President expanded his Cabinet and embraced the Government of National Unity (GNU).

Original_Search_Title: National Drama Festival Kicks Off With a Bang in Nyeri
Headline: Kenya: National Drama Festival Kicks Off With a Bang in Nyeri
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The 48th edition of the Kenya Schools and Colleges National Drama festival kicked off with a bang Tuesday at Tumu Tumu Girls School in Nyeri.
The opening competition entries were full of colour, and creativity.

Original_Search_Title: Josamu - Active Till Death
Headline: Zimbabwe: Josamu - Active Till Death
Body_Text:
Harare — ONCE, music was not about money or fame but it was in the blood and a matter of life for the old music guard.
Making money, becoming famous, settling down and having a wife and children came along in life.

Original_Search_Title: PTDF Saga - The Ethnic Dimension
Headline: Nigeria: PTDF Saga - The Ethnic Dimension
Body_Text:
Lagos — The whiff of the controversial report of the Senator Umaru Tsauri's Senate Review Committee of the Petroleum Technology Development Fund (PTDF) has continued to dominate matters concerning the Upper House despite the fact that its members are currently on recess.Correspondent COSMAS EKPUNOBI examines the new twist in the case.
Senator Umaru Tsauri is the Chairman of the Senate committee on Business and Rules and by virtue of that is a major custodian of the senate's standing rules and convention.

Original_Search_Title: How Safe is That Mumbwa?
Headline: Uganda: How Safe is That Mumbwa?
Body_Text:
Kampala — AFTER an agonising labour experience during her first pregnancy, a friend introduced Fatuma to emumbwa. Her first labour was a gruesome two-day experience. However, with the mumbwa, labour during her three subsequent pregnancies took only up to four hours. Fatuma, a mother of four, says she took more than three mugs of mumbwa everyday.
But Stella, a 26-year-old Makerere University student, says despite having taken a mug of mumbwa everyday during her first pregnancy, she had a gruesome labour experience and subsequently a caesarean birth.

Original_Search_Title: Lots of Going Forward, Little Falling Down
Headline: South Africa: Lots of Going Forward, Little Falling Down
Body_Text:
Johannesburg — BUSINESS is like riding a bicycle, it is said: you have to keep going forward or you fall down. And going forward defines what Probike of Edenvale, sole and official representative of the world-famous UK Raleigh bicycle brand in SA and the rest of Africa, is all about.
In SA, Raleigh and Probike are almost synonymous with each other. The company arrived on the local scene in 1991 when two bicycle retailers, JH Slotar of Johannesburg and Cycle Centre of Port Elizabeth, merged to form Probike.

Original_Search_Title: The Endless Battles of Anisulowo
Headline: Nigeria: The Endless Battles of Anisulowo
Body_Text:
Abeokuta — Ogun State is highly reverred to be the home of prominent women of national and international status. The list of the amazons is as endless as those that are just coming on board.
They include Chief (Mrs.) Funmilayo Ransome Kuti who carved a niche as a woman activist, the great warrior and heroine of Egba history, Madam Efunroye Tinubu, late Chief (Mrs.) Bisoye Tejuosho, a renowned industrialist and one time Iyalode of Egbaland who was the mother of the reigning Osile of Oke Ona Egba, Oba (Dr.) Adedapo Tejuosho, Chief (Mrs.) Simbiat Abiola, Chief (Mrs.) Ebun Oyagbola, Chief (Mrs.) Titi Ajanaku and few others.

Original_Search_Title: Can Someone Listen to Ohanaeze?
Headline: Nigeria: Can Someone Listen to Ohanaeze?
Body_Text:
Lagos — THERE is the Igbo adage that says a man rejected by others should not reject himself. Another says that eternal vigilance is the price of liberty. It is very clear that all the political parties have had their primaries and anointed their flag bearers. Irrespective of what the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission, EFCC, or the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, will say, any changes in submissions cannot be said to represent the dictates of party faithful.
Again, it is irrelevant to listen to the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, presidential candidate, Governor Umar Musa Yar'Adua's claim that the South-East will get priority attention after the elections. Only a fool will take such promises, hook, line and sinker. Of course, the Igbo I know is nobody's fool.

Original_Search_Title: The Craze for Exotic Names
Headline: Uganda: The Craze for Exotic Names
Body_Text:
You will be surprised at the high affinity our fellow countrymen have for truly exotic names. Even where someone has a truly culturally rich name, the person would rather spice it up to make it sound foreign, as if this would enhance its value or that of his stature!
In a wide sweep of your hand, you will find some person once named Gidaguyi, now calling himself Gidds. A Kasapuri now called Kasa-son, a Segirinya now called Seggae (pronounced Sege), and a Mukwana now called Mukwana-son, Jjuko now called Jjuko-san! The list is endless. I refuse to be called Malebbs.

Original_Search_Title: How We'll Resist Atiku's Disqualification - Obi
Headline: Nigeria: How We'll Resist Atiku's Disqualification - Obi
Body_Text:
Lagos — SENATOR Ben Obi is the Vice-Presidential running mate to Atiku Abubakar. In this interview, Obi took a swipe at the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, for disqualifying Vice-President Abubakar arguing that the commission has no power to do so.
The lawmaker, nonetheless, stated that the Action Party would abide by the judgement of the law court if, it eventually disqualifies its presidential candidate even as he pointed out that the party would look for an alternative, as regards the issue.

Original_Search_Title: Where a Dollar Won't Buy You a Banana
Headline: Zimbabwe: Where a Dollar Won't Buy You a Banana
Body_Text:
Harare — Poverty levels around the world are usually measured against the value of one United States (US) dollar a day, but calculating that value in Zimbabwe can be a nightmare; one US dollar is officially pegged at Zim$250, but in the parallel market it will buy Zim$25,000.
If you convert money at the official rate, then Zim$250 can only buy a sweet, which cannot sustain life anywhere; in Zimbabwe it is not even enough to buy a banana, which costs Zim$600 (US$0.02 at the parallel market exchange rate), nor is it enough for a one-way ticket to town by minibus-taxi, which can set you back by Zim$5,000 (US$0.20).

Original_Search_Title: Banned Sect Has Defied Several Efforts to Fight It
Headline: Kenya: Banned Sect Has Defied Several Efforts to Fight It
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Mungiki is a proscribed sect, but its gangs have remained active, unleashing terror on Kenyans for more than a decade.
Its followers are inducted into the outfit on oath, and are responsible for crude and organised crimes especially in Nairobi and major towns.

Original_Search_Title: DUJIS: MP 'For Life' to Come Up Against a Challenger He Least Expected
Headline: Kenya: DUJIS: MP 'For Life' to Come Up Against a Challenger He Least Expected
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Dujis MP Hussein Maalim, who has earned the distinction of "MP for life", faces the acid test of being challenged for the seat by youthful close relatives.
For 28 years, he has dominated the politics of Dujis, and by extension the entire Garissa District. He was nominated MP in 1979, but was elected in the 1983 snap elections and subsequently appointed to the Cabinet.

Original_Search_Title: Voi - Minister in Dilemma Over Loyalty
Headline: Kenya: Voi - Minister in Dilemma Over Loyalty
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Voi MP Boniface Mghanga is a politician in a big dilemma. He is torn between party loyalty and President Kibaki's Government of National Unity.
The former civil servant, who is best remembered for heading Muungano choir that was popular under retired President Moi, is an assistant minister for Public Service in the Office of the President.

Original_Search_Title: Moi Factor to Play Key Role in Determining the Winner
Headline: Kenya: Moi Factor to Play Key Role in Determining the Winner
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Rongai is one of the constituencies being seen as the "Moi family affair".
Mrs Alicen Chelaite, a self made politician, who became mayor of Nakuru then MP for Rongai, and went on to become an assistant minister, grew up in retired President Daniel arap Moi's house in the 1950s.

Original_Search_Title: Foreigners Held in Drug Bust
Headline: Namibia: Foreigners Held in Drug Bust
Body_Text:
POLICE say they nabbed two Zimbabwean nationals with 6,21 kg of dagga and 110 grams of cocaine, with a combined value of N$118 500, in a sting operation on Thursday.
The woman is reportedly a known dealer.

Original_Search_Title: Energy Saving Stoves to Reduce On Rural Women Rape Cases
Headline: Rwanda: Energy Saving Stoves to Reduce On Rural Women Rape Cases
Body_Text:
Kigali — The Rwanda Environment Management Authority (REMA) has once again issued a stern warning against defiant users of polythene bags, contrary to last year government's directive.
By law, the importation and use of polythene bags in Rwanda is prohibited. And, according to the law, anyone found in possession of a polythene bag may be sentenced to one to five years' imprisonment or pay a fine ranging from Frw5 million to Frw50 million.

Original_Search_Title: Oil Communities Ask . . . When Will This Poverty End?
Headline: Nigeria: Oil Communities Ask . . . When Will This Poverty End?
Body_Text:
Lagos — A visit to the remote communities of the Niger Delta region, where oil exploration takes place in Nigeria, exposes the neglect of the proverbial goose that lays the golden eggs. Devoid of modern indices of development, the communities say they are not happy with the way government and the oil corporations have abandoned them.
Godwin Haruna writes

Original_Search_Title: Anything Worth Celebrating About Local Theatre At 50
Headline: Uganda: Anything Worth Celebrating About Local Theatre At 50
Body_Text:
Tuesday March 27 is World Theatre Day. In Uganda, the occasion will mark a belated celebration of theatre's golden anniversary. The commemoration is tagged to December 18, 1956, the day former British Governor Sir Andrew Cohen laid the foundation stone of the National Theatre, local theatre's most visible structure.
There are however mixed feelings among the theatre community in the run up to the grand day on whether there is much to celebrate. The recent past has seen theatres like Pride Theatre repossessed, dwindling audiences and competition from alternative entertainment like the UK's Premier League, cinema, TV soaps and even Nigerian movies.

Original_Search_Title: Love Letter to Mabira Forest
Headline: Uganda: Love Letter to Mabira Forest
Body_Text:
Kampala — LAST October, Matthias Mugisha and Gerald Tenywa became the first journalists to trek through the 312sqkm Mabira Forest. Accompanied by a team of forest officials, they fell in love with the forest during the 12-night adventure. Last week, they were devastated at the news that Cabinet had decided to degazette part of the forest to give way to sugarcane growing. Here, they eulogise Mabira.
Dear Mabira,

Original_Search_Title: Woman of My Life - the Inside Story
Headline: Uganda: Woman of My Life - the Inside Story
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE popular soap opera ended last week. Did it end on the right note? Elizabeth Agiro analyses.
SOMEWHERE in Latin America, a love story breaks. It's a blend of comedy, tragedy, suspense and romance. You could probably add loads of stupidity and a knack for dwelling on silly issues for long periods.

Original_Search_Title: Coalition Offers the Best Option of Dealing With Ethnic Rivalries
Headline: Kenya: Coalition Offers the Best Option of Dealing With Ethnic Rivalries
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Just before the 2002 General Election, a lot of Kenyans predicted that Kanu was headed for defeat by the Narc. At the same time many observed that the composition of Narc was suspect; it had a mix of people who could not really work together.
On December 30, 2002, Mr Mwai Kibaki was sworn in as the third President of Kenya. In January 2003, he formed his Cabinet. It was an impressive group, comprising professionals and experienced public and private executives. I actually thought we had come of age and we would see a working cabinet in a working nation, as President Kibaki would put it.

Original_Search_Title: 100 Years And Counting
Headline: Namibia: 100 Years And Counting
Body_Text:
"WELCOME to the Kingdom of Etosha," says a beaming Sunday Nelenge, breaking out into jovial laughter as he registers the surprise on the faces of a new group of tourists arriving at Okaukuejo.
"The park is an animal kingdom of course and that kingdom celebrates 100 years on March 22 after being officially proclaimed a nature reserve by Germany's colonial governor Friedrich von Lindequist," the resort manager explains.

Original_Search_Title: We'll Go All the Way to Resist Atiku's Disqualification - Obi
Headline: Nigeria: We'll Go All the Way to Resist Atiku's Disqualification - Obi
Body_Text:
Lagos — SENATOR Ben Obi is the Vice-Presidential running mate to Atiku Abubakar. In this interview, Obi took a swipe at the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, for disqualifying Vice-President Abubakar arguing that the commission has no power to do so.
The lawmaker, nonetheless, stated that the Action Party would abide by the judgement of the law court if, it eventually disqualifies its presidential candidate even as he pointed out that the party would look for an alternative, as regards the issue.

Original_Search_Title: 'Hostages in Our Own Land'
Headline: Cote d'Ivoire: 'Hostages in Our Own Land'
Body_Text:
DuekouÃ© — Every morning at 8 a.m. Honoré Zon pedals his bicycle out to the village of Fengolo to work in his cocoa fields. As dusk approaches he knows it is time to leave when he hears the trucks.
"When I hear the noise of the vehicles of the United Nations soldiers I run to my bicycle and I follow them to Duekoué," said Zon. "Many other people do the same as me. That is how we're able to return to our homes without worrying."

Original_Search_Title: Food Shortage Looms for Disaster Victims
Headline: Mozambique: Food Shortage Looms for Disaster Victims
Body_Text:
Maputo — About 350,000 people, affected by flooding in the Zambezi valley in central Mozambique, and by Cyclone Favio, that hit the southern province of Inhambane in late February, may face a shortage of food in the near future.
According to a report in Thursday's issue of the Maputo daily "Noticias", the Mozambican government's relief agency, the National Disasters Management Institute (INGC), is running out of food in its warehouses.

Original_Search_Title: PTDF - Between Nnamani's Senate And Obasanjo's Presidency
Headline: Nigeria: PTDF - Between Nnamani's Senate And Obasanjo's Presidency
Body_Text:
Lagos — Kenechukwu Nnamani, President of the Nigerian Senate, comes from the part of the country-Igbo land in the Southeast zone- where dancing is a part of the people's very rich culture. He is a red cap chief by virtue of his being the Onwa (the moon) of Enugu; and he is therefore very conversant with the art of dancing. He may not be able to do the king of dancing by the Atilogu dancers. But he seems to be practically versed in some other kinds of dance on some other terrain: politics. This is why he is dancing to the melodious rhythm of his soul as the entire country is regaled by the odious tales of sleaze, aiding and abetting of the criminal offences that wafted from the windmills of the Petroleum Technology Development Fund (PTDF). Far away from the ordinary eyes, the dance may become more agitated Tuesday, this week, if the Review Committee on PTD is able to submit its report to the Senate.
Nnamani is happy with the turn of events that has virtually vitiated the moral base of the nation's presidency. And, his happiness is understandable: the hands of the key operators of the Presidency that worked very hard and quietly, though unsuccessfully, behind the scene in a bid to stop his emergence as Senate President have been caught in the cookie jar of crime. The PTDF jar is indeed a "honey pot" and very tempting. The high and the mighty in the Presidency cannot just resist the allure of it. This is why the Fund is a veritable and potential Achilles' heel of its custodians. It is not only the PTDF that has become a slush fund at the disposal of the Presidency. There are others. This is why the Presidency is a powerful long distance runner in matters of conflicts between it and the National Assembly. Ask the late former Senate President, Dr. Chuba Okadigbo who was removed from office in the aftermath of the executive sponsored plot that allegedly gulped millions of naira in execution; and, it would be clear why he went eventually went down after all the political razzmatazz and braggadocio.

Original_Search_Title: Govt to Crack Whip On Polythene Use
Headline: Rwanda: Govt to Crack Whip On Polythene Use
Body_Text:
Kigali — Defiant users of polythene bags contrary to last year's government policy banning their use may face it rough, the Rwanda Environment Management Authority (REMA) has warned.
