Kampala — OVER 20 businesswomen, who were ferrying goods to South Sudan capital, Juba, were involved in an accident at the Uganda-Sudan border on Saturday.
Their truck overturned near Oraba immigration and custom offices in Koboko district.

Original_Search_Title: Jilted Ex-Husband Speaks of His Deep Love for Bishop Wanjiru
Headline: Kenya: Jilted Ex-Husband Speaks of His Deep Love for Bishop Wanjiru
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Barely a week after tele-evangelist Bishop Dr Margaret Wanjiru Kariuki introduced in her church a South African, Mr Samuel Matjeke, as her fiancée, a man from Gachie in Kiambu has now emerged to claim the self-confessed witch-turned preacher has been his estranged common-law wife.
Mr James Kamangu, Bishop Margaret Wanjiru's former husband.

Original_Search_Title: Ugandans Will One Day Gang Up Against This Greed
Headline: Ugandans Will One Day Gang Up Against This Greed
Body_Text:
Dear Tingasiga: I am in that zone once again, hovering between tears and laughter, a consequence of things I have seen and heard in recent weeks.
I was home over the Christmas/New Year's season, my unplanned trip occasioned by the death of the Hon John Bikangaga, whom we buried in Kabale on December 23, 2006.

Original_Search_Title: Is It Evil to Have Last Funeral Rites?
Headline: Uganda: Is It Evil to Have Last Funeral Rites?
Body_Text:
Kampala — DESPITE attempts by mainstream Christianity to harmonise religion and culture, controversy still surrounds the custom of performing last funeral rites.
Although the ceremony is not a universal one, those traditional societies which subscribe to it, accord it great importance. In the Ganda tradition, the main idea behind it is to pass on the dead person's family/social responsibility.

Original_Search_Title: Free From Booze - a Minister's Story
Headline: Uganda: Free From Booze - a Minister's Story
Body_Text:
Kampala — IT was not until he broke the destructive habits of smoking and drinking that Freddie Ruhindi, now a state minister, found the path to success.
My name is Freddie Ruhindi. I'm the State Minister for Justice and Constitutional Affairs and the Member of Parliament for Nakawa. There was a time in my life when I was in love with smoking and drinking alcohol.

Original_Search_Title: ECK - There Was Need for Consultations
Headline: Kenya: ECK - There Was Need for Consultations
Body_Text:
Nairobi — President Kibaki finally appointed new commissioners on Thursday night to fill vacancies at the Electoral Commission of Kenya, in line with his constitutional mandate.
Predictably, his move has drawn instant and sharp reactions from Opposition leaders, mainly on the basis that he did not consult in spirit of the gentleman's agreement crafted in 1997 under the auspices of the Inter-Party Parliamentary Group (IPPG).

Original_Search_Title: Uganda is Still Bananas
Headline: Uganda is Still Bananas
Body_Text:
Farce, comedy, tragedy, is the sure way to summarise 30 Years of Bananas by Alex Mukulu. It's a play written at a time when the republic was really 'going bananas' -thanks to its past leaders.
The lead character, Kalekeezi, is a Rwandese refugee in Uganda who has survived over five turmoil-clad regimes and whose satirical insight into what the 'Banana Republic' has endured and lost since independence raises many questions than can be answered.

Original_Search_Title: Rachel Kiwanuka On Her Upcoming Album
Headline: Uganda: Rachel Kiwanuka On Her Upcoming Album
Body_Text:
Rachel Kiwanuka is Ugandan Afro-pop singer Halima Namakula's daughter. She is a fixture on Uganda's social scene, a young TV personality and an up-and-coming musician who is evidently taking after her mum.
What have you resolved to do this year?

Original_Search_Title: Children Train in Buganda Culture
Headline: Uganda: Children Train in Buganda Culture
Body_Text:
Kampala — NABAGEREKA Slyvia Nagginda has asked parents to partner with teachers to instill good morals in children.
Nabagereka was on Tuesday opening a two-week peer training (Ekisakaate) at St. Agnes Nagalama Primary School in Mukono.

Original_Search_Title: Report on Politician's Murder Published
Headline: Uganda: The Real Kayiira Report
Body_Text:
Kampala — The New Vision got hold of the original Scotland Yard report on the murder of Dr. Andrew Kayiira leader of the then Uganda Freedom Movement on March 6,1987. The report, dated May 7, 1987, which was reported missing, sparking a lot of speculation in the past weeks, is reproduced on this website.
1. This report concerns enquiries into the murder of Dr. Andrew Lutakome Kayiira age 46 years, former Minister of Energy for the Ugandan Government who at the time of his death on March 6, 1987 was residing at Lukuli-Konge village, Kampala, Uganda, and the assistance given to the Uganda Police by Detective Chief Superintendent Thompson (Serious Crime Branch) and Detective Sergeant Sanderson (Laboratory Liaison Officer) at the direct request of President Museveni of Uganda.

Original_Search_Title: Why We Love Oprah And Mandela!
Headline: Zimbabwe: Why We Love Oprah And Mandela!
Body_Text:
Harare — MS Oprah Winfrey, America's queen of talk show hostesses and hosts has done it again, and she has come under heavy fire from opponents.
On January 4, 2007, Ms Winfrey opened a U$40 million school for black girls in South Africa. Led by ET (an entertainment network), a host of evil people descended on the sister like a tonne of bricks.

Original_Search_Title: What Are We Really Worth?
Headline: Zimbabwe: What Are We Really Worth?
Body_Text:
Harare — "MAGNA est veratus et prevelebit". This is a Latin phrase that can be translated into English as "Truth is mighty and will ever prevail".
There is no real economic "turnaround effort" in our country, no matter how much we may try to claim. We must begin to address the true economic problems -- or challenges, as we prefer to call them.

Original_Search_Title: Gombya Describes Kayiira Murderer
Headline: Uganda: Gombya Describes Kayiira Murderer
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE late Andrew Kayiira's former housemate and one of the last surviving witnesses to the murder, says in his most revealing testimony to date that the killer is a high ranking Muganda officer still serving in the Movement government.
Speaking almost 20 years after the assassination of the former rebel leader, Henry Gombya, 49, told Daily Monitor yesterday that the killer is a Brigadier which is a high office in the army. He says the officer lives in perpetual fear of being exposed.

Original_Search_Title: Batons, Tear Gas And Water-Cannons Good for Dissidents
Headline: Uganda: Batons, Tear Gas And Water-Cannons Good for Dissidents
Body_Text:
Twenty years ago a common saying among the bright young revolutionaries who had just seized power in this country was that a person holding a chuck of meat in one hand must remember to carry a spear in the other hand.
This peasant rendition of Niccolo Machiavelli's dictum that the first way for a ruler to lose his state is to neglect the art of war has stood many an African leader in good stead. "You are bound to meet misfortune if you are unarmed," writes Machiavelli. "It is unreasonable to expect that an armed man should obey one who is unarmed."

Original_Search_Title: Who is Kayiira?
Headline: Uganda: Who is Kayiira?
Body_Text:
Kampala — The mystery that has surrounded the murder of former rebel leader and former minister Andrew Lutakoome Kayiira for almost two decades is in the news. The opposition Democratic Party last week promised to release the report of Britain's Scotland Yard which investigated the murder, but this did not come to pass. But who was Kayiira and how did he die?
A few minutes after 11.00pm on March 6, 1987 four people settled for dinner outside their house in Makindye, a Kampala suburb. Among them was a former Minister of Energy, Dr. Andrew Lutakoome Kayiira. Kayiira was a visitor to Henry Gombya, a journalist working with BBC in Kampala at the time. Kayiira was in a jolly mood and chatted with everyone. He never looked bothered or worried. He dominated the conversation which bordered on the new era of peace and freedom which had been ushered in by the National Resistance Movement (NRM). "In the past you could not sit outside your house at night and eat," the Weekend Standard quoted Kayiira as having said.

Original_Search_Title: Celtel to Send 2 to Dubai, Nairobi
Headline: Sierra Leone: Celtel to Send 2 to Dubai, Nairobi
Body_Text:
Freetown — Following raffle draws held at the Lumley Beach in Freetown, two Celtel subscribers, Samuella Pratt of 16B Banana Water in Freetown and Edwin Shaka of 6 Kainyande Street, Bo Sunday won the 'Win A Holiday for 2' trips to Dubai and Nairobi, Kenya respectively.
They will each come along with their partner to spend a week in a five-star hotel and will be provided with money for other expenses.

Original_Search_Title: Exit of the Islamists Will See a Revival of Clan Conflicts
Headline: Somalia: Exit of the Islamists Will See a Revival of Clan Conflicts
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Even with the recent military success, reports indicate that the Transitional Federal Government (TFG) and its major backer - Ethiopia - will struggle to resolve the many multidimensional conflicts facing Somalia.
By defeating and running the Islamists out of key towns, Ethiopia has secured its strategic interests. However, the powers that be in Addis Ababa are also apparently aware of their inability to handle the emerging post-war scenario in Somalia and have called for the speedy deployment of a peacekeeping force.

Original_Search_Title: The Movers And Shakers
Headline: Uganda: The Movers And Shakers
Body_Text:
People who drive Busia district Amuru district
Politics

Original_Search_Title: Visiting Living Rooms
Headline: Uganda: Visiting Living Rooms
Body_Text:
A journalist by profession, Frank Walusimbi, 25, is a musician, has worked as a writer and radio presenter. He talked to DENNIS D. MUHUMUZA about his new life as a reporter and Luganda news anchor with Nation TV.
I like your Timberland cap.

Original_Search_Title: Obasanjo's Preference: Puppet, Not President
Headline: Nigeria: Obasanjo's Preference: Puppet, Not President
Body_Text:
Ukupangile nsofu kano uli ne fumo--Zambian Proverb
Oh, what a tangled web we weave, When first we practice to deceive--Sir Walter Scott, 1771-1832.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni 'Bans' Potholes in City
Headline: Uganda: Museveni 'Bans' Potholes in City
Body_Text:
State House — President Yoweri Museveni on Friday vowed to rid Kampala of dirt and potholes. "I no longer want to see dust in Kampala. I no longer want to see mud in Kampala. I don't want to see potholes in the major roads in Kampala," Mr Museveni told diplomats and journalists at the official launch of the Kampala Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting (Chogm).
He said he had issued "very clear instructions" to Kampala City Mayor Alhaji Nasser Ssebagala and the Minister for Works and Transport Eng John Nasasira to ensure the city is rid of potholes and dirt.

Original_Search_Title: Hundreds Enrol for Morality Lessons
Headline: Uganda: Hundreds Enrol for Morality Lessons
Body_Text:
Kampala — PARENTS yesterday thronged Buganda Kingdom headquarters in Mengo to see 300 children off for a two-week morality gala, Josephine Maseruka writes.
The training organised by Nnabagereka Sylvia Nagginda is codenamed Ekisaakate (royal enclosure) 2007.

Original_Search_Title: Kireka School Gives Disabled Children Survival Skills
Headline: Uganda: Kireka School Gives Disabled Children Survival Skills
Body_Text:
Kampala — Imagine what life would be like if you had hands but could not hold anything, legs but could not walk, a brain but could not think, a mouth but could not talk or if you tried to speak, the world seemed not to understand what you are saying.
There are many people living with such disabilities and it is unfortunate that society has not appreciated them.

Original_Search_Title: 'He is Too Ambivalent to Be Mr President'
Headline: Kenya: 'He is Too Ambivalent to Be Mr President'
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Local Government minister Mr Musikari Kombo would appear best suited for the presidency for two reasons. First, the Ford-Kenya national chairman, who early in his education desired to be a Catholic priest, has not been involved in any major corruption scandal in this country.
Secondly, he led an anti-corruption crusade in the Eighth Parliament as chairman of the select committee on anti-corruption. He is famed for producing the 'List of Shame' and it is from his committee's report that the Economic Crimes Act (2003) was drawn. The people's confidence in the current Government fight against corruption is low. No serious cases have been successfully prosecuted against any of the corrupt leaders. This should be the time the people should look around for a saviour and Mr Gabriel Musikari Nazi Kombo should satisfy this demand.

Original_Search_Title: Chaka True Son of the Soil
Headline: Zimbabwe: Chaka True Son of the Soil
Body_Text:
Harare — WHEN Wilson Katiyo titled one of his novels A Son of the Soil, he was talking about a patriot who was prepared to sacrifice life and limb for the freedom of his country.
Although the character in the novel was a war combatant, with the second Chimurenga war as the setting, the title however, is still relevant and appropriate today.

Original_Search_Title: Hanging Should Be As Efficient As Possible
Headline: Kenya: Hanging Should Be As Efficient As Possible
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The procedure used in hanging former Iraqi President Saddam Hussein is the same as that prescribed in Kenya for people convicted for murder, treason and robbery with violence. It can be as messy and uncertain as decapitating a chicken with a blunt knife.
The law requires that when any person is sentenced to death, "he shall be hanged by the neck until he is dead."

Original_Search_Title: Down to the Wire
Headline: Uganda: Down to the Wire
Body_Text:
I doubt that your cardiologist would agree, but the heart-wrenching English Premiership title race between Man United and Chelsea is exactly what the football doctor ordered.
So used had the fans got to one-horse races with Arsenal's unbeaten season and Chelsea's back-to-back victories that a proper title battle is seemingly unbearable as they despair over any dropped points and rush to premature conclusions with every slip-up.

Original_Search_Title: Mugabe's Wishes vs Zim's Needs
Headline: Zimbabwe: Mugabe's Wishes vs Zim's Needs
Body_Text:
THIS year marks 30 years since President Robert Mugabe ascended to the throne of Zanu PF, 27 years since he assumed leadership of the country and 20 years since he browbeat the late Joshua Nkomo and Zapu into a marriage of convenience.
Given his long tenure and advanced age, it has become inevitable that members of his party and indeed citizens of the country ponder his succession. In recent years the subject of Mugabe's succession has become imperative given the tenuous state of the economy.

Original_Search_Title: Ten Killed in Road Crash
Headline: Kenya: Ten Killed in Road Crash
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Ten people were killed in a grisly road accident along the Embu-Mwea-Nairobi road.
Nine of them died on the spot and one on the way to the Embu Provincial General Hospital.

Original_Search_Title: Police Kill Two Suspected Thugs, Recover Toy Pistols
Headline: Kenya: Police Kill Two Suspected Thugs, Recover Toy Pistols
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Police killed two suspected thugs in Nairobi's Karen estate after an hour of a dramatic chase.
The two had allegedly escaped to a thicket after they robbed a businessman of Sh10,000 on Wednesday noon.

Original_Search_Title: A Bull in a China Shop
Headline: Nigeria: A Bull in a China Shop
Body_Text:
As we begin a new year that is full of anxieties, hopes and promises, we are still saddled with the problem of certainties of uncertainties, with a bull in a china shop. We do not know how best we can get this bull out of this shop with minimum casualties as any wrong step could make it destroy all the crystal wares and other valuables. And by whatever means necessary, the bull must be brought out. This is our reading of the depressing state of affairs in the country that keeps deteriorating by the day.
The outgoing year 2006 has been one of the most challenging in the history of this nation. The year ended in the most incredible and bizarre manner with the president's unilateral sacking of his deputy in a most crude and ridiculous manner. How he ever thought he could do this and think of going away with it is far beyond my imagination. As much as one tries not to get depressed or worried, the deeper one sinks into a state of despair and disbelief about how our country is being turned into something worse of a banana republic. Though one was conscious that the president would go to any extent to see the political end of Vice President Atiku Abubakar, one never imagined it would be in such a fashion. How could the vice president be removed with a fiat without recourse to the constitution and the National Assembly? Why for goodness sake we do not have standards in this country eight years into democratic governance? The federal government's action has surely embarrassed and ridiculed the country and did a terrible damage to our image as a nation. In fact, I was also taken a back with the Vice President's picking up of the Action Congress's presidential ticket knowing fully well a big trap was waiting for him. I admire his courage and determination that looks like a political hara-kiri.

Original_Search_Title: Iraq - Any Respite After Saddam?
Headline: Nigeria: Iraq - Any Respite After Saddam?
Body_Text:
In the early hours of Saturday, 30th of December, former Iraqi dictator, Mr Saddam Hussein, who has been on American-supervised trial in Iraq for more than a year was led to the gallows and hanged. Most people would say that Mr Hussien's killing was a fitting tribute to his own brutal reputation, of summary execution and torture for anyone who dared to raise finger in opposition to his reign characterised by terror.
Nobody with the slightest knowledge of the extreme of repression and regimentation that Mr Hussien organised through his tightly controlled Baath party can disagree with his end. The truth is that his accusers and executioners, Western governments especially America and even many governments in the region were his accomplices. In the unprovoked war, Mr Hussien launched against the then young Islamic republic of Iran, he was generously supplied, bankrolled and cheered by the west and all the Arab governments except Libya and Syria. In spite of the determined and principled opposition to the war, mounted by Iraqi opposition at home , Hussien's regional and western supporters turned blind eye to the harsh repression of Iraqis who saw in his unprovoked attack against Iran ,a harbinger to a threat in the region.

Original_Search_Title: Opinion Polls And Fact in Political Arena
Headline: Kenya: Opinion Polls And Fact in Political Arena
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Save for the joy and comfort it brings to his supporters, there is nothing really surprising about President Kibaki being ahead of the pack in opinion polls. Kenyans are judging their President more against the record of his immediate predecessor than against his current of challengers.
The country had literally gone to the dogs in many ways, and Kenyans appreciate the way the Kibaki Government has turned it around in only four years.

Original_Search_Title: The Year of Slogans And Palm-Greasing is Here Again
Headline: Kenya: The Year of Slogans And Palm-Greasing is Here Again
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Shortly after the November 2005 referendum on the proposed constitution, Mwingi North MP Kalonzo Musyoka said: "2006 is definitely going to be an election year". It wasn't.
And in his New Year speech on Sunday, President Kibaki said the polls would be on as scheduled this year, dispelling fears that the Government would postpone the election as had been implied by Electoral Commission of Kenya chairman Samuel Kivuitu on November 2, 2006.

Original_Search_Title: Lumpen Politics Was Part of the Bad Bargain
Headline: Uganda: Lumpen Politics Was Part of the Bad Bargain
Body_Text:
In Kinyankore folklore, when a situation goes from bad to worse, they tell the tale of "Rwanjeramaganya aka twara embiira ha rufu rwa Nyninazara,".
Custom dictated that for a funeral, the bereaved would contribute foodstuffs.

Original_Search_Title: Political Alignment Shaping Up in Coast
Headline: Kenya: Political Alignment Shaping Up in Coast
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As President Kibaki enjoyed Christmas holiday at the Coast, a major political alignment was shaping up ahead of this year's elections.
Although Mr Kibaki has yet to identify the party he will defend his seat on, his foot soldiers in the province were busy mobilising support for Narc-Kenya.

Original_Search_Title: My Story - a Doctor in the House
Headline: Ethiopia: My Story - a Doctor in the House
Body_Text:
It Seems That I Have Been Given the Rather Arduous Task of Writing About Myself- about my journey, my career -- "anonymously, of course," they reassure me.
Easier said than done, I say. I have these flashbacks of applications galore for which I have had to write creative essays on "Why I want to be a doctor/pediatrician, etc.". Slight variations on a theme, as you can imagine!

Original_Search_Title: Bachelor in the House
Headline: Nigeria: Bachelor in the House
Body_Text:
Nigeria it seems is now perennially saddled with a record-breaking House of Representatives even if my friends
prefer to call it the House of Controversies! Not done with electing - some rightly called it 'imposing' - its first female Speaker who also happened to be the first hairdresser to be elected to the exalted position anywhere in the world, the House in maintaining its record-breaking spree also last week elected Dimeji Saburi Bankole as its first bachelor-Speaker!

Original_Search_Title: Members Support Bill On GMO Food
Headline: Kenya: Members Support Bill On GMO Food
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Genetically modified foods could soon become part of Kenya's national menu, if President Kibaki enacts a Bill whose debate Parliament began yesterday.
Locally grown bananas. GM foods could soon be part of our menu if the bill in Parliament becomes law.

Original_Search_Title: Fare Thee Well Good, Bad Polythene
Headline: Uganda: Fare Thee Well Good, Bad Polythene
Body_Text:
Kampala — Uganda's polythyene industry worth over $16 million (Ush25 billion) is expected to largely close before the end of 2007.
In turn, the country is in the long run expected to gain from the sustained and proper use of the environment and exploitation of the rich soil systems.

Original_Search_Title: 112 Killed in Gang Violence in June
Headline: Kenya: June Bloodiest Month in Recent Times As 112 Are Killed in Gang-Related Violence
Body_Text:
Nairobi — A total of 112 people were killed in gang-related violence and police shootings making June one of the most violent months in Kenya's recent history.
Twenty eight of the victims were members of the public who were beheaded or shot dead by assailants believed to be Mungiki sect members.

Original_Search_Title: Minister's Son Draws Gun Over Mungiki
Headline: Kenya: Minister's Son Draws Gun Over Mungiki
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Police are investigating a Cabinet minister's son who fired three shots at a Nairobi club, following an argument.
The incident at the exclusive club was ignited by a heated debate about Mungiki.

Original_Search_Title: Police Raid Criminal Hideouts
Headline: Liberia: Police Raid Criminal Hideouts
Body_Text:
Monrovia — The Liberian Police have raided several densely populated communities in Paynesville City, outside Monrovia and adjacent areas for criminal hideouts, the second in less than a week, making several arrests.
The raid, codenamed "Operation calm down fear", was led by the Inspector General of Police, Col. Beatrice Munah Sieh, at dawn yesterday in several criminally infested areas including the famous 'Old-School' in the Gobachop Market in RedLight, Banana Island, in Sugar Hill and 'Baghdad ' respectively.

Original_Search_Title: Sect's Gangs Kill Four
Headline: Kenya: Mungiki Gangs Behead Four Men
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Mungiki have struck again, killing four people and leaving the hallmarks of their grisly trade on their victims to instill terror and force their targets into submission.
Grief-stricken women at a scene of crime where three people were butchered by suspected outlawed Mungiki sect members in Kianjogu village, Murang'a District, on Monday. Picture by Moses Omusula

Original_Search_Title: Two Sides of Same Coin
Headline: Kenya: Two Sides of Same Coin
Body_Text:
Nairobi — In character and political dispensation, Raila and Kibaki are as different as day and night, but their paths have crossed several times.
While President Kibaki and Lang'ata MP, Mr Raila Odinga, share little in background, character and public profile, their paths have crossed at crucial moments.

Original_Search_Title: Decoding Ouma 'Pardon'
Headline: Uganda: Decoding Ouma 'Pardon'
Body_Text:
Kampala — As long as he speaks responsibly Ouma papers are not a priority.
But if he deviates from the script....

Original_Search_Title: Banana Police Moved As Mungiki Tension Persists
Headline: Kenya: Banana Police Moved As Mungiki Tension Persists
Body_Text:
Nairobi — The Mungiki menace has brought Banana, a small but busy town in Kiambu, to a near standstill even as Central PC, Mr Japhter Rugut, overhauled the entire Karuri Provincial Administration.
Public transport operators on the Banana-Nairobi route pulled out their vehicles to protest at extortion and harassment by suspected members of the illegal gang.

Original_Search_Title: Marie Lumiere Recommends God-Fearing Candidates
Headline: Cameroon: Marie Lumiere Recommends God-Fearing Candidates
Body_Text:
The leader of the Yaounde Spiritual Hospital, Marie Lumiere, has called on Christians to vote only for God-fearing candidates in the upcoming twin elections.
She made the call in a chat with the press shortly after the banana feast that took place at the Mbankolo prayer ground in Yaounde, April 7. According the prophetess, if God-fearing people are voted as councillors and parliamentarians, they will work selflessly for the general interest of the Cameroonian people.

Original_Search_Title: Kibaale in Alleged ADF Rebel Scare
Headline: Uganda: Kibaale in Alleged ADF Rebel Scare
Body_Text:
Kibaale — Kibaale district residents have been warned to be security conscious.
State minister for internal affairs Matia Kasaijja has urged the residents following a suspected ADF rebel infiltration in the area.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni Woos Investors in Australia
Headline: Uganda: Museveni Woos Investors in Australia
Body_Text:
Kampala — THE Government will restore tax holiday for investors involved in export-oriented enterprises. President Yoweri Museveni announced this in Australia while meeting entrepreneurs in Sydney on Tuesday.
He said the tax holiday had been removed on advice of World Bank experts.

Original_Search_Title: Beti Kamya - a Hard Nut to Crack
Headline: Uganda: Beti Kamya - a Hard Nut to Crack
Body_Text:
"Once I am convinced about something, I will stand up and defend it," Kamya says. She narrates a story of how she was courted to join the Movement but told the President in his face, "Mr President one thing I cannot do is keep quiet when I see a wrong, you will sack me," she said.
She is the Rubaga North MP and special envoy to FDC President Col. Dr Kizza Besigye. Simon Kasyate brings you the other side you probably didn't know about Beti Olive Kamya Namisango Turwomwe .

Original_Search_Title: Locked Out of Market Over Mutuelle Cards
Headline: Rwanda: Locked Out of Market Over Mutuelle Cards
Body_Text:
Kigali — Northern Province: Gicumbi- Shock struck residents of Gicumbi District and others on Wednesday when people without Mutuelle de Sante Cards were blocked from entering a market in Byumba town.
Executive Secretaries of Gacurabwenge and Gisuna cells in Byumba Sector and other officials from the sector were stationed at all entry points in the market and nobody without a medical insurance card was allowed in.

Original_Search_Title: Woman Arrested for Killing Man Over Food
Headline: Rwanda: Woman Arrested for Killing Man Over Food
Body_Text:
Musanze — Police at Kigombe Sector in Musanze District are holding a woman for killing her husband, allegedly over food. The incident occurred on Tuesday, February 13 in Karwasa Cell, according to sources. When contacted, police admitted holding the suspect in custody.
Police said the suspect, Clare Ndabazungu, 53, confessed to have "accidentally" hit and killed her spouse, Sirire Nduwayezu, 56, "in self defense" after they [couple] had had a quarrel over potatoes.

Original_Search_Title: Rift Valley Fever Causes Meat Scare
Headline: Kenya: Households in Menu Dilemma
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Households are in a dilemma over what to eat and butcheries in desperate survival as the effects of the Rift Valley fever continue to bite.
With caution over the safety of beef and dairy products repeated from every corner, meal time is increasingly becoming a challenge for many meat-eaters, who are struggling to get an alternative.

Original_Search_Title: Museveni's Legacy On Trial
Headline: Uganda: Museveni's Legacy On Trial
Body_Text:
Nairobi — SOMETIME IN 1992, a satirical play - thirty years of bananas - on Uganda's post-independence politics opened at the National Museum in Kampala.
The characters playing Uganda's post-independence leaders - Milton Obote, Idi Amin, Yusuf Lule, Godfrey Binaisa and Tito Okello Lutwa - all appeared without ears. This, the playwright, Alex Mukulu, explained, symbolised their failure or refusal to heed advice about how to govern the country well.

Original_Search_Title: Forced to Sleep With Ugly Widow Cleansers
Headline: Kenya: Forced to Sleep With Ugly Widow Cleansers
Body_Text:
Nairobi — Despite the dangers posed by HIV/Aids scourge, some men in western Kenya are hired to perform sexual rituals with widows to cleanse them, writes Tom Arocho.
It begins just after sunset. There is commotion inside the rickety hut, occasionally interrupted by muffled screams of a woman in concealed resistance.

Original_Search_Title: Palms - Where You Have Decent Meal in Peace
Headline: Zimbabwe: Palms - Where You Have Decent Meal in Peace
Body_Text:
Harare — TIRED of noisy restaurants that are sometimes so filthy that you cannot have a decent meal in peace?
Then visit the Palms Restaurant at Bronte Hotel.

Original_Search_Title: Why Was Andrew Kayiira Killed?
Headline: Uganda: Why Was Andrew Kayiira Killed?
Body_Text:
Kampala — ANDREW Kayiira's murder and the futile hunt for his killers has lately attracted the same attention it did 20 years ago when the murder took place. Was Kayiira's murder planned or he was a victim of circumstances? Arthur Baguma spoke to Sam Njuba and Bidandi Ssali, then ministers who spoke to Kayiira during the days preceding his death.
Several theories have been advanced on who could have killed Kayiira, why and how. But none of the theories has helped in bringing the matter to a logical conclusion. Shortly after his release from prison where he had been sent for allegedly plotting against the Government in 1987, Kayiira met a number of people including government ministers at the time. It is rumoured that he was scheduled to meet President Yoweri Museveni after his release.

Original_Search_Title: 'Protocol Officers Not Banned From Airports'
Headline: Nigeria: 'Protocol Officers Not Banned From Airports'
Body_Text:
Lagos — Aviation Minister, Chief Femi Fani-Kayode yesterday denied giving a directive that protocol officers at the nation's airports should no longer be allowed to work there, noting that the circular reportedly crediting him with such a directive was at best a fabrication by an official of the Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria (FAAN) at the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport, Abuja.
Fani-Kayode who stated that he could not have handed down such a directive, which he described as 'ridiculous' , said what he directed was that protocol officers should no longer give foreign diplomats and other prominent personalities coming into Nigeria or going out of the country easy passage to beat Immigration screening and other airport formalities.

Original_Search_Title: Calypso, Calypso, Calypso ...
Headline: Uganda: Calypso, Calypso, Calypso ...
Body_Text:
The ideal Ugandan party involves nice music (the definition of which depends on the average age of those in attendance) and good food. Also, let the liquor flow freely and let the people dance undisturbed till the wee hours of the morning.
Now about the dancing, your party isn't a party if you don't see a number of revellers oddly stretching one leg, doing what the uninformed would call a limp all the while moving the arms in a way that would make the chicken feel they've found a counterpart. Don't get alarmed-that's the calypso dance. And it has taken Uganda by storm.

Original_Search_Title: Young Family Perishes in Crash Hours to New Year
Headline: Kenya: Young Family Perishes in Crash Hours to New Year
Body_Text:
Nairobi — As Kenyans all over the country ushered in the New Year with pomp and ceremony, two families in Kiambu District were in mourning.
The families of Mr and Mrs David Njuguna Githinji of Gatamaiyu, and Mr and Mrs Jacob Njogu Wainana of Banana are yet to come to terms with the loss of their four children who perished in a road accident on Saturday afternoon at Makindu on their way to Mombasa for the New Year festivities.
